api.name: The Terror of Mongolia
api.description:
The Terror of Mongolia is a rogue-like shooter set in the 13th century, recounting the tale of Genghis Khan. Riding the plains of Mongolia, Khan is on a quest to rescue Börte, his first wife, kidnapped by the rival tribe leader Jamukha the Jadaran.

Find Börte before the timer runs out, and reach Jamukha.

About

This game was produced for JS13k 2023 gamejam

Controls

  • Use arrow keys or AWSD to move
  • Press Space to shoot
  • Hold Space while moving to keep shooting in the same direction.

Features

  • Horse riding.
  • Archery.
  • Shoot-em-up style gameplay.
  • A dozen upgrades.
  • Ramping up difficulty
  • Morricone-style music.
  • Inspired by real historical events.

Credits and tools

  • Soundbox was used for composing music
  • Both songs composed are covers from Ennio Morricone
  • Roadroller and uglify were used for file size reduction
  • “The Galloping Horse” (1878) from Eadweard Muybridge (the first motion picture ever made) was used as model for horse animation.
  • ChatGPT used to help me learn about historical events and Mongolian culture in the 13th century.

comments.author: Brian Douglas
comments.text:
An excellent entry.

I am super impressed with the horse animation.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Thanks! that horse animation was the first big item I focused on.

comments.author: Santiago Zapata
comments.text: The visuals were really good. Gameplay was a bit repetitive but it worked, I really liked when giant enemies started showing up. Will definitively play more of this one!

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Thanks. I’ll try to improve the game and add more variety.

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text: The music is awesome. I wish I could control the horse a bit better but other than that it’s great!

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: The controls is meant to make it feel like you’re riding a horse. But eventually, you do get upgrades for better controls.

comments.author: Chris Subagio
comments.text:
LOVE the music, so well done. The art style is also just gorgeous, love the muted palette. The rotoscoped vector art is just chef’s kiss.

Was not super crazy about the slow speed ramp on the horse. My suggestion would have been to let the speed ramp quickly, but then sell the momentum by damping the turn rate and slow down rate at high speed instead. So you get to the “action” quicker, but once you’re committed to a path, you can’t back out as easily and have to land shots to survive.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: That’s an interesting idea. I think with the upgrades do give the option to improve speed or controls. I didn’t have enough time to fine tune all that for optimal experience though.

comments.author: Alpine Games
comments.text: The graphic and animations look amazing and the gameplay is fun but a little repetitive.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: I think some players have found the game repetitive because they missed the progression to the next hut. Have you tried following the brown circle to go further?

comments.author: Vivian Easley
comments.text: Nice take on a Vampire Survivors style game. Also, I liked the simple vector art. The controls were a little awkward and felt sluggish. Other than that solid game.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Hopefully the upgrades do help with controls, but I might try improve the game and fine tune that from the start.

comments.author: Ariel Wexler
comments.text: The animations and graphics style were quite pleasant. The music also had me vibing :) I didn’t realize I was supposed to follow the brown dot to the next hut during my first playthrough – once I realized that, the progression felt better. Enemies would sometimes spawn directly on the screen instead of offscreen which felt a bit weird. Fun entry!

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Thanks, I’m glad you found the progression, and realize I need to make it obvious. The enemies appearing on screen is one of those things I ran out of space to fix.

comments.author: Rémi Vansteelandt
comments.text: I love the characters animations. It wasn’t immediately clear what I was supposed to do, especially if I didn’t pick the recommended option in the beginning, I feel like the objective indicator could be more obvious.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Noted, I think it needs to be more clear that you have to follow the dot.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: The vibe of the game is outstanding - the graphics, the music, the animations. The game graphics seem to lag a bit, maybe it’s just too demanding for my XPS. It seems like enemies start warping around when I travel far from the starting position; could it be due to floating point errors in large numbers? The game is quite challenging and i keep dying quickly. Is the brown ball the direction I’m supposed to go? Overall a solid submission and I keep trying to play a bit more and better.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Glad you liked the vibe. I’ll try to fix some of the issues with enemies appearing out of nowhere. I wish there was also a way I could evaluate performance of other devices, that part is hard.

comments.author: Ben Fox
comments.text:
The music, style, and animation really awesome. I like how it became clear from music when I got to the “final” level.

The controls felt a little sluggish, not sure if it was intentional or based on how many enemies there were.

The shop system seemed broken - I could buy whatever I wanted and go into negative money. Didn’t mind too much since it made things a lot easier lol.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Glad you got to experience the final music! I’ll try to improve the controls. They’re meant to increase the realism of riding a horse though. I didn’t realize the bug with the shop.

comments.author: 0ctothorp
comments.text: I enjoyed the game. Love the music and the art. I like the upgrades system. Really cool entry!

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Thank you! I had fun making the art and music.

comments.author: Benjamin Brown
comments.text: The horse animation and the music are both lovely.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Thanks. I had a hard-time figure out how to squeeze the music into those 13kb, so I’m happy it made it through.

comments.author: Marcin Walczak
comments.text:
Music and animation are definitely the strong sides of the game.

Tracing “The Galloping Horse” was a brilliant idea. (Rotoscoping!? In 13kb game!?). That’s probably why it reminds me visually a little bit of “Another world”.

I’ve happened to stuck between trees once or twice and enemies tend to spawn in the middle of the screen but beside that it’s a very solid and entertaining entry.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Nice! I’m also a big fan of “Another Word”. I’ll work on polishing this entry post jam.

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text:
I quite like the visual style and music. It’s a simple but effective game.

Having better control over the horse and the aim would have helped a lot. That and maybe spread the trees out a bit more, felt like I got pretty stuck on them a few times.

But, still had fun, and the music was hyping me up to keep playing. Nice job.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Thanks for trying the game, and I’m glad you enjoyed the style! I had a hard time deciding how many trees to have. With too little trees, they seemed just decorative. Perhaps improving the tree navigation pre-upgrade might help.

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Very cool, I really like the fluid animation!

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Thanks, animation was the first thing I worked on. I’m glad I found a technique to make it smooth.

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Well done:

  • It was fun running around on a horse and shooting things
  • Graphics and animations look great
  • Love the historical influence of the game

Feedback:

  • Took awhile to notice the brown circle at the edge of the screen was suppose to be guiding me to the goal. I just wandered around aimlessly for my first few attempts
  • Tree collision is glitchy and hard to navigate when in a forest
  • Looks like I can purchase all upgrades at the hut and go into negative (money?)

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Glad you liked those historical elements. I had very little space, but try hard to have those references in the game. Noted on all the feedback. Thanks for providing those.

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: Great game, pretty playable, interesting mechanic of movement and shooting. Graphics is ok, abstract. Also there is some music (but no sounds) Great Job!

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Thanks for playing it. I wish I had sound effects, but pretty much ran out of space at the end.

comments.author: Matthew Diamant
comments.text: Amazing entry. Really liked every aspect of this rogue-like. Music was also really awesome, good job. Looked great and felt great to play. Upgrades felt meaning and choices between upgrades were challenging.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Thanks. I really tried to think of the kind of upgrades I could provide, despite not having a large variety of weapons like in other roguelikes.

comments.author: Crystal Parker
comments.text: Excellent game! It was definitely worth the effort for the animated horses. It’s a bit hard to not get killed.
games: Journey to the East; Silk Road Adventure

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: It’s definitely a challenging game. I tend to test the game a lot, eventually getting good at it, and finding a bit easy so I increase the difficulty. I should find a better way to balance that.

comments.author: Justin Sullard
comments.text: This was one of the first entries I saw posted, and one of the first that I played. This game is an amazing entry to the jam, with quite a lot going for it. I loved the basic mechanics (once I knocked the rust off a bit from my gaming fingers), though I never did manage to survive long enough to complete the game. I will be playing this game more once I get through reviewing all of the entries. I’m hooked.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Thanks, I hope you check out the game again post jam. I’ll try to improve it. It’s definitely a hard game, but after several tries, I find that you can reach the end consistently, dying perhaps once or twice.

comments.author: Chris Glover
comments.text: Very fun but perhaps gets too tough toward the end

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Thanks, glad you liked it. What can I say, I love it when games get tough!

comments.author: Andrei Iacob
comments.text: I love those vector graphics, and the colors suit it nice! But the controls are kind of weird… Great game overall!

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: There are some upgrades that help the controls, but I agree that I should make them more user-friendly right from the start.

comments.author: Armaan Mohammed
comments.text: I Love this game the only feedback is that the arrows don’t seem to match up with the bow at high speed. Otherwise a fun addictive game 😀.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Thanks! Well if that’s your only issue, I can definitely try to fix that!

comments.author: Aniket
comments.text: Theme is being followed but can be better if levels or waves were added.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: I think it’s my bad for not making the goal obvious. You do get levels, by basically following the brown dots to the next hut, then the game becomes more difficult the more huts you visit. I hope you give it another try!

comments.author: Lakshman
comments.text: It was fun playing the game!! Awesome Game!!

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Thanks for playing it! I’m glad you had fun!

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: Impressive horse animation! The music is great! I liked to play this game

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Thanks for checking it out. Yeah I admit I felt quite good about that horse animation.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
I enjoyed playing this game, the graphics were good (especially the horse running animation). The music was really nice, and despite looping wasn’t too repetitive.

Once I’d lost bow and arrows I couldn’t find a way to get them back again.

Holding space made the horse go much slower, wasn’t sure if that was intentional.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text:
Thanks, I’m glad you enjoyed the game!

So losing the bow and arrows could happen, and in that case you need to follow the circle and reach the next hut without shooting, just by dodging all enemies.

Holding space does slow down the horse, but there’s an upgrade to shoot without slowing.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: good work and amazing stuff, however what could be nice way to pause way to mute music was hard to figure out how to start only left right shooting with auto aim, why not let us aim Graphics is very good for horse but other entities are not same league looks like when horse is fast the rider is trying to follow it and move behind good luck

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Thanks. I know there’s some features to work out, which should be included in the post-jam release.

comments.author: Luan Ferreira
comments.text: I like the animations and the moviment, its really satisfying. The general design miss something.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: I’m glad you liked the animation. I’ll try to improve the post-jam version, I have some ideas on addressing some design gaps.

comments.author: CodePA77
comments.text: Nice looking game. Controls feel good. But the gameplay is a bit repetitive.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Thanks for checking out the game. I found that some players find the game repetitive because they’re not aware that you need to follow the circle to reach the next hut. When you do that, the levels increase.

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text: Wow, i love the horse animations (and your process behind it) and the music and the story. Also the upgrades after each stage are very cool and add to the long-time motivation. The lock of the direction to shoot is very usefull to defend against huge armies following Khan. Very nice game!

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Thanks! Very glad you enjoyed the game and followed my game development process. Hopefully you get to try to post-jam version, I’ll work on it after the votes end.

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text:
I love the animations in this game. They are fluid and looks stunning. It feels good to hit the enemies with arrows. A sound effect would be the final touch to make it feel even better.

I sometimes got hit by enemies spawning directly next to me, but other than that I didn’t have any issues. God job!

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text:
Glad you loved the animations, and enjoyed the gameplay.

I’ll try to improve the game post jam. Sound effects, and fixing the spawning issues are on my mind.

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text:
A nicely rounded game with levels and upgrades and decent animations, shadows, etc. Music is nice too, upbeat but surprisingly not annoying/

Bit frantic which is ok, but very hard to miss the trees when riding, which became was a bit annoying.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text:
I found several players had a hard time with the trees. I’ll try to address that in my next iteration of the game.

Thanks for trying it out!

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
I like the graphics and animations, plus the soundtrack is very good. Controls are a little difficult, I played an earlier version which seems a lot faster.

Lost my bow and arrow right away which made it difficult, was unsure how to get them back.

Loved how you made the horse animation.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Thanks for the feedback. The earlier version didn’t incorporate any shop upgrades, but this is something I wanted to include for progression (basically you need to move to the next hut by following the dot, then you get upgrade options). When you lose the bow and arrow, you need to travel to the next hut without shooting. I’m glad you liked the animation.

comments.author: Aleksey Litvinov
comments.text: Love the vector art: looks simple and creates proper impression from the game. Sick character animation and music! Also was feeling that in the early demo hero moved faster, which I liked more :)

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text:
Glad you liked the animation and music.

In the earlier demo, I didn’t have any shop upgrades, but that’s a major part of the game. There’s a speed upgrade that would make the horse as fast as it was in the demo, but you have to get it from a shop.

comments.author: John Swana
comments.text: This game turned out to be better/ easy to play than i estimated kudos but controls/gameplay meh, still awesome though

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Glad you enjoyed how the game turned out. I’ll try to improve the experience with the controls in the next iteration.

comments.author: Alexander Sumarokov
comments.text:
➕ “The Terror of Mongolia” is playable, among some exotic games this year.

➖ There are few amplifier cards to choose from, and unfortunately, the game tends to lag.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Thanks for the feedback. Initially, there are only 2 cards but as you reach the 2nd hut, the number of cards should increase substantially. Were you able to reach that?

comments.author: Michael M.
comments.text: For some reason, the game won’t run for me. I can move in the area visible at begin, but can’t leave it, nothing else happens.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: I’m sorry you weren’t able to run the game properly. Did you enter the hut initially? That’s what triggers the real game to start.

comments.author: Alexander Petrov
comments.text: Looking good, gameplay is a bit clunky. Issues: Objectives are non-evident. Took me some time to figure I should enter the yurt, then that I havet o go to the brown dot. So I have run out of time and lost the bow, effectively losing the game. Horse animations looks good, but for some reason play at slow motion. Also, rider keeps being misaligned with horse. Enemies spawn right in the middle of the screen, sometimes right over me.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Thanks for the feedback. I’ll work on addressing all those issues post-jam.

comments.author: Umz
comments.text: Really fun to play, enjoyed taking down huge numbers of enemies. Only thing for me was the horse felt a bit slippery but still great fun.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: I’m glad you enjoyed the experience. The horse has shop upgrades to improve its control, but that’s randomized so you might not always access it. I’ll try to improve that.

comments.author: Grav
comments.text: Awesome rogue-like, felt cool shooting backwards while charging off. Very innovative death consequences. Good job.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Glad you liked the shooting back control. I was really trying to find a way to shoot back while moving forward, without resorting to add the mouse. I tend to prefer when all controls are within the keyboard.

comments.author: Allen Schmidt
comments.text: This was great! I like the animation and overall look of your graphics (especially the sketchy style of the animation), and running around on the horse felt nice by itself. The archery felt satisfying as well! One thing I didn’t like is how sometimes enemies would appear directly in front of me while I was running, so I didn’t really have a chance to dodge or shoot them. Not sure if that was just a bug or something, but it happened quite a few times. And personally I think the collision area on the trees felt a bit big, so getting through the forest felt hard at times (though I do appreciate the bigger collision area on enemies, so it made it more likely a hit would land!). Overall though, well done!

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text:
Thanks for this feedback. I’m glad you enjoyed the graphics and shooting aspects.

The issue with enemies appearing was something I wanted to fix, but I pretty much ran out of space.

I did get other feedbacks regarding the trees, so I’ll make sure to improve that.

comments.author: Johan Åhlgren
comments.text: A bit repetitive, but I really like both the sound and the graphics! Good job!

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: I’m glad you liked the sound and graphics aspects. I found that some players find the game repetitive if they don’t know to get to the next hut, by following the brown dot. Were you able to catch that?

comments.author: FoumartGames
comments.text: This game was super responsive initially when I tried it out and it was fun even if unbalanced with no collectibles and so on. Now it lags so much that it is almost unplayable for me.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text:
Thanks for the feedback. One design decision I should have worked out a bit better was the introduction of shops.

Basically, the speed that you used to get in the initial version became a shop item that you need to acquire. It’s free, but in the first shop, you have to choose between speed and bow.

I’ll try to improve that initial experience, perhaps by making sure the horse is faster initially. But in the current version, those are upgrades you acquire as you go through levels.

comments.author: Brian Hambley
comments.text: This game has changed 3 times since it was submitted? I dont remember the coins thing being there last time I played it? Still, food fun. Simple to just pick up and play. Like the chaos of it all with hordes swarming from all directions. Will attempt to complete :)

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Great! I’m glad I can get you to keep coming back! The game only changed twice though. If you don’t remember the coins (I assume you mean the shop prices), it’s because the first shop does not have prices. Both options are free.

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text: The music is rad! I think I saw a horse regenerate a rider, which is actually the coolest mechanic :)

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Thanks. Hum, the horse regenerating a rider is not actually planned, but I’m glad you like it ;-)

comments.author: Tim West
comments.text:
Fun little game! The animations are very aesthetic, especially with the shadows. Beautiful art style!

It felt really good to control the horse around the map. I like the arrow implementation which provides a limited ability to “aim” accurately with some practice.

It took me some time to realize that the brown dot was leading to the next hut to buy upgrades. I think an arrow might be a better indicator.

A few nitpicks:

  • The health indicators had some missing/corrupt assets (Firefox, 1440p monitor).
  • The horizontal lines for grass are a low point for a game with otherwise very high-quality graphics.
  • It’s a little too easy to get “stuck” on trees where the player is constantly bouncing while trying to get through a forest.
  • Sometimes fast enemies will appear on-screen and won’t leave enough time to react to avoid them. I wish the spawns were a little more consistent.

comments.author: Jeremy Burns
comments.text: The art style and animation are really cool, but it seemed to run at a fairly slow frame rate for me which detracted from that a little bit. Otherwise fun and straightforward shooter mechanics! Nice entry!

api.name: Robin Hood
api.description:
you step into the legendary shoes of Robin Hood. your mission is to make the world a better place.

  • explore forest and hunt animals
  • trade with merchants
  • take quest from villagers
  • improve your stats and defend the village a w s d / arrows for movement e interact, q menue, space fire

comments.author: Alpine Games
comments.text: A little confusing at first but a solid entry otherwise.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: appreciated, thank you for the feedback :)

comments.author: Ariel Wexler
comments.text: I wasn’t quite sure what to do after buying a bunch of apples and killing a bunch of bears O_O

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: using points to level up and have ability to encounter vast mob count, each level will add more mobs to spawn, as a first version of the game having castle and cave just spawn mobs but later will have another purpose like entring them

comments.author: Vivian Easley
comments.text: Really cool game. Loved the retro style. The controls were intuitive although I did accidentally tap the “q” and quit my game while trying to navigate. Might be worth having a “Are you sure, you’d like to quit” message. Also, might be nice to have a clearer goals. I Immediately went to the castle and got savaged.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: castle part was partialy my mistake lol, its just because of not having castle world fit in the size of 13kb. q will go to menu but progress is not lost. prolog was actually cut in half in process to save some kbs and I could have made something more clear sory about that.

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text:
I wasn’t quite sure what to do at first. I murdered so many critters and now I feel bad :P

Once I figured out the quests it’s actually quite solid.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: murdure is bit harsh for hunting hehe, could have made like an anouncement for level cleared now that I think of it.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: Alright this looks really interesting. Cute graphics and sound effects. I couldn’t figure out out to get fruit. I got 1 of each accidentally but then never got any again. I tried shooting and interacting with trees. The user of alerts is a bit disruptive to the game. I’m not sure if I’m supposed to be able to go to the castle, that seems impossible with constant guards spawning. The game has potential but I couldn’t figure out how to progress.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: thanks for feedback, right that when I told peple about game I went ahead and said do that and that, maybe a more clear progression next time. actually progress in this version is not limited to doing something hard other than hunting animals through the map

comments.author: Benjamin Brown
comments.text: I never figured out how to do quests or whatever was in the top right, but I kited all the bad guys out of the castle for like 15 minutes and that was pretty fun.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: glad to see I managed to keep you more than 10 minutes lol. quests are given from villagers asking for stuff you buy from trader, for this version it was that simple. hopefully in later version quests would be more fun and challenging

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Well done:

  • Graphics style was great
  • The level was interesting to navigate around

Feedback:

  • I’m not entirely sure what I was suppose to be doing. I’d kill things but not sure why or how to win
  • For the longest time I though the yellow / orange circle in the top bar (under the red circle) was coins (and not oranges) and so I thought I wasn’t getting any coins when I killed enemies and picked up the coins
  • I’m not sure why I buy apples and oranges
  • I’m not sure what points are or how to get them to buy apples and oranges with (could only buy from the center trader)
  • Not sure the diagonal movement (and attack from enemies) worked well with the graphic style. Also it lead to getting stuck in trees or walls when diagonally moving into them

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: you are supposed to kill things to give to poor, instead of stealing from rich winning is a state of satisfaction that all the poor have food to eat :p surly this was the plan but I ended up with a slightly shorter version hope I could make the full game and show the idea to its full potential

comments.author: Crystal Parker
comments.text: I didn’t understand the quests… I figured out how to kill the characters that came out the mountain with lots of running about.
games: Journey to the East; Silk Road Adventure

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: the quest, main one, is to hunt and give. like just go around forest hunting and come to village to sell to trader and give villagers, where later things become harder while you gain more skill and ability to handle. running about is quite right, since you would not expect your prey to come to you i guess

comments.author: Ben Fox
comments.text: I like the map, layout, and style but there were scrollbars so I couldn’t keep all the stats in view at once (window bumped up every time I hit space). This also made it confusing to figure out what was going on.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: right I have seen that case when we open game with header of the website, but on its own this does not happen, I guess adding fullscreen mode should be on the todo list next time

comments.author: Justin Sullard
comments.text:
I instantly felt like I was 10 playing some of the first video games I can remember. It took me a couple of times playing to start to get the hang of things, and to discover the whole leveling system and game progression system. Once I got the hang of things it was quite the stroll down the better parts of memory lane for me running around and fending off killer rabbits. I will likely be playing this one more to try and get that blasted expensive bow I’m hoping becomes affordable.

The use of alerts for user confirmation, while very code-efficient, causes issues with the music/sound, as the alert halts execution of javascript, leaving tones running in the background. Maybe pausing before the alerts would have resolved the issue, not sure, but I ended up taking off the sound fx (but leaving on the music) as they could also end up getting stacked and hurt my ears a bit.

I’m not 100% certain I understand all of the leveling system, as I maxed everything out at 10 pretty quickly in Level 4, so I’m looking forward to seeing more about the games internals when I go perusing the github projects.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: very refreshing feedback thank you very much and hope to meet your expectations in the final version

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: The game controls are smooth, the retro style is greate and I enjoyed playing the game. I was not really able to understand what I was meant to do after killing enemies and buying apples and oranges.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: I blame the dum me refactoring code and cutting the prolog lol, could have kept the walkthrorugh in the game description, silly me

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: Not a bad game, several activities and complicated gameplay. Controls aren’t good enough, it’s sometimes really hard to move in a taget way.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: I agree it may be challenging to move along x or y only to shoot, targets have pathfind toward you so drawing them into a suitable position may be good strategy

comments.author: Abed Al Rahman Majzoub
comments.text: Great and wonderful game. Nice strategy

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: appreciate the feedback, thank you

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
It all works well, nice map, castle and small animals running about. The combat requires a lot of running about.

The popups give quests and tell you not complete is a bit comfusing, would be good to use in game UI instead.

Good entry over all, lots of features.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: good advice to utilize in game ui for these, i was a bit lazy there, thx for feedback

The usage of window.alert() is not so nice. I’d prefer to display a div with the message, and that div could even auto-hide after a couple of seconds. So the gameplay is not interupted that much. That div could also be styled like a speech bubble and positioned near the “speaker” (questgiver/merchant)

comments.author: Richard Davey
comments.text:
You control Robin Hood who must move around the grid-like forest, shooting the animals and collecting the coins they drop. These can be spent with the merchant in the village who will exchange them for apples and oranges (note: the amount you get and how much it costs is randomized, so you can try it a few times to get a better deal). Once you have enough items, you can give them to the 4 ‘quest giving’ villagers outside the huts.

If you clear all animals, the level increases and a new set spawn out. If you go too close to the dark castle a stream of baddies come out, so don’t stay there for too long! If you go near the cave, bears come out. Killing this requires a lot of navigating, back-tracking and shooting arrows until they’re gone.

I ended up playing this for much longer than I expected, getting to Level 7 before I ran out of arrows and promptly died! Also, I would question the ability for a little rabbit to kill Robin Hood :)

What a great fun little game this was, though. I know lots of people have knocked the use of the Alert pop-up for dialogue, but I liked it because it naturally paused the game (which you can’t do any other way). If you play with WASD be careful about hitting Q by mistake, as it quits out. Cursors are safer.

api.name: The Cat Jumper
api.description:
A new queen has ascended the throne of the country. But where all the family wealth was stored, the new queen’s parents took to their grave You’re her cat, trying to find your parents’ treasure. You’ll face various obstacles along the way.

Controls

  • left screen tap: change direction to left,
  • middle screen tap: jump,
  • right screen tap: change direction to right, or you can use WASD

Tip: You can kill archers & catapults by touching them, you can set respawn point by touching the scepter, the respawn point is saved permanently.

comments.author: Richard Davey
comments.text:
It’s a cat-based endless runner, except it has an end! As the feline hero, you charge across the ramparts, leaping with a massive mighty bound and trying to find the treasure chest (which really won’t take you very long). Baddies can be killed by landing on them, although you jump so high you often clear them entirely.

I’m not sure it’s entirely on-theme, but I guess they did have castles, and cats, in the 13th Century, so I can forgive it.

It scrolls fast, I quite liked the challenge and it didn’t outstay its welcome. Short, sweet, and graphics that look like they were randomly searched for on giphy 🤣

comments.author: Jedidiah Weller
comments.text:
Thank you for submitting your game and good work on getting it submitted! Here’s my feedback after playing a couple times:

  1. I liked the music and sound - thought it was mostly appropriate for the ‘castle’ vibe and the sound on the archers was fine
  2. The biggest point for improvement is going to be in the responsiveness of the game itself. There were issues with the collision boxes (I found myself hitting the knights way before I actually touched them), the respawn point (found myself being able to spam respawn if you approach it from below) and the size of the screen. I’d suggest using view-height and view-width or percentages when styling your game.
  3. Folder structure - I did a quick review of the source code and there wasn’t any folder structure or readme. Attention to detail is always an important thing, especially when creating new projects. Thinking through folder structure forces the developer to think about concepts from an OOP perpsective which is very helpful in game design.
  4. I’d also like to see a README on any repository - that’s an industry standard and very important for the community.

comments.author: Armaan Mohammed

comments.author: Clément Pasteau
comments.text:
Hey there!

I’m gonna repeat what some others said, the music is what stands out at first, really puts you in the atmosphere. But it’s also what makes the game hard to play, too bad it’s that repetitive! Maybe you should have gone for a full 8 bit music that doesn’t take space at all, to make it real longer.

The drawing is cute, you can definitely it’s not perfect, but I think it gives a nice atmosphere to the game. I would have loved to have the screen bigger to actually see the characters more.

In my opinion, I would have kept the controls on the screen at the beginning. A tutorial is so important to get players to stick to the game, and most player will skip the first modals to get right into it…. and then you’re wondering what key to press to talk to the NPCs. And kept the objectives on screen too. If you close the modal saying what to do, you’re stuck.

Regarding the touch detection, I’m wondering if you could have done it automatically? Using the touch events and switch a boolean on touchstart to activate the touch controls for the rest of the game.

Well done on the overall submission though!

comments.author: Armaan Mohammed
comments.text: Hi, Thanks for commenting!😃 I really wish I could have made the music better but ran out of space and time😅. I will definitely add a tutorial or a tutorial level in my future games!😁 Hmm… I didn’t realize I could do that with the touch but it definitely feels more intuitive now that i think of that!😅😄. I will definitely try to fix most of the feedbacks in my future games!😁

comments.author: Tom Harris

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
Good game!

I loved the story and being a part of it. Completed the game to the end. When I started playing, I forgot how to interact with objects :), but when I read it again, there were no more problems. There were a lot of clues in the story, so this context was enough for me to have fun.

I’m not sure, but I think that telling the story at the beginning of each level, without the possibility of parallel play, would focus the player to first read part of the story, and then live (play) it.

Where to give the opportunity to scroll through the text yourself, or the possibility of auto-reading, which is now available, and then already in the application the “stop” button, if you turned on the auto-reading mode before that, and you don’t have time to read it… something like that :D.

Thanks and congratulations!

api.name: Path to Glory
api.description:
1254 AD

The Kingdom of Syldavia is being invaded by the Northern Empire.

The Syldavian army is outnumbered and outmatched.

One lone soldier decides to take on the emperor himself.

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text:
It’s packed with great details: the trees fading when you cross behind them, the little animations, the lighting, the infinite map… great job!

On the minus side - I found it awkward to control the mouse, and perhaps it would be nice if you could just skip some waves - if you continue walking past one you get trapped by the next waves until you have to fight them (or simply block a portion of the screen to make you fight). Otherwise you can walk forever and nothing happens :)

comments.author: Santi Herranz
comments.text: Loved from the first sight. Nice entry tutorial, great movements and beauty slow zoomed camera.

comments.author: Jure Triglav

comments.author: Vivian Easley

From advanced fight mechanics (light and strong attacks, blocking, dodging, stunning), through telegraphing enemy attacks to such small details like trees getting transparent to not block the sight or moving grass.

It’s definitely one of my favorite entries.

Great game! The grass swaying and birds flying by were all nice touches! The perfect block and slain slow downs with the smoke and blood particles were super satisfying!

It would have been very helpful if the player character would have stood out just all around, and also didn’t look the same as the enemies. Like give my dude a golden sword and royal blue shield and red tunic or whatever. Especially with how the player dashes towards whoever he hits, it’s very easy to loose track of your character.

Also I was able to take away about 90% of the kings health while the game with in the level transition screen :)

I really loved this game though. Sort of a dark souls wave fighter. It’s satisfying, polished, and really cool!

Lots of nice features well implemented, could be a bigger game for sure.

love it! Great Tutorial and really engaging to play. I want to play again Theme - possibly the least. Not super imaginative but swords and shields!

Graphics - well done and the cohesive nature of all the elements is great Controls - smooth and intuitive Well done Sir!

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text:
A well executed game, 5 stars on everything. The amount of work put in this game is astonishing.

And don’t get me started on all the juice. From the water drops to the perfect block, everything is carefully thought of.

It’s difficult to think of improvements, because everything works well together. Adding one more type of enemy, like a pikeman or an archer is the only thing I can think of. Good job on this one.

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text: A masterpiece in 13KBs. Great job, so much attention to detail…

comments.author: Luke Stapley
comments.text:
Probably the most complete game I’ve played. Give yourself another 2 - 3 months of development and add to a web publishing site like poki or Crazy Games and make money on it.

Biggest things to improve:

  • Much more enemy types
  • Cooldowns on flips (just one second is ok)
  • More levels
  • More costumes

Historians might enjoy it especially with my high score.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
This is a well though out fun game.

I love all the little touches, from the rain and the grass swaying to the ripples on the water.

The music was pretty funky and had a good beat to it.

I’ve never been a mouse and keyboard combo player, I like to use either one or the other or a gamepad, but your control system worked well.

Was I supposed to be able to defeat the dummies on the tutorial or the characters who appeared? The hit scores which I assume were percentages never seemed to go above 90% for me so the targets of my attacks seemed to never be defeated.

When I played the first time it came up with a hint to follow the path to the right, but the further I went along it the further it continued without arriving anywhere, was the path endless?

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text:
A very solid game, simple but aesthetically very pleasing graphics with shadows on everything. (For me) a bit hard to control, but the controls looked cool to. Nice touches with nearby trees going transparant and dropping down and slowing down in water.

Tintin! LOL

comments.author: Lakshman
comments.text: Nice game I enjoyed playing it!!

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text: I love the amount of mobility you gave the player! It does, however, trivialize the gameplay a bit, since nothing is able to chase you bunny-hopping across the screen. They’re unable to land a single hit if you kite, long press, then space-space-space out of range. I guess the emperor should have a rifle or something to compensate :)

comments.author: Joep van Duinen
comments.text:
The combat in the game is really nice! The snappy attacks together with dodge rolling and the precision blocking makes for some really fun and active gameplay. Really good job there!

The only feedback I’d have is on the visuals. For the characters, they’re simplistic and nice, although they could have a bit more variety. For the environment they could’ve been a bit more interesting. However, that is nitpicking a bit since the game has to stay quite small of course.

All in all, really nice game! Great job!

comments.author: Markus Fisch
comments.text: So amazing! I really enjoyed this one! All the tiny lovely details like that he gets dizzy from rolling too much or that he sinks into the water or or or ;) Just great!

comments.author: Taufik Sani
comments.text:
Can I just say.. your game is class as always. For 13kb, this is amazing.

Theme - 5/5 capturing the 13th century and the narrative embedded with the game itself. You can play this game blind without reading any documents outside the game and you can learn how to play the game.

Gameplay - I like how it guides the player from the beginning - teaching basic controls and slowly increasing the tempo of the game. Players can skip if they want to. Stamina bar is also a nice touch to add some realism to gameplay.

Having a restarted game to the last checkpoint is something that you don’t see in a 13kb game. I was relieved!

Graphics - walk into water, your character slows down. Wind, rain, moving grass, lightning. Nice additions to the environment. The slow-mo killing of last enemy is also a great touch.

Control - the field of vision is a helpful implementation, esp for 2d hack & slash rougelike games. This is really good.

I think being able to swap weapons would be nice to create variation in gameplay?

comments.author: Isaac Benitez
comments.text:
Congrats on the game! The things I liked the most were the controls. The attacks feel pretty good!

Also loved the transparency effect on the trees.

api.name: Knight Dreams
api.description:
Knight Dreams is an endless runner arcade action platformer (yes, that’s a genre now), where you control a 13th century knight - or at least a 13th century knight helmet with legs and arms. Your goal is to do things that most 13th century knights do: collect gems, kill everything that moves and keep running until you die. As simple as that. Or maybe you are a 13th century knight having a fever dream, that would explain the name of the game.

Controls

  • Left & right arrow (or A and D): move left or right
  • Up arrow (or W): jump (when on the ground) or “fly” (when in the air)
  • Space: extend your rubber spear (at least I think it’s rubber)
  • Enter: pause

Game mechanics explained

You gain points automatically or by killing enemies with your spear (the enemies die automatically when they hit your spear, extending it (or “attacking”) is not necessary). Collecting gems will increase the amount of points you earn by doing these heroic deeds (1 gem = +10% score bonus), but they will also give power for your… uh, propeller, that you can use to fly.

Important notes

  • AUDIO DOES NOT WORK ON SAFARI! No idea why, but since I don’t have an Apple device, this is almost impossible to fix, so please use either Firefox or any Chromium-based browser.
  • On lower end hardware it might take a few second for the game to generate all the graphics assets, so please be patient (on a decent hardware it should take only a second, though)
  • Your best score is stored automatically to localStorage, but it is not uncommon that your browser security settings block localStorage calls. In these cases your best score will disappear when you leave the page. What a pity.

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text: Nice control, nice graphics, awesome tiny font :)

comments.author: Alpine Games
comments.text: A fun quick endless runner arcade action platformer. Graphics are cute.

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text: Beautiful retro graphics, a cute knight catching gems, a magical spear to kill enemies… what’s not to love?

comments.author: Ariel Wexler
comments.text: Flawless controls, beautiful graphics, and overall fun experience. Only ding is no music.

comments.author: Konrad Linkowski
comments.text: One of the most beautiful games I’ve seen in this competition

comments.author: John Swana
comments.text: This game is a bit generic but for what its worth you nailed the gameplay and theme interpretation

comments.author: Rémi Vansteelandt
comments.text: Only thing I’d improve about this game would be the difficulty/controls. Aside from that, everything felt right, great job!

comments.author: Thiago Romão Barcala
comments.text: I struggled to get used to the movement. I think maybe the speed could start slower in the beginning, cause the first speed was already very hard for me. I wish there was a way to fall from a platform by pressing down for example. Graphics are very nice and feels polished.

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text:
Beautiful design, love the artwork. For me, the endless runner style just feels limiting. This same game but with levels you could progress through regularly instead of auto running and I’d love it.

As it is, still impressive and nice entry, the gameplay style just isn’t for me.

comments.author: Marcin Walczak
comments.text:
Pretty solid and fun endless runner.

Rayman style gliding is a cute little detail.

comments.author: Samir Hodzic
comments.text: Nice pixel art, pretty simple but fun. Love how it plays.

comments.author: Justin Sullard
comments.text: This is a great entry, but I’m afraid I’m not very good at auto-scroll platformers so I wasn’t able to get very far. This said this is a great entry, with lots of juicy bits.

comments.author: Andrei Iacob
comments.text: Amazing game, the graphics are really cool, the SFX are great! Would be nice to see a full game release! Good luck!

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: Great game Good combination of runner and platformer, a lot of interesting mechanics. Good controls. Helicopter helmet is really funny) Nice pixel art graphics.

comments.author: Crystal Parker
comments.text: It’s a fun game, the graphics are great, the sound is great, and the controls are great! I especially like the helicopter hat. I think it’s a bit too fast at the start. I do find the spear a little hard to use, but because it’s fun it’s okay. I like that you can also jump on bad guys.
games: Journey to the East; Silk Road Adventure

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Loved the game, scored 10,260 on one run. Could keep playing over and over for hours.

Graphics were great (as yours always are), love the pixel art.

The gravity and platformer mechanics were spot on.

comments.author: Elliot Nelson
comments.text:
Tough but fair speedrunner – I found the controls a little bit tough to get used to, but the player has a LOT of survival tools (long-range spear attack, jump on enemy heads, fly around up above spikes for a short distance, etc.).

No background music, but the sfx are effective and fun – and the graphics are top-notch. My high score is 2995 after surviving several speed ups :)

comments.author: Luan Ferreira
comments.text: One of my favoritos. For sure. I like the colora, the title, the map, the enemies.. great work.

comments.author: Chris Glover
comments.text: Amazing graphics and style. Really nice level of polish too. I was initially a bit frustrated with not being able to run backward, I think because I was expecting a traditional platformer, but I think in the context of an endless runner that’s actually fine.

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: Very weel done! I liked the graphics and the rubber spear! Maybe It should have been even better adding a background music.

comments.author: CodePA77
comments.text: Good looking and well build game.

comments.author: Christopher Lis
comments.text: This game is so solid from a gameplay perspective, am so impressed with how polished and robust this is. Really nice work, especially with the propellor mechanic—makes the game really fun.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Great UI, menu, intro screen and graphics. All put togeher nicely.

The flying is a nice touch, quite nice to control but not a big fan of the auto scroll, prefer to be able to control movement myself.

comments.author: Aleksey Litvinov
comments.text:
Reminded me Mario game, but with benefits :D I liked the gameplay, especially flying experience and extending lance. And graphic looks awesome and unique.

Would be nice to get some music for the full immersive experience!

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text: Excellent graphics and handled the visual details perfectly. Nice controls and SFX.

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text:
A very polished game! Love the fact it’s all honest pixel art. The dithering is classy.

Your art style is immediately recognizable, too: I remember seeing both The Maze of Space Goblins from 2021 and Dying Dreams from 2022 and thinking to myself, wow, this look good.

The sheer amount of reviews on this page speaks for itself, great job!

comments.author: Adrien Guéret
comments.text:
Every times I die, I say to myself “It’s only because I’ve made a mistake, let’s try one more time to beat my score!”.

In short: I could play this game for hours :D

Arts are gorgeous, controls are convenient and original (love the spear idea and the helicopter thing!). I would prefer to be able to go down on platforms, and more variety in ennemies would be perfect. But hey, it’s a very solid entry, definitively on my top this year :)

comments.author: Antti Haavikko
comments.text:
11700!

Really captures that “one more try” feeling. The start isn’t boring either (which is important for retries) as it gets back to action pretty quick and even the easier part is still important for scoring well.

Nice enemy variety. Super easy to distinguish different enemies once you’ve accustomed to them. Those red flying ones are the bane of my existance. Really tough on faster speeds. But even more deadly were the spikes that I for some reason just could’t see when it got faster. That blinking animation helped to spot them out first but it wasn’t enough when moving fast. And I don’t even have any red-green colorblindness…

Controls were pretty damn smooth. Though I would have wanted a separate jump button. I pretty much stopped using the attack completely because I kept confusing myself that key to be jumping. And the attack usefulness was really situational too and too often missing (jumpers or fliers) spelled your doom as the return from extend was so damn slow. Almost always it was way safer to accomplish the same with just moving forward myself. Having the spear shaft be part of the kill box would have helped alleviate this some though it would have been less realistic.

The scoring system could have been more transparent in the game itself. Just read the “mechanics explained” now and had no idea about the gems contributing to scoring like that. Having some indication about it in the game too would have been nice. And the harder enemies should have given more points as a risk vs reward kinda thing.

The flying meter was a bit too big in my opinion. Never had any issues in keeping the tank full as the gems were so plenty. Even so plenty that I basically didn’t intentionally go for them at all unless they were on my way as I didn’t know about their scoring connection.

Very good looking pixel art. The sound effects were fine I guess but there was only few different ones so they kinda got monotonous. Having music would have obviously helped with that too.

Good job! 👍

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text:
Superb controls, and a game which can be replayed many times. It doesn’t need a tutorial, and you learn a few new tricks for each run. It’s a great game with a nice color pallet, and cute graphics. Very well done.

I wish we would get some points for killing enemies, which incentives the player to take additional risks for a higher score.

The only thing missing in the game is music, but overall a fantastic game.

comments.author: Lakshman
comments.text: The game control is very smooth and I really liked the graphics. Very cool game.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: Adorable entry! The animations are smooth. I got hooked for way longer than I want to admit. I got tricked thinking the smiling enemies were friendly at first. It took me a while to figure out the flying (oops, someone didn’t read the instructions!) I wish there was a way to continue at the same speed or on a higher difficulty. After reaching high speeds and dying, returning to the initial speed was torture! The sounds are cute but got a little annoying after a while. Add a background music and a little better polished sounds, and you’ve got a solid game you can release to the public here!

comments.author: Nathan Long
comments.text: Really nice! The controls feel responsive and the format leads itself well to the runner/arcade style. The only thing I wish I could do is drop DOWN from a platform.

comments.author: Nights
comments.text: A very beautiful and tiny game，I really like this game

comments.author: Aurélio A. Heckert
comments.text: OMG! This is good! I want more lives!

comments.author: Björn Ritzl
comments.text: Really well polished game! Tight controls, good graphics, nice retro sound effects.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text:
rubber spear, gum gum spear :p

nice graphics remind me of sonic games,

good addition of fly ability i think, give it a kick

comments.author: Victor Debone
comments.text:
Hello! Thanks for your submission to js13k! I’m sure you’ve worked hard on your game, but I’m not aware of how much time you spent on it, so excuse me if things I mention were in your mind, but got left out in the rush of the month or just because you hit the 13kb limit :)

  • My first impression and understanding (before playing the game, only reading the description, screenshot and title):
    • From the pixel art on the screenshot I am expecting some really tight core loop into the endless running world. The lance also give me some power move to keep going forward and have some fun.
  • Great work on:
    • Having a complete working game!
    • Graphics! They are really nice and working really well together.
    • Sound, it really nailed down the feel of the actions and made for a more enjoyable game.
  • I liked that you:
    • Took care of the platforming part of the game. All the details to make jumping and flying around enjoyable and “fair” were taken care of.
  • You could improve (again, I’m aware you might already know all the below):
    • At some point I realised it was a better strategy to ignore the extending lance mechanics. It was too confusing with jumping and it was not really clear it was helping me.
  • I took some screenshots https://twitter.com/javascripl/status/1709691814683271661

title: Sir Coadalot and the Holy Grail
author: Nik Lever

api.name: Sir Coadalot and the Holy Grail
api.description:
It is the year 1213 and Sir Coadalot is 13 years into his quest to find the Holy Grail. He has reached the final hurdle, the Castle of Doom. To reach his goal he must navigate the castle with your help.

Instructions

  • To move use the right thumbstick
  • To turn when attacking use the left thumbstick
  • To use your sword press and hold the trigger button
  • To start press the vr button below

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: Don’t have VR, so can’t test this game (

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text:
Solid small game, simple but effective graphics, clear goal and nice ending with the grail.

More detail outside of the castle than in. Seemingly little progression, easy to just walk through without fighting. Showing how many lives left, tougher enemies as you progress would have made it a bit less repetitive and a bigger challange. Having towers obscure the playing field at every door also did not help. I didn’t care much for the bumper care locomotion. Some sound would have made a big difference making it more immersive.

comments.author: Nik Lever
comments.text: Thanks for the feedback. Lives appear as a green to red circle floating above the player. This appears when lives change. Totally agree with your other comments. It’s my first attempt at the competition and I learned a lot for next year.

comments.author: John Swana
comments.text: I’ve No VR h/w so i guess it’s a pass.

comments.author: Sébastien Vandenberghe
comments.text:
I was unfortunately never able to try the game out due to a script error on startup :-(

The error seems related to the hosting: “Access to script at ‘https://js13kgames.com/webxr-src/2023/three.module.js’ from origin ‘https://play.js13kgames.com’ has been blocked by CORS policy: No ‘Access-Control-Allow-Origin’ header is present on the requested resource.”

comments.author: Nik Lever
comments.text: Do you mind sharing the script error. I only have a Quest2 to test on and it worked fine on that.

api.name: Breakout Game
api.description: Left / Right Arrow turn < / >

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Well done:

  • If this was your first game and/or game jam, congratulations! Always impressive to get a game done (and Breakout is a great game to start with)

Feedback:

  • The paddle controls could be more smooth. Instead of slowly jumping from side to side, a smooth transition would make the controls feel much tighter
  • Adding audio on the ball bouncing off walls, the paddle, and bricks would really help give the player a pleasant feedback and juice (see https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Fy0aCDmgnxg for what game juice means)

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: This is simply a re-implementation of a classic game. I hope this submission inspires you to try something more creative next year! This one also doesn’t fit this year’s theme. The paddle movement seems to be tied to the character repetition speed. I suggest you use the keydown event to enable movement but don’t make the paddle move each time the event is triggered. Use keydown and keyup as “on” and “off” instead

comments.author: Andrei Iacob
comments.text: Hmm… Did they have breakout in the 13th century? :) Maybe you can add some sound effects, try ZzFx, they are simple to use and very small. Also, add juiciness, like particles and screen shake. Try to work on nicer colors, try coolors.co

comments.author: Brian Hambley
comments.text: As a 1st attempt well done. More to follow we hope! You could have made the ball a canonball or a flaming fireball and the bricks a castle wall perhaps and gained some theme points. The paddle movment matches the game speed so wasnt problematic but think about how your capturing key events to avoid that initial delay on keypress.

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: Simple arcanoid game, not the greatest quality, but you can play it. Physics has some glitches, sometimes ball bounces in wrong direction or bounce several times per point. Looks like you need some compensations after collision (push the ball outside the object)

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
A very simple breakout clone. Wasn’t sure how it fitted the theme?

The mechanics of the game worked pretty well when hitting blocks.

The control method was a little clunky, perhaps the paddle could be smoother?

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: This game brought me back to 80s when I used to play Arkanoid ! According to me you could try to add some bonuses to spice up the game.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Good job on making a game!

Keep improving and working on your own game ideas once you have learned all the basics!

comments.author: John Swana
comments.text: Niceyyy hey next time add effects pucks and bonusses still amazing

comments.author: newman s lanier
comments.text: You are a winner! Thanks for creating a game. Otherwise, this game sucks. You can do better and I hope you do next year. Way to go, Javi! You are a game builder and participated in JS13K2023!

comments.author: Elliot Nelson
comments.text: Functional breakout clone! For next steps, I would look into using using keys “held down” (instead of pressed) and move every frame, so that the player’s bar can move with same smoothness as the ball.

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text: Classic breakout, always fun. I’d recommend moving the paddle faster, and let it react immediately when the player presses the arrow keys.

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text: As a first game, that’s cool! I love how the paddle is called ‘user’ in the code :)

comments.author: Ania Kubów
comments.text: Very cool game of breakout! I hope you got inspiration from my own Breakout game tutorial on YouTube :). What would be cool is to see some theme applied that is in-keeping with this years competition theme of the ‘13th Century’. Or perhaps add some unique spin on the breakout blocks and ball? Would love to see how you take this project to the next level!

comments.author: Javi Rana
comments.text: Yes i sawn ur you tube videos and learnt a lot from them. I have made this just from copying you before than made it myself i would love to take this project to nect level. I made more games through your videos like candy crush, trex run, space invaders, doodle jump, molly game, memory game , Rock paper scissors, Whack a mole , Chess and i found the way u make is really simple . Really appreciate your work i have learned a lot from you thanks a lot 😊😊

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: as simple as they get, some games are a good start point, clear prespective is always encouraged keep it up

api.name: K1n3kt Archery
api.description:
This archery simulator game uses the webcam to detect lines on image and create a medieval virtual bow.

How to play

  1. Hold a pen or something similar close to camera and rotate it to aim your virtual bow 🖊️🤏
  2. Hold and release spacebar to shoot a arrow and scare the chicken 🐔 and avoid it cross the road
  3. If it doesn’t work well, try playing in a brighter environment with fewer objects that can trick the game.

comments.author: Alpine Games
comments.text: Wow this is one of the most unique entries I’ve seen!

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text:
I feel like I need to go back to all the past entries I’ve ever scored and deduct 2 or 3 stars from “Innovation”.

This is definitely the most innovative game I remember playing!

That said, it’s a bit janky. I felt it got easier if I pointed the camera up and used a pen in front of it.

comments.author: Thiago Romão Barcala
comments.text: Worked for me on Firefox, but not on Chrome. I didn’t manage to make it work very smoothly though. Nice idea! My highscore was 1125 points.

comments.author: Daniel Cohen
comments.text: So impressive to use the camera. Once I got to Firefox it was really cool. Great job.

comments.author: Crystal Parker
comments.text: I like the innovation, but I couldn’t get it to work.
games: Journey to the East; Silk Road Adventure

comments.author: Artur Augusto Martins
comments.text: Hello, test on Firefox. The way the dev.js13kgames.com server has been configured is blocking some webcam features.

comments.author: Justin Sullard
comments.text: I tried but no matter what I did I couldn’t get the game to aim. I’m not convinced that it was actually using my webcam to be honest, but I gave it a go and was still able to occasionally shoot a chicken or two as the bow bobbed back and forth. I would love to have seen the aiming work for me.

comments.author: Artur Augusto Martins
comments.text: Hello, test on Firefox. The way the dev.js13kgames.com server has been configured is blocking some webcam features.

comments.author: Andrei Iacob
comments.text: This game has the coolest controls I’ve ever seen in a game jam entry, and I’ve seen a lot of controls. Like Xbox Kinect but in a Js13k entry. Though the image processing could be more accurate if you asked the player to use a certain color of a pencil, and take the highest and lowest color pixel.

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Well done:

  • Camera image processing in js13k!? That’s amazing

Feedback:

  • I didn’t realize that the pen had to be sideways to aim the bow. For a good while I was trying to hold the pen in the direction to aim the bow (so straight up to aim straight up) and it wasn’t working. Thankfully the processed image in the background helped me understand eventually, but would have liked that to be more clear (either in the instructions or the game)
  • My arm is way tired lol

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: Looks like the game can’t recognize my laptop camera ( I tried different places and backgrounds, but it looks like a noise without stable lines.

comments.author: Armaan Mohammed
comments.text: 5 stars for innovation!!!!!!!!! 😃😁😁

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: Unfortunately I was not able to have this game working with my camera (Edge & Chrome), but I really liked the concept!

comments.author: Artur Augusto Martins
comments.text: Hi, test it in Firefox. The way dev.js13kgames.com server was configurated is blocking some webcam features.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Ive given points for innovation even though I could not get this working, tried mobile and PC but no luck.

Cool idea just a shame it did not work. If there is more information on how to test this happy to re-vote.

comments.author: Artur Augusto Martins
comments.text: Hi, test it in Firefox. The way dev.js13kgames.com server was configurated is blocking some webcam features.

comments.author: John Swana
comments.text: Nice game mate good chickens too!!!

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text:
It’s impressive that you managed to incorporate camera capture technology within a 13kb game! Careful though, the entry doesn’t work on Chrome and Brave. I had to switch to Safari to properly play it.

Controls are still a bit wonky when I played with the camera on, but the idea is interesting.

comments.author: Luke Stapley
comments.text: I wasn’t quite able to use it with Chrome, but I like the idea. Something that makes me wonder if you could do something more with the LIDAR on iPhone as well. This is great.

comments.author: Jeremy Burns
comments.text: Really like the concept, but I couldn’t get it working unfortunately!

comments.author: Ania Kubów
comments.text: Thoroughly enjoyed the uniqueness of this game! I was not expecitng to get a pen out while judging js13k (and to be honest it took me a while to find one in 2023) but once I did I could not put it down! Very well done on maknig something original. I am also a big fan of silly concepts so chickens mixed with bows and arrows was very much up my street! The background visuals also helped in understanding the relationship between the camera and the game.

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text:
Sadly, I couldn’t find a position where the webcam thing would work for me :(

But I appreciate the thought process very much, such a cool idea!

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
I wasn’t able to play as I don’t have a useable webcam.

I like the concept of the game and the novel control method.

The graphics are cute.

I’ll have to try on a different platform

api.name: One Guard
api.description:
A 13th century tower defense game, set before the invention of the balloon in 1824.

You are the one and only guard protecting the largest defense tower. Nothing much happens here so you took the job. One day day you are attacked by another kingdom.

Can you defend the tower?

Controls

  • Click to start, reset, and shoot
  • Press “m” to toggle mute

Enemies

  • Basic > A slow moving enemy
  • Superfast > Faster green enemy
  • Megafast > Twice as fast red enemy

Upgrades

  • More Arrows > 5 more arrows for every following round
  • Arrow Speed > Faster arrows
  • Bow Speed > Less cooldown between firing arrows

Thank you for playing!

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text: Super simple gameplay loop - but I found myself playing it multiple times. Kudos!

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text: Simple and fun. Those red-shirted guys were super fast! I found that Arrow Speed is probably all I needed.

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text:
The audio was really nice, and the bow following your cursor was a nice touch.

Either a health refill upgrade or the ability to continue would have been good, and the hurtbox on the tower seems a little big, it felt like I took damage just before the enemy hit it, which felt unfair.

Still, while it’s simple, it’s well done. Nice game!

comments.author: John Swana
comments.text: Short game lots of ideas nice sound

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Well done:

  • A literal tower defense, nice
  • Laughed at the Bloons TD reference
  • Simple controls and upgrades

Feedback:

  • Megafast lived up to it’s name. Not sure I was ever able to hit one :)

comments.author: Justin Sullard
comments.text: A solid entry, and one with some a bit of strategy at play with the upgrades, and enough variation to support different player styles. I liked the music and the “tips”.

comments.author: Andrei Iacob
comments.text: The music is super cool, great game! Would like to see more upgrades/enemy types.

comments.author: Andy Killorin
comments.text: I loved the atmosphere, nice audio.

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: Simple game defence game, just need to hit enemies with a bow. Not super interesting, but where is some gameplay and even upgrades.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
I enjoyed playing, felt a bit like a missile command game but upside down.

The different enemy types were good and I played through a few waves with the various upgrades.

I can see this game being easier on a touchscreen.

comments.author: Crystal Parker
comments.text: I liked it, the music was good, and the bow and arrow worked well. The graphics could have been a bit better. The kill box was a bit generous as you could shoot over their head and still kill them. I found it a bit annoying that you couldn’t shoot them at the bottom of the tower.
games: Journey to the East; Silk Road Adventure

comments.author: Thiago Romão Barcala
comments.text: Simple but fun! The megafast ones were very tricky for me. I managed to get to round 10 after a lot of tries, but didn’t manage to finish the game.

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: Super cool entry, I enjoyed playing it. I liked the faster arrows and was really scared by megafast red enemies :). I also liked the relaxing music!

comments.author: Brian Hambley
comments.text: Very likeable. Determined to complete this, got to lvl 9 so far :)

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Simple but fun!

What could have improved the game:

  • More pixel art, background, trees, dirt path, sky etc.
  • Keep the pixel art all the same size
  • Cursor could have been a crosshair
  • HP etc to collect
  • Some particle FX

Could have maybe had the tower fall down by one block height everytime it was hit to add to the tension of being hit.

Over all a fun game!

comments.author: CaptainAligna
comments.text:
Despite being a very simple game, I had a lot of fun playing it!

It is exactly what I expected of a tower defense, the controls were simple and everything was clear.

And yes, I did enjoy the music~

comments.author: ch!k0
comments.text: it was spot on the theme and the gameplay was interesting. however the controls didn’t include the mouse pointer/movement to change directions for the aim and it would have been nicer if there was a target symbol for the player to identify which direction the bow was facing-all in all it was fun! thank you very much!

comments.author: Matt Hackett
comments.text:
Playable & fun little castle defense game. Reminds me of Asteroids but with a medieval theme. I believe there was an upgrade screen but it skipped by quickly when I thought I was clicking to shoot my arrow (a delay before showing the popup might help with that).

Appreciate the music, sounds, & breaks between levels. Nice work!

comments.author: Björn Ritzl
comments.text: Congrats on completing JS13k! The game is pretty well balanced, although the red mega fast enemies are annoyingly hard to hit.

comments.author: Jedidiah Weller
comments.text:
Hi there!

Great job on this submission - it was a classic tower defense with a good implementation and solid mechanics. Here’s my review and feedback:

  1. Overall, great game and fun to play. Made it to level 9 on my first play through and was upset I couldn’t get further :)
  2. I’d suggest a little more randomality to how the enemies spawn. I could see the pattern of left to right to left (etc) and thought it became a bit predictable towards the end.
  3. I couldn’t review the source code because the link didn’t show any public repositories
  4. It would have been nice to add some cinematic elements to the transitions between levels, such as dramatic pauses or fade/in fade out. Perhaps you can consider something there for transitions?

Feel free to hit me up on Twitter/X @jedihacks to have a discussion!

comments.author: Alpine Games
comments.text: Does this link work for you? https://github.com/Alpine-Games/One-Guard

api.name: Gibbonacci
api.description:
Gibbonacci is an idle/incremental game about you getting bunch of monkeys to check numbers for you to find all that belong to Fibonacci sequence. Why? Only you know the answer to that…

Theme - 13th Century

Fibonacci sequence first appeared and was popularized in Europe, in 1202 through a book called “The Book of Calculation” by Leonardo of Pisa, also known as Fibonacci.

Decentralized

Game was also partially inspired by Bored Ape Yacht Club NFTs

comments.author: burntcustard
comments.text:
Played this all the way through on my phone yesterday evening!

Only 3/5 for theme because although the Fibonacci sequence is 13th Century themed (I did not know that before!) I’m not so sure about the monkeys lol.

It’s not 100% clear how helpful each monkey and the gorilla are, even with the numbers and the wiggling. I think other cookie-clicker-like games suffer from that too, so it’s not a major issue. It also feels a bit like there isn’t much choice in the game, until you can choose between upgrading the gorilla or upgrading the monkeys, and even that choice doesn’t seem to not make a huge difference, as they’re both good things to do.

The colour changing over time (or over the progress bar filling up, I guess it could have been either) was very cool, although I was a little concerned my monkeys were sick when they went green haha.

Also it’s nice to see games that work really well on both mobile and desktop!

comments.author: Alpine Games
comments.text: If you like clicker games, this is for you. Simple and easy Gibbonacci is well made and simple.

comments.author: Ariel Wexler
comments.text: Loved it. I don’t think I’ve ever played a cookie-clicker style game to completion. Great work.

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text: Simple clicker. Quite entertaining!

comments.author: Konrad Linkowski
comments.text: Real cool idea and perfect execution

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text: Having finished the game when it was first announced on Slack, I like its style: the changing colors, the particles… It looks very polished.

comments.author: Andrei Iacob
comments.text: Cool game, I’m very bad at maths. Very juicy, I like the color pallette

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: Well, weird idle game. Not so interesting for me and gameplay isn’t great, but, it’s clear and accurate.

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Well done:

  • Graphics and UI were clear. Liked that the price color changed to indicate when you could purchase it
  • Liked that you got a resource for not Fibonacci numbers to use to upgrade the monkeys
  • Little calculation symbols around clicking and monkeys is a nice touch

Feedback:
