  • Once you yourself become a monkey, there’s not much left for you to do. Would have liked a bit more interaction at that point other than click to upgrade an auto-clicker

comments.author: Crystal Parker
comments.text: Fun clicker game. It was original to tie in the Fibonacci sequence. I thought it would have been nice to have some gentle background music.
games: Journey to the East; Silk Road Adventure

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: I really like the graphics and the game is very well made! I would have loved having also a background music.

comments.author: Viljami Peltola
comments.text: The series is familiar but I found it a bit hard to understand. Graphics and animation was great!

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text: Nice idle game. I like the juice and animations.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text: Simple and fun, played on mobile and ran smoothly. Well put together if not a little quirky :D

comments.author: John Swana
comments.text: Good audio soothing…… but besides that i honestly don’t know what’s going on :(

comments.author: newman s lanier
comments.text:
Theme: I love fib! GP: on the one hand, it’s simple. but on the other, why? :) I guess I’m still discovering. I just click on the check but what do the monkeys do? I could click on them and then not the others. Why are they positioned there?
Audio / Graphics - pretty good audio and it somehow fits with the graphics Controls - just clicking the check button. I think . It’s a nice button

Thank you I love your effort and idea. To improve, maybe a cool fib visualization or some good documentation on how to play.

comments.author: ch!k0
comments.text: very simple and entertaining. the graphics were very cute too.

comments.author: Allen Schmidt
comments.text: This was way more fun than I thought it would be! I love all the animation and movement, and the slightly offset sounds for any actions makes clicking feel very nice early on. I was a little disappointed once I became a monkey since I couldn’t click as fast as I could anymore, but it gets kinda hilarious after that when the numbers change to scientific notation. Really well done!

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text: Well, hard to describe. Curious nonetheless. Nice presentation!

comments.author: Brian Douglas
comments.text: I enjoyed this. Was confused at the start, but actually it’s a real achievement. Well done.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
I liked the look of the game and the concept, but I wasn’t really sure what I was doing, I’m afraid.

The history behind it is cool. Lots of clicking makes it more suitable for a touchscreen than a mouse or touchpad.

Just clicking the check button or one of the monkeys when their value was lower than the one at the bottom allowed progression on the progress bar, but that progress was very slow.

comments.author: Tim West
comments.text: This is a surprisingly fun & stylish clicker game! I appreciate that it has a definitive ending as something to work towards. I felt the total time commitment to complete the game was well-calibrated.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: ok clicking and clicking, plus on graphics and a bit annoyed that the game is just a click though the idea could have been utilized into something very challenging math quiz or so

comments.author: Tim Sulmone
comments.text: Fibonacci go brrrrrrrrrrr. I liked the reference to bored ape club. Lacked decentralized elements, but really well designed UI and sleek animations.

api.name: Archery Master
api.description:
Engage in thrilling archery duels against AI opponents in Archery Master.

Load your bow, aim precisely, and be the first to strike 70 targets to claim victory.

With three levels of complexity:

  • Easy.
  • Medium.
  • and Hard.

Challenge your archery skills and emerge as the ultimate archer in this intense competition.

comments.author: Ariel Wexler
comments.text: Love the idea, but i need WAY more juice to love this. I want sparkles and particles and clear signs of kicking my opponents butt. I want the bow to flex when I’m pulling it back and give me a sweeeeet boingggg sound/visual on release. The idea here is bang-on, but just needs that juice to take it over the edge.

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text: I keep getting a bug where the arrows fire sideways XD It was funny at the very least!

comments.author: Ben Fox
comments.text:
Love the graphics and style, it’s easy to figure out. The sfx were also pretty satisfying. Medium was a nice level of “challenge” - is Hard even possible? I may just be slow lol

The controls were a little tricky, using a mouse I wanted to bow to move depending on where my cursor was before I pressed the button, I feel like that would have made it easier to compete against the ai

comments.author: Justin Sullard
comments.text: The graphics looked great, and the sound effects were nice as well. I never could quite get the hang of my firing my arrow each round, but it was pretty fun to click around. Hard mode sort of just became a button mashing fest. A solid enry overall, though a much better experience on mobile.

comments.author: Andrei Iacob
comments.text: Cool game, the graphics are nice and the sounds are juicy, but it is very easy to beat if you can click fast :)

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: Simple mause controlled game, you need to aim and fire to targets. Not the best controls, but interesting enough. Simple, but good quelity.

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Well done:

  • Different difficulties for the AI was nice.
  • Sounds worked out really well

Feedback:

  • Aiming took awhile to figure out. Wasn’t clear how to control the bow or shoot
  • Sometimes the arrows wouldn’t fire in the direction the bow/arrow was pointed. They would go off at an angle or sideways even
  • I eventually figured out that if you aim straight ahead and fire as fast as you can, you’ll hit the majority of targets and always win (even at the hardest difficulty). Having levels where the majority targets aren’t always straight ahead would have forced me to try to aim more carefully

comments.author: Crystal Parker
comments.text: Nice clean game. I found it a bit hard since I had to click to turn the bow and click to shoot it. It might have been nice on desktop to have it follow the mouse.
games: Journey to the East; Silk Road Adventure

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Enjoyed the game, was more of a mobile game than a desktop one.

The CPU player is pretty good even on easy, you don’t have many arrows to play with and aiming is pretty tough.

The graphics were good and the swoosh sound effects worked quite well.

comments.author: Chris Subagio
comments.text:
I liked it! It’s a great take on a casual game. Graphics are nice and sharp, and communicate the intention really well. I love that it’s a head to head competition in such a small space, and the rivalry feels strong with the rush to get stuff before the opponent does.

On desktop the controls felt fiddly though, I often felt like it didn’t do what I thought it was going to.

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text: I like the graphics and the overall idea. This game would deserve some more “levels” and variations. Maybe some kind of “trick-shot”-mode? This would add to the longtime-motivation to play the game. Also, if you click fast and just straight, you can win every time with ease. But the few rounds i’ve played were fun ;-)

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: Nice casual game, very entertaining! I like the fact there are different levels of dfficulty and the sounds are great. To me, aiming with the mouse was quite difficult.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Nice graphics and very cool sounds! Nice simple idea well put together.

I think these games needs lots of cool FX to help spice them up a bit, could have helped to make it more visually pleasing.

A little tricky to control but got the hang of it.

comments.author: John Swana
comments.text: Nice game concept couldn’t tell which button does what but awesome!!

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text:
Simple concept but fun! It could be develop for two-player game in the future.

It’s a bit tough to control on desktop now. Maybe it could use mouse to control the direction and additional key for firing.

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text: Clean, polished, and with beautiful presentation, great job!

comments.author: Isaac Benitez
comments.text: Simple and engaging. I will for sure have a look to the AI implementation.

comments.author: Allen Schmidt
comments.text: Pretty cool little game! Definitely felt a lot like it was a minigame from the 2 Player Games app. Graphics and sound were nice, and I liked all the movement variations of the targets. Great job!

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text:
On the medium difficulty, the game is really fast (or maybe I’m really slow?), so my only chance of getting any score is by firing through the middle at the start of each round. Which kind of works, too.

Neat concept though! Would love it to be physics-based, like Angry Birbs of sorts.

comments.author: Tom Harris
comments.text: Simple but fun game with a nice concept. The visuals and SFX are great. The gameplay is enjoyable but doesn’t hold your interest for long. I found hard mode almost impossible and while playing on desktop, there isn’t much screen space dedicated to controlling the bow, making it a little awkward.

comments.author: Victor Debone
comments.text:
Hello! Thanks for your submission to js13k! I’m sure you’ve worked hard on your game, but I’m not aware of how much time you spent on it, so excuse me if things I mention were in your mind, but got left out in the rush of the month or just because you hit the 13kb limit :)

  • My first impression and understanding (before playing the game, only reading the description, screenshot and title):
    • From the screenshot it seems that I will be aiming at the box behind the it. I wonder what the circle in the middle is for.
    • The 70 number scared me :)
  • Great work on:
    • Having a complete finished and somewhat polished game!
    • A core loop that really works. I wonder where the 70 targets number came from :)
  • I liked that you:
    • Made multiple levels of dificulty.
  • You could improve (again, I’m aware you might already know all the below):
    • I tried to play on desktop but aiming was quite challenging.
    • I hope it works really well on mobile, because the harder level probably needs some fingers agility to beat the bot. I wonder if the placement of the aiming area does not cause problems on buttonless phones like the iPhone.
  • I took some screenshots https://twitter.com/javascripl/status/1709310356773097751

comments.author: Jedidiah Weller
comments.text:
Hey there Jorge,

Haha man - my fingers hurt from trying to outclick the “Hard” difficulty! Have you actually beaten it?

Really good job on this - loved the progression of easy-medium-hard, and loved the concept in general. Here’s my feedback:

  • Great graphics for a 13k game - I didn’t really notice typical pixelation
  • The UI/UX itself was self explanatory which was nice - my only feedback is on the bow controls. It seemed a bit counterintuitive for aiming - I often found myself clicking off the bow and on the screen. I’m not sure if that was intentional
  • One idea for the future is potentially having a visual hourglass / slow-down mode to see how much time you have before the AI shoots. It wouldn’t change the gameplay much; but I’ve found that showing visual representations of time decreasing often increase a player’s engagement
  • On the code side; I’d suggest implementing eslint & stylelint into your packages - especially if you want other community members to contribute; it’s best to have code standards in place first.

Overall, great game and great job! What’s next? Feel free to hit me up on Twitter/X @jedihacks to have a discussion!

comments.author: Lakshman
comments.text: Nice game!! I enjoyed playing it!! After playing some games I found that gameplay should be in full screen. The game play would have been more good looking if it was played in full screen by default.

api.name: Sir Bruno’s Minnelied
api.description:
The Minnelied is a traditional medieval love song.

In this game you will help the knight Sir Bruno, who set out to sing his Minnelied to Lady Sylvie, but instead was thrown into the dungeon.

Click (or tap) to move, to look at things, take them, or interact with them.

Smartphone users should turn their phone to landscape and enable fullscreen mode.

Music is based on melodies from the Jenaer Liederhandschrift and Tannhäuser by Richard Wagner, and can be turned off with the lyre icon.

comments.author: Alpine Games
comments.text: Fun puzzle game! Controls don’t really make sense for desktop.

comments.author: Ariel Wexler
comments.text: Nice little point-n-click adventure. I like that almost every room had its own song.

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text: Nice point-and-click game. I played on desktop and the controls felt natural to me.

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text:
Interesting little game. I think the key could have stood out a bit more ;)

I also could not find the helmet after 20 minutes.

comments.author: Andrei Iacob
comments.text: Fun point-n-click story! The graphics are cool, would be nice to see some SFX, and turn up the music level. Very cool game for a js13k submission!

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: It’s a whole adventure or quest like game, with characters, items and story. Of course not super complicated, but still.

comments.author: Crystal Parker
comments.text: I liked the music and how it changed in different scenes. I somehow ended up picking up 2 swords… maybe I double-clicked? The people looked pretty funny, but it was fun that he walked. The backgrounds looked good.
games: Journey to the East; Silk Road Adventure

comments.author: Rémi Vansteelandt
comments.text: I usually don’t understand/enjoy point and click games but this one was intuitive and short enough that I was able to finish it. Nice little adventure :)

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Well done:

  • Loved the music. Worked really well for the game
  • Good job on the hand drawn graphics. Impressive skill
  • The diagonal lines for stairs was very helpful

Feedback:

  • Took awhile to figure out that I could take the torch, even longer to see the key (it’s very difficult to see in the low light)
  • I have no idea where the helmet is, even after backtracking multiple times
  • The slow backtracking…

comments.author: John Swana
comments.text: Nice game ayyy… sir i love the lighting effects mostly

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: Cool game! I love the character animation, the lightening and the story.

comments.author: Ben Fox
comments.text: This is a very satisfying short game! The music reminded me a bit of old kings quest games.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text: Reminded me of a BBC micro adventure game, could not find the helmet.

comments.author: Luke Stapley
comments.text: Very fun point-and-click game that has me wondering if you could work on building more. Really fun game, and should look to do more of this. It’s not too tough, but has enough puzzles to keep you interested.

comments.author: Johan Åhlgren
comments.text: After a while, waiting for Sir Bruno to walk across a room became tedious. Maybe have a way to walk faster, or cross the room without waiting, at least after visiting the room once? A nice little adventure, well done!

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text: Funny to see a point and click adventure in this competition. It would had been fun to be able to use objects by clicking them to make it more like a puzzle. Great work.

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
You’re not a knight if you don’t have a sword, you’re not a knight if you don’t have a shield… what kind of girls… Give them a knight on a horse :).

Nice game - congratulations!

comments.author: Björn Ritzl
comments.text: Point and click adventures are usually quite story driven, but this is more towards a hidden objects. Not necessarily bad, and the game was enjoyable, although relatively short.

api.name: Number Knight
api.description:
Have you ever seen this fake ad for a mobile game? You know, the one with a small knight in his tower, and you have to kill monsters from other towers. “Number Knight” is a full complete game based on this fake ad!

Kill monsters and absorb their power to become more and more powerful! But beware, you’ll soon discover fire, water or plant monsters, and your attacks will have to be a little more tactical…

Game content

  • Resolve 30 levels
  • Create your own levels and share them to everyone thanks to the ingame editor

Mobile devices

Even if it’s optimized for desktop, the game should be playable on tablets and smartphones. But have in mind the experience is a bit degraded on small screens!

Decentralized bonus

Even if you can share your own levels via the editor, you can also save them on Pinata cloud (you DON’T need an account on Pinata to use this feature). On this external (out of the zip) page, you can set a private (and free) “Number Knight” key to save and access all your custom levels.

/!\ Because of some limitation with Pinata cloud API (it’s quite slow), your level list may not be up to date. It can take some minutes to refresh correctly. /!\

In bonus, a specific URL is generated for you: everyone clicking on YOUR dedicated link will be redirected to the main game, but instead of playing the base levels they will play all YOUR levels!

Graphics

Most assets are based on Kenney job (https://www.kenney.nl/assets), edited by myself. Except towers, made by BlackSpire Studio (https://blackspirestudio.itch.io/medieval-pixel-art-asset-free)

Sounds

Sounds created via tools from Xem:

  • https://xem.github.io/MiniSoundEditor
  • https://xem.github.io/miniMusic/simple.html

Thanks for your interest in this game, and happy playing :)

comments.author: Alpine Games
comments.text: This is one of my favorites! A fun and good looking puzzle game. Really makes me think about what move I should take next.

comments.author: Ben Fox
comments.text:
I enjoyed this a lot. Nice to be able to play a well-built version of this game style without a lot of “freemium” bs making be pay to revive myself!

I wish there was an in-game legend or key that could remind me of the various strength/weakness relationships and enemy specifics (skeletons, etc). The mechanics made it interesting and more fun but it was a lot of information to remember.

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text: Neat puzzle game. Another one in my top list! The level editor is awesome, really well done.

comments.author: Marcin Walczak
comments.text:
Nice little puzzle game with extra mechanics to spice things up.

Level editor with ability to share your creations is a big plus for me.

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: I didn’t think I’d enjoy this but I was wrong, great effort! I love the music because it’s really random, like my entry this year :)

comments.author: Crystal Parker
comments.text: Easy to play game that builds up to be more complex. The only thing that was annoying was on my old android I had to drag the game to recenter.
games: Journey to the East; Silk Road Adventure

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Well done:

  • Haha, love the idea of basing the game off a fake ad that I have indeed seen
  • The various types of enemies and elemental powers made the game have a lot of depth I wasn’t expecting
  • Kenney assets are always great

Feedback:

  • A legend to remind me of the elemental power rock-paper-scissors would have been great

comments.author: Justin Sullard
comments.text: I got stuck. I’m not sure if I just didn’t understand the skeleton mechanic, but no matter what sequence I attempted (of all 6 permutations) I always landed at a power of 10, which left me dead no matter which of the second tower block I attempted to take on. Perhaps I missed the correct sequence, but again, I may just not be getting how the skeleton mechanic works when in conjunction with the elements.

comments.author: Andrei Iacob
comments.text: Wow, very cool game, the graphics are rad! Cool way to decentralize, the gameplay is very fun!

comments.author: xem
comments.text: Congrats for releasing a game so polished without using a 2D canvas! Love the art, animation, sounds, music and level design. Great work poto!

comments.author: Lakshman
comments.text: I liked this game. It is very fun to play!!

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: Great job. It’s not only this fake ads, but for real, but also some logical puzzle like gameplay around with simple rules. Interesting puzzle game.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Easy to understand and play. Graphics and animations were great. Loved the little cute characters.

After each level in the voting app, the game disappeared, so I had to restart the game and then choose the next level from the level select screen.

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
Oh, how so i hate these ads, and how often did i think “why is no one REALLY createing such a game?”

Now it’s reality.

And it’s really well done, including a in game tutorial, the twist with the elements is cool.

I like the graphics, and the gameplay.

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text: Love the in-game editor, I had a similar idea wrt custom levels.

comments.author: Rémi Vansteelandt
comments.text: Cute graphics, simple gameplay, was able to finish a bunch of levels despite not really liking puzzle games usually. Great job!

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Solid entry, was a really fun game. Took a little while to remember the info I was taught but once you know the rules you can work out the order to attack.

Good Job

comments.author: John Swana
comments.text: I like the graphics for the most part good game and concept

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: This game is amazing! The graphics are smart and having a level editor is even more fun.

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
Fun game! I would never download the weird ad game, so thanks for making this– I can finally try it!

Nice introduction of new mechanics throughout to keep things interesting.

Didn’t realize you didn’t use canvas for this– I will definitely have to look at the code for this one..! Nicely done!!

comments.author: Armaan Mohammed
comments.text: Nice game, The thing you could improve is the background music. I noticed that there seems to be some but is extremely quite for some reason. Normally I tend to play longer on games with a nice background music sometimes even unknowingly. Music aside it is a fabulous game with beautiful pixel art. Great Job!

comments.author: newman s lanier
comments.text: Great game! I love the tutorial. I need to take a screen shot of the element power scale, but delightful strategy

comments.author: Christian Staudte
comments.text:
At the beginning of this game I was skeptical, “how difficult could this be?”. Welp, 5 minutes later and I was stuck at a level for quite some time.

The idea is great and the ability to design your own levels? Just wow! The only thing I didn’t like too much was the background music (compared to the rest of the game). But as there was a mute button that is fine (:

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text:
Incredible puzzle game!! The game is highly challenging, but it also provides a great sense of achievement when completed. The rules are simple but it becomes more and more challenging when all elements combined. Excellent game design! Furthermore, the level based tutorial is great. Well done!

The only suggestion is the game could add a level restart button.

comments.author: Allen Schmidt
comments.text:
Very cool interpretation of that ad! I like the tactical/puzzle aspect of it, even if I did get stumped a ways in. The music seems very random (and there’s a weird click at the start/end of every note?), but the other sound effects work well. I was missing a restart/choose level button, as I forgot a few times the order of what elements win over what other elements.

I kept getting stuck as I was writing this review! I like the amount of elements you have included as the levels go on… unfortunately I was unable to make it past level 29. Couldn’t figure out the correct sequence on the last set.

Overall well done!

comments.author: Alexander Petrov
comments.text: Could use mouseover tips when attacking x2 or /2. And an Undo button, so player does not have to replay entire level.

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text:
I can finally play the game that appears on my ad. I like how you interpreted the theme, because it’s very fitting. The rock paper scissors mechanic makes the game more interesting, and you have to think more carefully. Taking over the power of the enemy you kill is cool, and limiting the power to work only once is a great twist, you really have to think carefully to win the difficult puzzles. The graphics and animations are well made. Everything fit well together.

I wish the music had more variation, but sound effects worked well. Great entry overall.

comments.author: ch!k0
comments.text: fun, cute, the tutorial/help was very helpful in understanding the mechanics of the game and the sound effects were on point! amazing job done here! maybe shorten the sentence of the help/guide because it was a bit long for a very excited player to read and make the icons/images used on the help bigger and it’s 150/100 :)

comments.author: Tim Sulmone
comments.text: Really cool seeing Pinata cloud being used for the custom levels from the editor and was well implemented. The audio reminded me of old school dragon warrior/quest, gave me a little bit of nostalgia.

api.name: 13th Century City Builder
api.description:
This City Building game includes the inventions done during 13th century. Maintain the population, coins, amenities, industrial buildings to prosper the town and unlock new resources.

Controls are click, click, click…

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text:
I always enjoy a city builder.

I feel like this needed some feedback sounds of some sort. It’s not always clear what each thing does either but you learn as you go.

comments.author: Aniket
comments.text: thank You, I made a relaxing sound for the background but the file size was found to be too large for the rules so did scraped it.

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text:
Your idea is very cool but there does not seem to be any progression. It seems random when things happen and I left the page in frustration. maybe a day ticker would show the player that something is actually happening? Also when you click the mouse.. the hitpoint is wrong so you can click the wrong tile by mistake :(

After a while it just seems to crash and it does. There’s a console error.. Uncaught TypeError: cityData[(e - 1)] is undefined.

A pity, it looked like it might be fun.

comments.author: Andrei Iacob
comments.text: Nice game, reminds of the good old days of 8-bit computers :). Keep up the good work!

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Well done:

  • I liked that the houses could combine, was a fun discovery
  • Lovely graphic style

Feedback:

  • I was never sure what I was doing. What each building did or why I needed it was never clear
  • I’m not sure what the relationship is between the number of people and buildings. Do they work at the farms / mines? If so, how many do I need per building? Do I need a certain number to produce resources? More clarification would be very helpful
  • There seems to be a bug with producing resources and the number of farms you build? Twice I was doing just fine (with like 9 or so farms) and then built some more farms (like 5 more) and then no longer got resources at all. Couldn’t figure it out but would have to quit the game

comments.author: Crystal Parker
comments.text:
It’s a fun strategy game with replayability, I enjoyed it.

I had to open in another tab wouldn’t work properly in the embed, many of the games are having trouble with resizing partially due to the unexpected dimensions of the embed.

The only way to scroll through the research bar is to highlight the text and then it will scroll.

It would be nice to undo buildings for a partial refund, if you build too many houses to start with you run out of money and can’t earn any coins.
games: Journey to the East; Silk Road Adventure

comments.author: Aniket
comments.text: Thank you for your review,, actually I learnt HTML CSS JS during this competition and had a less knowledge of CSS which made the dimensions unable to work on PCs with different aspect ratios. Bg music and destroying buildings was a feature I did want to implement.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
I can only place road, tried everything to add houses, I had loads of roads and enough coins (I think).

I also placed a few farms but still could not add a house.

comments.author: John Swana
comments.text: This game has a nice concept however it breaks out of the window couldn’t play it thoroughly

comments.author: Aniket
comments.text: Sorry for your inconvenience, Its actually my first game and learnt html css js on the go, which made me have least experience in this field, would fix this in github repo further and improve it

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: The concept is easy to understand and you can have fun with this game very quickly. I think some sound / music could have improved the player experience.

comments.author: Aniket
comments.text: Bg music was a part of the game but learning html css js on the go and implementing it made the size of file unable to fit music/sound.

comments.author: burntcustard
comments.text:
I got far enough to be able to research everything and buy at least one of every type of structure, but I’m not exactly sure what was going on…

The artwork on the map was nice, but the UI and UI interactions (like figuring out where something was going to be built before clicking) was a bit wonky, and there was a lot of funny overflowing, scrolling, and showing/hiding of information like resources that maybe should have been always visible.

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text: I tried to build a city just like what your screenshot provide. But I’m not very sure how to earn money and what to do next. If the game provide a tutorial, I think it will be much better!

comments.author: Vivian Easley
comments.text: Really great game! I totally started playing it and losing track of time. I might add something to denote that the number next to buildings in the UI denotes cost. I assumed it was the number of those items. Great job.

comments.author: Luke Stapley
comments.text:
Very fun and played for quite awhile. The biggest thing I have an issue with is that there isn’t really a lot of information on the items, what they do, and why you need them. I still don’t know what the sword does.

It would have been nice to have at least instructions on how to add my first house as it took me a few minute to realize the entrance was on the left and not at the top.

Great work on a fun tile-based game!

comments.author: Victor Debone
comments.text:
Hello! Thanks for your submission to js13k! I’m sure you’ve worked hard on your game, but I’m not aware of how much time you spent on it, so excuse me if things I mention were in your mind, but got left out in the rush of the month or just because you hit the 13kb limit :)

  • My first impression and understanding (before playing the game):
    • This is going to be a clicker style game where I can put my buildings and watch as it goes.
    • The roads on the screenshot reminds me of “Mini Motorways”, I wonder if those are going to take some crucial part on the game.
  • Great work on:
    • Putting together a complete game play with growing levels.
    • Building a core loop where I put buildings, sell the produce and put more buildings, and so on.
  • I liked that you:
    • Created a small puzzle with the placement of the buildings. I need to plan a bit ahead to make enough space between the roads and know where I will have each building, and I can’t just start pilling up buildings in a corner.
  • You could improve (again, I’m aware you might already know all the below):
    • Apart from the obvious quality of life improvements in placement and buildings management, I think the next thing is giving better feedback to the state of my village.
    • For example, I went through 3 restarts with trial and error to get my coins somehow incoming at the beginning of the game. I still don’t think I understand what is the relationship between the amount of houses and farms, but it seemed that adding a lot of houses was not that bad as having overflow of farms. Eventually I started placing the more expensive buildings and got to the windmill and paper, where I think I got that windmills do paper after the research and then I felt I could reproduce this effect by adding more windmills.
    • I don’t think I know what half of the buildings were doing in my map. I could just feel that a building was about production of a sort or happiness.

I took some screenshots https://twitter.com/javascripl/status/1708978524218868129

comments.author: Taufik Sani
comments.text:
13kb SimCity, but in the 13th century. Loving the concept!

Really amazing that you’re able to fit your game in 13kb.

Controls are straight forward.

Suggestions:

  • Introducing a save-game option (or for the settings to be saved on browser cache) would be helpful for players who want to resume where they left off.
  • Not too sure if I haven’t played long enough, but is there a lose scenario for the player? From the looks of it, happiness dropping to 0% seems like the negative end game?
  • I think there is potential for this game to be developed into something more. SimCity is quite complex, and some gamers just want a less complicated game loop for city building games.
  • Add MIDI music that can reflect 13th century city building might help the player to get more immersed into city building

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Very much a sim city type vibe.

The low-rez graphics were good and the screen layout was intuitive.

I ran out of money very quickly following the “click, click, click” advice.

Will have to come back and play when I have more time.

api.name: Genghis Castle Battle
api.description:
Genghis, you have been summoned to the field of battle.

Defeat the enemy armies, build your horde, and survive the final onslaught.

Controls

Built for desktop. Control game using mouse.

comments.author: Alpine Games
comments.text: Game is fun to play, and the battle music is nice. The last round seems a little unbalanced.

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text:
Super fun to play. Nice music too! Took me a while to realise I could adjustr the soldiers’ positions.

It’s a bit annoying when the characters decide to kill the big boss while there’s an enemy archer picking your guys off

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Great game but I found the units hard to tell apart, even though they are different.

comments.author: Marcin Walczak
comments.text: Fun to play auto-battler with nice music. It took me a while before I figured up I can position units.

comments.author: Andrei Iacob
comments.text: Very fun game, though I would like to have no limits in placing soldiers, I can’t place soldiers after a certain number.

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Well done:

  • Fun auto battler
  • Good music
  • Changing graphics for winter was fun

Feedback:

  • The difference in unit types was hard to tell apart at first. They all looked too similar to me
  • Would have liked to see the unit count while buying units to understand how many I currently had deployed and the max allowed
  • Ability to delete / sell units. I got to wave 8 and my army couldn’t win, but I also couldn’t change it or upgrade it so was stuck always loosing the battle

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: Well, interesting auto battler/TD like game, you need to improve and develop your army in many battles. Some small problems with a balance, but good in general.

comments.author: Crystal Parker
comments.text: Fun battle game. I found it hard to win after the first level. It was a little hard to remember which soldier each was based on the color.
games: Journey to the East; Silk Road Adventure

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Enjoyed playing this to the end.

Liked that when you died, you respawn with the same army and with full health and more money!

Some of the characters looked very similar so were hard to tell apart.

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text:
Great game with nice graphics and good sounds. The theme is spot on.

The final boss is tough to beat, but it makes the game repayable, and you have to think about the best strategy to take him down.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Very well made, love the graphics and audio. The pre battle UI is neat, got to wave 8/12 and then was stuck, even with load of gold was unable to buy or upgrade. Would have been nice to sell troups so I could build a new army.

A little more visual difference on the types of soldiers would be useful.

Good Job!

comments.author: John Swana
comments.text: Nice game and i was able to finally win love the little physics and character ai hmmm…

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: I really liked to play this game. It is addictive, I like the gameplay and the simple graphics. Well done!

comments.author: Christer Kaitila
comments.text: That was so fun! I defeated the final boss thanks to special positional tactics (you can drag soldiers around before starting! I only figured that out near the end when my army was failing, lol! I always just started with default positions and won until the final battle. =) Great game, excellent art, everything about it was polished and enjoyable. Awesome work.

comments.author: Brian Douglas
comments.text:
Thank you very much for the kind feedback.

Positioning units is really useful. I hope player can discover the functionality.

comments.author: Noël Meudec
comments.text:
What I liked:

  • Simple gameplay mechanics, but it works
  • Requires some thinking before purchasing or upgrading units
  • Music and sound effects

What I liked less:

  • Watching the fights was not particularly exciting
  • I didn’t really feel my decisions were really impacting the game. It might come from the fact that I couldn’t really distinguish a type of unit from another. Update: there is more to the strategy of the game than what I experienced the first time around. Thanks to the developers for explaining the feature I missed!

Overall, it is a simple tower defense with fine graphics, music and sound effects. Well done!

comments.author: Brian Douglas
comments.text:
Thanks Noël,

In regards to “I didn’t really feel my decisions were really impacting the game.”.

Did you know you can move the units to position them before battle? Drag a unit to change it’s position. This allows for some battle tactics as different unit types have different targets. It also allows you to take upgraded units away from the front line.

api.name: It's Merlin
api.description:
Step into the mystical world of Medieval dungeon, where you play as the legendary wizard Merlin facing relentless waves of enemies and formidable bosses deep within the confines of a foreboding castle dungeon.

As Merlin, you must harness your magical prowess to thwart the advancing hordes and unravel the mysteries hidden within the ancient castle’s dark depths. Challenge your spell-casting skills, fend off menacing adversaries, and uncover the secrets of this enchanting dungeon in a relentless battle for survival.

Controls

  • WASD - move
  • Mouse - aim
  • Click/Hold - shoot
  • M - music on/off

Tested on the latest Chrome, Firefox, Safari.

comments.author: Sebastian Dorn
comments.text: Fun to play, I tried it multiple times. Would be nice to have the size scale up to the browser window. Also I died instantly at wave 12, because the boss spawned where I stood.

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text: The music rocks! I found the first waves to be a bit too long (I would prefer shorter waves that allow me to improve at a higher rate but adding crazier things later on). Still, a nice game!

comments.author: Alpine Games
comments.text: Challenging but fun to keep at it.

comments.author: Ariel Wexler
comments.text:
The early game fire rate / game pacing was too slow for me to get in-to. It was a deterrent from playing again (for me). Having bullets do half the damage but allow firing them twice as often would engage me more fwiw.

Amazing variety of enemies and bosses – great work. I wish there were SFX and not just background music.

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text:
I wish firing was a bit faster (even if it did less damage). The game feels a bit slow-paced because of that.

Great art and nice tunes otherwise

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text:
Medieval Smash TV! The graphics are amazing, and the controls are there, but the fire rate is so slow, and there aren’t enough upgrades.

I think following the Smash TV path more would have helped. Have weapon upgrades spawn when you kill enemies, and then have more advanced powerups between rounds. Plus at least double the base fire rate.

Some little tweaks and this would be one of my favorites.

As it stands, it’s graphically amazing, and still not bad, the fire rate and slow pace just prevented me from going back for any more than 2 play throughs.

comments.author: Marcin Walczak
comments.text:
Pretty good game. Nice graphics. Smooth controls. Catchy music.

Fire rate is a little bit slow at the start and you have to get used to fact you shoot from the orb and not center of your character.

comments.author: Andrei Iacob
comments.text: Nice game, but you should make hp heal itself faster… It is very hard. But I guess it’s because I’m a terrible wizard. Anyway great game, love the graphics!

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: Good game, some sort of archero-like top down shooter with perks and different enemies. It’s interesting that you are firing not from your character point but from his magical ball.

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Well done:

  • Graphics were fantastic
  • Audio track was good, didn’t find it repetitive and wasn’t too loud
  • Lots of nice variety in the monsters and they are easy to tell apart

Feedback:

  • The enemy ranged attacks from the 2nd monster could be more visually popping. I got hit a few times at first and didn’t understand why I took damage. Took a bit to see that they were shooting things at me because the projectiles blend in too much with the background
  • The first boss’s ranged attack could use some telegraphing to let the player know when it’s about to go off. It just happens without warning and doesn’t give the player any time to react before it hits them. I also didn’t know when the boss entered the different states (ranged attack vs fast follow) so didn’t know what to expect and how to react during the fight

comments.author: Crystal Parker
comments.text: The music and the graphics are good. I liked that the ghosts push you back if they reach you. I didn’t like the reload speed it was too slow.
games: Journey to the East; Silk Road Adventure

comments.author: Chris Subagio
comments.text: The sprites in this looked fantastic, really sells an aesthetic. The gameplay felt a little slow though. Maybe it got better later on with more upgrades, but I didn’t end up playing for long enough to find out. On these smaller games, you gotta unleash all the excitement up front!

comments.author: Thiago Romão Barcala
comments.text:
Very well made! I liked the graphics and the music. It was too hard for me (but maybe I’m the problem).

After playing some 30 matches I only managed to pass the first boss a few times. I wish there was an easier mode for people like me. :D

The upgrades didn’t help me much. I was always going for +1 HP per kill or damage increase, because restoring HP wouldn’t help me getting through the harder levels, but still, the damage increase was so mild that it almost didn’t change the result: I still had to shoot the skeletons twice to kill them, and the mages three times.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Pixel art is great, simple controls and some really nice animations and particle FX. Nice variation of enemies. Music is a nice loop.

Critical Feedback:

  • Add sound FX
  • Less enemy HP
  • Screen Layout could use full window
  • Disable right click

comments.author: Emre Guneyler
comments.text: nice, everything should be bigger, sound fx is a must, at least let me powerup before the game even starts so I know what the game is about.

comments.author: Rémi Vansteelandt
comments.text: Really cute and fun little game. I would maybe add a few more animations, particles when enemies are hit, sound effects, more feedback overall. Also maybe balance out the upgrades, I felt like the game got really hard really quick and I couldn’t get enough upgrades to counter that.

comments.author: Aleksey Litvinov
comments.text: Awesome graphics and music! And nice gameplay. Would be nice to have a possibility to use a key to shoot while aiming with mouse.

comments.author: John Swana
comments.text: Nice game there i love the sounds and characters and graphics and also the interaction between them

comments.author: Michael M.
comments.text: Unfortunately, I couldn’t properly move: I have “search as you type” enabled in my browser, and you don’t call .preventDefault() on the key events, so trying to move opens up the search for me.

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text: Great graphics, and supercool (but short) music! pretty hard gameplay, but the upgrades are good. Very good entry!

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text: The graphics is excellent! Especially the cute character design and the sprite animation. It’s definitely 5/5 on graphics rating. Great game but super challenging for me! Maybe the game can adjust the difficulty to make it easier for players in the earlier levels.

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text:
I enjoyed playing this game. It started to lag a few times when there were many enemies on the map. I also wished I got a warning before the boss appeared, because he spawned right on top of me, so I died.

Great music. Add a melody on top of it and you’ve got yourself a hit. Some sound effects will make the game shine even more, and add extra feedback to the player.

I’ve seen these characters on Itch.io before, but that’s not against the rules, and it looks really great in this game. Really cute animations.

The controls are easy to use, and the theme is well interpreted. Good job on this entry.

comments.author: Jeremy Burns
comments.text: Great concept and graphics. It’s balanced really hard! You could probably double the player fire rate and still not have it be too easy for the early levels. IMO you want the first few levels of a game to be sleep-walkable so that a player can learn the mechanics in a safe environment and learn how progression works before being thrown into the inferno. Still, it’s fun to play when you get the hang of it!

comments.author: Christer Kaitila
comments.text: such phenomenal graphics! I really love all your sprite work. the game played just wonderfully, smooth and exciting. I wish I could fire 4x as fast!!! I desperately wanted to SPAM BULLETS and felt really constrained by the fire rate, but it fit the pace of the game and it was fun moving in circles until I was overwhelmed. excellent game nice work.

comments.author: Frahaan Hussain
comments.text:
WOW. What a beautiful game, when it loaded I just thought, this is a game I could see on the app store as it is from a visual perspective.

Gameplay was nice, would have liked a tutorial/controls in the game itself to make it well rounded and some settings options but overall fantastic.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Love the graphics

The gameplay is a little tricky with combined mouse and keyboard in terms of coordination. I seem to have to spin around the enemies to corale them into the middle and then loop around them to shoot them.

The appearance of the enemies who shoot back adds another element of peril.

api.name: Battle commander - Middle ages
api.description:
Prepare for battle!

Choose your army and send them to victory. Press fight button to begin. Score points when you kill the enemy. Enjoy and conquer the campaign.

Controls

Mouse control

  • Left button to place the unit;
  • Right button to remove;
  • Use the scroll wheel to place multiple units;
  • Drag mouse to place continuously;

Better performance in Chrome.

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text: I like it very much! I wish there was an incentive for trying or retrying missions in different ways - once I found the best units, the composition of my armies changed very little.

comments.author: Alpine Games
comments.text: Creating your army is fun and the battles are interesting although it would have been cool for more types of soldiers later on.

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text:
A very fun little game. It’s simple but effective. Maybe the troops shooting the giant cannons are a little OP, but that’s kind of okay to me, in this type of game I like to be able to just make crazy armies and have fun.

I do think some incentive to like use less troops, or some other mechanic like that would help, but still, was fun! I was impressed by the fog of war effect as well!

comments.author: Andrei Iacob
comments.text: Very cool game! I love the idea, very juicy. But my computer’s fans are screaming when playing level 8 and up. Would be nice to see some music

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: Interesting puzzle like game about army building. You need to use different type of troops to deal with different types of enemies.

comments.author: Crystal Parker
comments.text: Good battle game. I was a little confused with the blank screen in the unrevealed army. I would have liked a move tool to reposition individual soldiers.
games: Journey to the East; Silk Road Adventure

comments.author: Chris Subagio
comments.text:
Great game! I finished the whole campaign happily. Graphically maybe the units could have had a little more differentiation, it was hard to see who was who on the big battlefield!

Gameplay was really fun. Not sure it was entirely balanced yet, but the idea works great. It’s engaging to set up formations and see how they behave. Well done.

comments.author: Thiago Romão Barcala
comments.text:
I had a lot of fun with this one! I completed the campaign in the easy mode, and played some levels in the hard mode.

I found a couple small issues:

  • When I finished the campaign, I clicked some buttons (don’t remember the exact sequence), then I clicked “Play” again somewhere, and I went back to my finalized game, and I couldn’t go anywhere from there. Not even the back button would work.
  • The enemy artillery seems to attack itself when there aren’t any units in their range to shoot. In the level 4 I added one “Testudo” at the very left side of my area, and one “Cavalry” at the very right. At first the enemy artillery would shoot my Cavalry unit, and then they start to explode themselves. It is also fun, but probably not intended. :D

comments.author: Rémi Vansteelandt
comments.text: I really like the ability to place a ton of units very quickly and come up with formation patterns. Animations are really cute, sending a hundred tiny soldiers to fight is really satisfying. Music would have been a great addition

comments.author: Misael Braga
comments.text:
It is really fun.

This is the funiest game i have played in this contest.

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Well done:

  • Great auto battler
  • Units had distinct visuals that was easy to tell them apart
  • Had fun with the different levels and unit combinations
  • Controls worked out really well with being able to place a lot of units at a time

Feedback:

  • Artillery seems a bit too powerful. I would mostly just line up a group of artillery and then a group of shield warriors in font of them to tank the army and let the artillery clean house
  • I was a bit confused at first on Level 5 (I think) where the initial playfield is in darkness. Didn’t know if it was a bug in the game or the game broke

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Great entry! Played this for quite some time and would love to come back and play again.

Graphics are good, cute animations, be good to have some music also.

The cannons are a bit overpowered, would be good to limit those on some levels and also see some walls etc on the levels but for a 13k game this is very impressive!

Can see this doing well overall.

** I think you have a typo campaing rather than “campaign”

comments.author: John Swana
comments.text: Good concept and nice fights couldn’t get past fist obstacle

comments.author: Alexander Petrov
comments.text: Solid autobattler, but a little but unbalanced. Artillery erases everything even on Hard

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: Lot of fun playing with this game! I like the battles with hundreds of warriors, I like the way warriors jump when they win, I like the controls (possibility to put lots of units just by left cliking),… Maybe the only improvement I may suggest is to add some music.

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text: My favourite game so far from the Jam. Superfun to play, very well presented.

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text: Super fun! It’s a very exhilarating game. The character in the game are so cute. And that’s great to see the ability of all solider types. Well done! The only advice is that maybe color scheme of the visuals could be adjusted to optimize the overall experience.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Graphics were cute. game mechanic was fun.

Sometimes difficult to see what was going on.

The mousewheel idea to change placement size was handy. Ran out of money very quickly then had no choice but to fight and ultimately lose.

Didn’t manage to win any battles, wasn’t sure how to strategise an efficient win.

The battles were really slow, could have done with a FFWD button.

comments.author: Jeremy Burns
comments.text: Nice game! It was fun figuring out a strategy for level 4 specifically, to avoid a tie from both slides blowing everyone up. The sounds work really well, and the interface is intuitive. The fog of war existing on my side of the map threw me off a bit, I thought the game had frozen on a black screen or something, but over all not a big issue.

comments.author: Arthur Brongniart
comments.text:
very fun an addictive game, I beat it twice :D

cool details such as the army page, with all the cute animations.

gameplay wise, If think the artillery is a bit overpowered, especially since the IA doesn’t use it much.

I like the level where the IA align a lot of artillery, where one cavalry triggers a chain reaction of suicide.

in term of performance, I could feel the game lagging with large army. Especially with firefox. I think it might show the limit of canvas 2d rendering (?)

The source code is pretty neat. congratulation !

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
I loved the graphics of the cores that had a shadow in flight! I also liked the graphics of the killed, as well as fog!

When artillery became available it became very easy :). My strategy: artillery, shields and archers :).

Although the theme of the game does not really resonate with the theme of the contest, I had fun and interesting, I completely completed the game, thanks and congratulations!

api.name: Trench Fisher
api.description:
In the 13th century, within the rugged lands of Livingstone, there lived a curious caveman named Jim. He defied fate’s unkind hand, driven by an insatiable urge to explore. Lake Kariba, an enigmatic expanse, lay before him, separated by the Eight Gorges, challenging all who dared.

Armed with a humble fishing rod and boundless determination, Jim embarked on a quest to conquer the treacherous gorges, unveiling Lake Kariba’s secrets. You, as Jim, must navigate these rocky trenches, battling nature’s fury and discovering ancient mysteries.

Guide Jim, and relive the legend of the caveman who defied destiny. The 13th century beckons; how far will you go in Trench Fisher?

Controls

Tap to press and use arrow keys to move left and right.

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
The art style is really cool! The colors and small screen size feel pretty unique! Main menu and maps look nice, too.

One thing I’m curious about is how the game takes place in such a small part of the screen. I don’t dislike it necessarily (feels even more unique), but there’s a lot of space above/around the “fish and hook collision area”.

But anyway, it’s a cool and relaxing game!

comments.author: Andrei Iacob
comments.text: Cool game, but all the levels are the same. Love the graphics and music!

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: SImple fishing game, not bad, different behavior of the fish and some small text based narration. But not super complicated.

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Well done:

  • The music worked really well for the game
  • Good visual indication to know when the fish was hooked

Feedback:

  • The different fish or fishing spots didn’t seem to be any different. Would have been nice if the levels weren’t all the same or the fish required different techniques to get or something

comments.author: Crystal Parker
comments.text: I like the backgrounds. The mechanic is somewhat innovative. It would make a good mini-game in a bigger game. Just like fishing, it’s kind of boring. The music was somewhat unpleasant.
games: Journey to the East; Silk Road Adventure

comments.author: Aleksey Litvinov
comments.text:
I guess it is an early MVP, however, I liked the overall idea!

Some feedback:

  • Would be nice to hide “hint” screen entirely after showing it multiple times (or show other hints).
  • Reflect global map position on the level screen, i.e. when player selects particular level (1-8).
  • Add possibility to close the screen from the keyboard.
  • Also had some issues in mobile safari with controls.

Loverly art and music, good job!

comments.author: Rémi Vansteelandt
comments.text: Everything looks, sounds and feels really good, however I do think the game would have benefited a lot from switching up the map slightly. Once I realized it was always the same spot, it felt repetitive which held me from playing more levels.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Over all a good idea but needs more gameplay to make the game more fun.

You have music, menus, a game loop etc just no variation. All fish act the same and its the same method to catch them all.

Nice pixel art also!

comments.author: Brian Hambley
comments.text: I quite liked this once i got going. Perhaps some variation, like a shorter time or horizontal and vertical sections, multiple fish? Played to completion.

comments.author: Ben Fox
comments.text: I was looking so hard at the fish/lure that it took me a long time to see how the Progress % above worked, putting that at the bottom would have been helpful

comments.author: Grav
comments.text: The presentation here is very nice. The graphics and audio work together to create a fresh feeling, it actually reminded me a lot of a GBA game. As mentioned some gameplay variety would have gone a long way, but easier said than done in this competition. Great work.

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text:
Nice if simple little fishing game.

While a bit more depth and variety would be nice (multiple fish, some faster and slower, different fish weights, different lures, etc), I think the biggest issue for me is being bombarded with popups I have to close. Don’t make users sit through a loading screen if you’re not loading anything, thats being actively hostile to a user. Show the hints in small windows at the top of the screen so they don’t interfere with my navigation or gameplay and have them go away after a certain amount of time.

The graphics are nice and if you just tweak some navigation and gameplay I think it’s a really nice basis for a fun relaxing fishing game

comments.author: Richard Davey
comments.text:
“You caught a tigerfish!”

I’m a complete sucker for fishing games and honestly, this was pretty fun :) You can pick from a variety of lakes to fish from, and you simply have to keep the float lined-up with the fish as it swims left and right. Hold it for long enough, until progress hits 100% and you catch it.

And that’s it really. The fish get a little more tricky the further you get, but not a whole lot. There isn’t much challenge here, but in a way, there doesn’t need to be. It’s still quite engaging. The only downside is that you have to take your eyes off the fish in order to check both the progress % and time left. Perhaps a shift in the UI could help here? i.e. a progress bar above your rod, or similar. I would have liked to see the fish actually ‘caught’ too, but I appreciate there’s only 13kb. Bonus points for the music from the Atari ST too :)

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text:
The graphics are cute and work well together. I’m not sure how to play the game properly and don’t know what I’m doing.

The music sounds familiar, is it a cover of some sort?

api.name: Casual Crusade
api.description:
Use your deck of tiles to build a path covering all the lands spreading the good word while also pillaging some rewards as additions to your deck or sometimes even blessing you with some powerful abilities.

Played with mouse (or touch) only.

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text:
This game kind captured me for quite a while.

Graphics look amazing, the animations are super cute.

I wish there was an undo or restart button though.

comments.author: Jani Nykänen
comments.text:
One of the most original and polished games I have seen this far, definitely one of my favorites. I especially love the audio and the visuals.

Regarding visuals, the font does not seem to work on Linux (I tried on Edge & Firefox), at least it looks different than on the screenshot.

comments.author: Marcin Walczak
comments.text: A great puzzle game with interesting gameplay, nice graphics and sound design.

comments.author: Justin Sullard
comments.text: This is a pretty awesome entry, and gorgeous. The music was just the right feel, and it synced well with the animations. I felt like the balance of luck vs strategy could be tweaked just a bit, but that is my only real feedback I feel could improve what is an awesome game.

comments.author: Andrei Iacob
comments.text: VERY juicy game, love it! The puzzle factor of the game is nicely integrated, feels like a Nintendo original! Keep up the good work, your games are great!

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: Interesting and unusual puzzle game, themed with some religion motives. Interesting mechanics about building paths by cards.

comments.author: Crystal Parker
comments.text: I really enjoyed this game, it built up to be more than it started as, and it got quite challenging as it went on. Re-playable and fun. I liked the music and the lightning strikes add extra appeal too.
games: Journey to the East; Silk Road Adventure

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text:
I couldn’t stop playing, super cool game.

The only feedback I have is that I wish you couldn’t get more than one tile of the same type on your hand - in many occasions I had perfect tiles on my deck but had no way to use them and died quickly. (My record so far is 65k points! :) ).

comments.author: Adrien Guéret
comments.text:
I love this game! Very intersting mechanic, with smart bonus. We always want to progress and opening chests to increase our power!

Just a bit sad that it seems we don’t have anymore chests to open after a while: the excitation to continue fades a bit from this point.

But the core concept is great, I would love to see (even buy :D) a more complete version of this game!

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Great concept, all put together very well. Catchy music and nice visuals, rewarding game loop.

My only feedback - Was a little confused after collecting the chests why I had to continue putting down symbols, realised after I had to cover all the land but the chests felt like the natural target.

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Well done:

  • Unique concept of a puzzle / card game
  • Great visual and music
  • Loved that the pawn bobbed in time with the music
  • Great job with having the colored tiles have a popup as well

Feedback:

  • Like other card games, I wish there was a way to not choose a tile when given the tile options. I got pretty far into the game but didn’t get more useful tiles (only dead end ones or one direction), which started to hurt my ability to place tiles on the board. I lost with a hand size of 8 or so because i drew all my one way / dead end tiles (all my 3+ way tiles were in my deck) and couldn’t place them in such a way that would let me fill the board
  • I’m not sure what the “Gem” reward item does. Are gems the colored circles on some tiles?
  • At one point all the tile options to pick from were the same tile. Would be nice to have some logic to prevent being presented with all the same tlie

comments.author: John Swana
comments.text: Good sounds smooth i don’t know how to win. I love it still

comments.author: Ben Fox
comments.text: Interesting game, I wish when opening a chest that the card picked would go immediately into the hand to allow for a little more strategy, or maybe to be able to see more upcoming cards. I got some plays where early on I lost a lot of life just through luck of the draw.

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: Super game, I could not stop playing! Everything is very well done and I really loved the animations!

comments.author: FoumartGames
comments.text:
Absolutly astonishing game. The best idea I’ve seen so far. Kudos! Loved the puzzles and the randomness - some of the puzzle elements were quite a gamble like Fill neighbors, or dead ends with bonus. Addicting!

I played on an android phone and was not able to go fullscreen, also experienced a few glitches (Info popup covering the field or not getting hid, getting reward during endgame screen, but maybe it was just my lagging phone).

comments.author: Richard Davey
comments.text: What a superb game! I played this for ages (nearly an hour), getting through to the ‘THE ARAGONESE CRUSADE’ level, and after that repeated again, I stopped. I’m not sure how much further I was from the end of the crusade? The visuals are brilliant, with cute animations and sound that doesn’t outstay its welcome. The first time I played it, I didn’t quite get it, but on my second play, it all just clicked. Place the tiles to fill the land, don’t leave any gaps, and try to open all chests! The tiles do NOT have to form a perfect circuit (which caught me out originally), but the player does have to be able to move to them. Once you figure that out, it’s really quite joyous. Excellent game!

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Nice looking games, the graphics were good, also the music was pretty funky.

I wasn’t sure exactly how to complete the objective of covering the lands. I felt like I was randomly placing the tiles and always seemed to complete the levels?

The animations and level transitions look good.

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text:
Excellent game! Super polished. I can’t imagine that is within just 13kb! I can’t stop playing the game. This is definitely a game I will play again and again!

The sound, gameplay and graphics are so great! Besides, the UI is simple and attractive. It’s very intuitive to know how to play the game. Good job. Furthermore, the game provides several rewards greatly increases the fun of the game. Nicely done!

comments.author: Francesco Bedussi
comments.text: Nice graphics and sound. Fun to play.

comments.author: 0ctothorp
comments.text: I really enjoyed this game, although once I got a grasp of how to play, it got pretty easy on the further levels. Nice music and nice minimalistic graphics. For some reason, the whole experience brought the OG flash games feel, which is great 😄

comments.author: Allen Schmidt
comments.text: Found this on the front page of Itch! Really nice little puzzle game! Feels very complete, especially for only being made in 13k. Great job!

comments.author: newman s lanier
comments.text: simply wonderful. I didn’t really know what’s going on but still played. The pace and cadence is delightful. Really fun

comments.author: Isaac Benitez
comments.text:
Great game! Pretty juicy. It feels great to play. The graphics are pretty simple and nice, and the music and sound fx are pretty good as well.

I discovered that if you hit the “c” key, you can get rewards forever. :P

Congrats! Great game!

comments.author: Antti Haavikko
comments.text: Yup, completely missed turning off the dev keys before submitting.

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
Really fun game! Like single player “Carcassonne” with a nice music track! I spent quite a lot of time playing this and figuring out the various power-ups.

I did find one bug. If you click the “Sainthood” powerup (increase your health by two), it will add two health each time you click. I think that’s not the intended effect

comments.author: Antti Haavikko
comments.text: Yes, definitely not intended. Forgot to disable clicking on them (ie. picking the option) when they dock to icon mode.

comments.author: Noël Meudec
comments.text:
What I liked:

  • Cool music!
  • The puzzles are increasingly hard, at a reasonable pace
  • The gameplay mechanics (with items and some strategy)

What I liked less:

  • Nothing, really. This game is quite good!

Overall, a super fun puzzle game with a unique atmosphere. Well done!

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text: An original puzzle game! And a very clever one at that.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: graphics and animations are awsome, I liked the idea and played for a while, puzzle is cool

comments.author: Jedidiah Weller
comments.text:
This game was super engaging, seriously great job and great submission! Here’s my feedback:

  1. The interactions were on point - bravo! At first I was hesitant, but then the interactions brought me fully into immersive gameplay
  2. Good work on the subtle features, such as the Cross!
  3. The reward system & bonuses felt well placed, so really good work there
  4. In general, the gameplay itself was just superb so good work!
  5. UI - One UI improvement is on the text. Using a full screen desktop monitor, the text often looked blurry so you might want to look into using REM or responsive implementation there
  6. Source Code - Readme is needed! Always include a readme <3
  7. Also could use a license file and code standardization, such as eslint, prettier, etc.
  8. Webpack files should probably be moved into a folder for organization

Overall it was a great submission, and I look forward to seeing what you do with it next. Hit me up on twitter @jedihacks for any discussion!

api.name: Magna Carta
api.description: Try to fit all the given words on the provided space. The words are only allowed to overlap just like in crosswords or scrabble. Played with mouse only by dragging the words to the play area. You can also rotate the words by right clicking (or pressing space if you lack the button) while dragging them.

comments.author: Alpine Games
comments.text: That art is amazing for just 13k!

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text: Amazing art. Interesting concept

comments.author: Daniel Cohen
comments.text: This is so cute! One small level to do the rotation & overlap concepts would have been helpful. Maybe it’s just me, but by the time I figured out everything I could do, I felt like I had already gone too far in my placement choices. I loved the feeling you created with the animations and characters. Well done, great stuff!

comments.author: Justin Sullard
comments.text: I love the idea, but honestly I struggled to be able to get beyond 60% filled in before I realized that my… 7th or 8th main shuffling was still not going to work. I feel like some sort of glimpses at the solutions, hints, or some other way to avoid the inevitable massive shuffling necessary to start getting things to fit would put the finishing touches on a great little game.

comments.author: Andrei Iacob
comments.text: Amazing game, very juicy! Though it is very hard, I enjoyed it! BTW, isn’t magna carta in Latin? Anyway I love the music, very polished game.

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Well done:

  • Graphics, animations, and music are super polished
  • I got a good chuckle when I realized you could rotate words backwards (but at the same time this makes the game more difficult)

Feedback:

  • When the first intro popups are open I couldn’t figure out how to dismiss them. Didn’t realize I could click on the words at that point to make it disappear
  • Super difficult once you try to start putting words down as you have no indication on if your placement is close to correct
  • Could use some sort of way to limit the choices per square that allows you to narrow down the correct solutions. Like how in Sudoku the rules are set up so you can eliminate possibilities as you go, and the initial board setup dictates the game and solution

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Quite a tricky crossword fitting game. The sound was funky and the bouncing graphics were good.

Didn’t manage to complete it but tried a few times and with different orientations of words.

I’ll come back to it when I have more time.

comments.author: Emre Guneyler
comments.text: superb, unfortunately i don’t have the mental capacity to solve word puzzles which is quite challenging.

comments.author: Adrien Guéret
comments.text:
Ok, this is “just” a words game, but the animation are so well made…! I love this little god, I can’t stop myself to smile when seeing it :D

It’s a shame I’m bad at this kind of games. I would love to finish it, but I just can’t x’)

Very nice game overall, very well done!

I love the sheep popping in when you make a good move (though it would also pop in when I removed mistakes from the board haha). I didn’t manage to get all the words in place, but I really like that you can rotate the words to be any direction, as it made it so I could get to 80% at least. Well done!

comments.author: Victor Debone
comments.text:
Hello! Thanks for your submission to js13k! I’m sure you’ve worked hard on your game, but I’m not aware of how much time you spent on it, so excuse me if things I mention were in your mind, but got left out in the rush of the month or just because you hit the 13kb limit :)

  • My first impression and understanding (before playing the game, only reading the description, screenshot and title):
    • The cover art has a really good quality, and it apparently shows me that this game is going to be about actually making sentences together?
  • Great work on:
