I think there is an issue with the names showing as undefined (although the description helps). Controls and UI could also be streamlined a bit more.

Good start, welcome to the competition. Please continue working on this game.

api.name: When Your Neighbors Are Mongols
api.description:
As the brave samurai looked out across the field of battle at the endless horde of mongol warriors advancing from the west, he knew his time was near. Nonetheless, he gripped his BFG tightly and vowed to take as many of the foreign invaders with him as he could.

Gameplay

When you neighbors are mongols is a random as game I made for js13k 2023, where you must do your best to hold back the mongol horde as long as you can.

The samurai will fire his BFG automatically, leaving you to the important tasks of avoiding swords and spears thrown by mongols, as well as collecting powerup cubes to empower him and lay waste to many mongols at once.

Powerups

There are several powerups that will aid the samurai in his bloodlust…

  • Health (H) - Restores an amount of the samurai’s health.
  • Walkspeed (S) - Increases the samurai’s movement speed.
  • Reload (R) - BFG reloads faster.
  • Speed (P) - BFG projectiles move faster.
  • Bombardment (B) - Rains down bombs on the horde, obliterating them en masse.
  • Lightning (Z) - Lightning strikes the horde from the heavens.
  • RailGun (G) - Annihilates all mongols within the its beam.
  • Points (Y) - Awards 5,000 bonus points.

Controls

Move the samurai up with “UPARROW” or “Q” keys, and down with “DOWNARROW” or “A” keys. These controls can be changed in the options menu.

Collect powerup cubes by clicking on them with the primary mouse button. This control is NOT configurable.

Notes

When your neighbors are Mongols has been tested working in FireFox and Chrome on Windows 10, and is best played in full-screen mode.

comments.author: Oliver Smith
comments.text: I like the art style and characters but found it a little difficult to follow the mouse cursor with much action on the screen to get the powerups. Great production value and entertaining to play though.

comments.author: Christopher Lis
comments.text: Really nice job being able to fit all that art within 13kb (along with all the power up functionality too). Game looked and played great, awesome job.

comments.author: Thiago Romão Barcala
comments.text:
Audio, graphics and effects are very cool!

I couldn’t multi-task enough to control my samurai while picking up the power ups, so I was usually focusing on one of the two. When there were a lot of enemies on the screen I looked at the samurai to see where it was, and where it should be, then I couldn’t find my cursor anymore. Maybe it would be a cool improvement to have a custom cursor for the game.

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text:
The graphic style is wonderful, and I especially like all the effects.

For me, the gameplay just didn’t quite work. I couldn’t look at where my guy was on screen while also focusing enough on powerups to place my mouse cursor over them. Maybe if your samurai just followed the mouse cursor vertically instead of Q/A? That way i could move the mouse where I wanted to aim, and also click powerups along the way? I also would have appreciated icons on the powerups instead of just letters, as in the chaos of it I really had no idea what each thing was, and it was too busy to think about what letter meant what thing.

Still I think it’s great artistically, and a polished presentation. Some small gameplay tweaks could make it a really nice little game.

comments.author: Federico Jacobi
comments.text:
This game is awesome! I think the fire rate, collision boxes, and initial speed needs to be balanced a bit. It is super hard in the beginning, and the waves get difficult very fast.

I really enjoyed it tho, even though I couldn’t get past the first wave

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Thanks. There is actually only one wave. I’d agree at a certain point the difficulty ramps up a lot but that’s kind of how I wanted it, for the player to suddenly feel overwhelmed.

comments.author: Alexey Kalmakov
comments.text:
Very nice graphics. You made outstanding visuals with very low budget.

The difficulty curve is a little steep. So the game ended for me too early.

PS My high score is: 53700

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text:
This game is hard!

I loved the weird multitasking of clicking and moving the canon. It feels super chaotic xD

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text:
The animations, sound effects and graphics are really well made. I also love the splatter and juice this game has. I wish the music had some more variation, but it’s impressive to fit this much into 13kb.

Instead of having the power ups on the board, and clicking them like crazy, I think the game would benefit from having the power ups available on key “1,2,3,4”, and use a cooldown system after you use a power up. Maybe it can help you think more tactical with your decisions.

Good job.

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Thankyou. Having the powerups on a cooldown is a pretty cool idea :)

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
Nice presentation - congratulations!

Maybe it is possible to use bonuses not only by clicking the mouse, but also by using the keys - it would add to the gameplay.

comments.author: FoumartGames
comments.text:
The graphics and sounds are top notch. I did found a winning weapon combo at some point and started to clear the enemies easily, but I kept loosing my mouse position in the chaos. It was really fun! Score: 175400.

The game thumbnail is wrong though - in 13th century the Mongols followed Tengrism and Buddhism and for the Cyrillic script - it’s a quite recent adoption.

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text:
Thanks for that correction, I didn’t actually do any research for the thumbnail. I just translated from English to Mongol. In future I’ll try to be more scientific ;)

I get the mouse thing, a few people have commented on that. I think if I ever do anything else with this game I’ll use a cool-down hotkey combo system as suggested by John Edvard.

comments.author: Victor Debone
comments.text:
Hello! Thanks for your submission to js13k! I’m sure you’ve worked hard on your game, but I’m not aware of how much time you spent on it, so excuse me if things I mention were in your mind, but got left out in the rush of the month or just because you hit the 13kb limit :)

  • My first impression and understanding (before playing the game, only reading the description, screenshot and title):
    • This look like some sort of “Metal slug” side scroller from the screenshot?
  • Great work on:
    • Having a complete working game!
    • Putting together a core loop. I need to evade and click on power-ups.
    • Graphics and theme, it’s all consistent and the style of game matches the type of graphics.
    • The menus and key mapping. It’s a must for every game, but a surprise from a 13kb game :)
  • I liked that you:
    • Made the various power-ups and had somewhat a balance between the amount of enemies and the stuff I could throw at them. The laser was definitely a surprise for me :D
  • You could improve (again, I’m aware you might already know all the below):
    • While the game works, I think it has a bit too much? My attention was split between targeting the power-ups blocks correctly and trying to avoid the dozen of the enemies projectiles. I didn’t care so much about the shooting and actually hitting any enemy, the points and specials from the power-ups were much better than anything my character was able to shoot.
  • I took some screenshots here https://twitter.com/javascripl/status/1709338781050892580

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text:
Many thanks for your feedback on my wee game Victor, it’s much appreciated.

I have a few ideas (mostly from other comments) on how to improved this game and I will hopefully have time in the next couple of weeks whilst I recover from an upcoming leg operation :)

comments.author: Richard Davey
comments.text:
Oh, the poor samurai! Move him up and down (he shoots automatically) and try to waste as many oncoming mongol warriors as possible. You’ll see lots of squares with letters on. Click them to unleash whatever power-up they contain. It plays out like an ultra-violent version of the arcade classic Tapper.

Graphically, this game is superb! The animation, the design, the font - it all just works and looks great. My only issue here is that I struggled to remember which powerup did what, although I appreciate that having icons instead of letters would have just added to the already crammed file size.

The gameplay is fun, in a ‘patting your head while rubbing your tummy’ kind of way. It’s very easy to lose your mouse cursor in the mongol chaos that ensues, all while trying to remember to move up and down as well. It starts off calmly enough, but the ramp from ‘I can manage this’ to ‘there’s no room left on my screen for any more mongols!’ can happen in the blink of an eye.

That aside, this is one quality entry and well worth playing.

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text:
Thanks for your feedback Richard. I did a quick search for Tapper and maybe it’s the one where you serve beer? It does look terribly hectic like my game.

I think that I probably could have squeezed more graphics into my game, but I got lazy with the power-ups I think and used text, because it was easier ;)

I get the “mouse cursor got lost” a bit in multiple peoples feedback so that’s an issue I’ll resolve when I get round to it :)

api.name: Portolani
api.description:
The 13th-century was a time of nautical innovation. The caravel was in development and early charts were being made. It’s your turn to captain an expedition to map uncharted waters and become a legendary explorer!

Portolani is a turn-based puzzle game. Your goal is to visit all of the ports, exploring as much of the map as possible. A new map is released every day at 00:00 UTC.

An optional leaderboard for the daily challenge is available via the NEAR testnet. If you would like to save your high score, please sign in to your testnet MyNearWallet (https://testnet.mynearwallet.com)!

Portolani is available on IPFS at:

  • https://aged-brook-5336.on.fleek.co
  • https://cloudflare-ipfs.com/ipfs/Qmc4yrTFr6tf8qnDBhPCtYQUUcBBUBomxPkT73bxhrFte7

Post mortem: https://onebytegone.com/articles/js13k23-portolani-post-mortem/

Keyboard Controls

Arrow keys can be used for cardinal-direction (i.e. N/S/E/W) movement. For 8-point compass rose (i.e. NW/N/NE/W/E/SW/S/SE) movement:

  • QWERTY: q/w/e/a/d/z/x/c
  • AZERTY: a/z/e/q/d/w/x/c
  • Dvorak: ’/,/./a/e/;/q/j
  • Number Pad: 7/8/9/4/5/1/2/3

Hints

  1. It’s best to sail broad reach (45deg to wind heading) or beam reach (90deg to wind heading).
  2. Your visits to a port may grant a bonus:
  • ⊛ (Local Crew): Grants advanced knowledge about fish, pirates, and ports
  • ⇶ (Nav Log): Grants more insight into wind conditions
  • ≊ (Sounding Line): Gives better understanding about the location of land

Music

The soundtrack is based on “Mirie it is while sumer ilast”, a 13th century song about the desire for summer in the face of coming cold weather.

Decentralized

  • NEAR Contract Source: https://github.com/onebytegone/js13k23-portolani/tree/master/near-contract
  • NEAR Contract Deployment: https://explorer.testnet.near.org/accounts/js13k23-portolani.testnet

Automatically deployed via CI/CD to IPFS using Fleek

comments.author: Paul Bredenberg
comments.text: Such a fun casual game of the daily variety.!

comments.author: Kevin Muncie
comments.text: A delightful game I have enjoyed playing each day. Surprisingly complex mechanics encourage exploration and resource management. The audio is on theme and pleasant without being distracting during longer play sessions.

comments.author: Viljami Peltola
comments.text: Beautiful puzzle game execution. I love how effort was put to make it a full game and that I found my self wanting to play a few rounds. Didn’t always get exactly what I am doing but the casual feel of it was refreshing - even when I didn’t finish a level.

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text:
I wish I had reached thie game earlier.

The theme plays so well into the puzzle mechanic of this game. The art style is very simple (like flavourful info) and it works so well.

The puzzles look super fun. I’ve bookmarked this so I can do it every day :D

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Great entry, nice graphics and music, simple but challenging game play.

Really nice that you show the map after each game is over and show how far has been travelled and how much of the map was revealed. The Rivers and Streams were stressful, knowing its a long way back along some of them with little fish left.

Good job.

comments.author: FoumartGames
comments.text: This reminded me of Ultima IV. Liked the puzzle element - it works really well. Also the way shores can be seen from afar is cool. Would love scrolling animation when sailing and some feedback when approaching fish or battle - at first I thought they just disappear. Nice chill music. Really good job on the decentralized part!

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Well done:

  • The music is really lovely. Fits the game really well and sounds great
  • Really cool concept of using the wind direction / strength to determine the cost of movement
  • As much as I wished I had a general map of where land / the edge of the map is, I also understand that it’s the actual point of the game and so it accomplishes it’s goal of making you feel like you’re having to explore when things were unknown
  • Graphics are really nice as well

Feedback:

  • I don’t think moving your ship to an invalid direction (like into the coast or the edge of the map) should count as a movement in terms of cost. At first I tried to move into the first port and kept losing “days” but didn’t understand why
  • I’m still not sure the exact cost a movement will cost me. I generally understand moving with the direction of the arrows but I have no idea how to gauge which direction will be more cost effective

comments.author: Allen Schmidt
comments.text: Really cool concept! I couldn’t exactly figure out the most efficient way to sail, but it was still fun making my way around and exploring the map. Really liked the music and graphics as well. Greay job!

comments.author: Tim Sulmone
comments.text: The near integration is well done, i really enjoyed the map downloads of voyages made, also good use of IPFS and the daily generated voyages is a neat feature.

api.name: Super Castle Game
api.description:
This is a game about building a castle. Magnificent and sempiternal, the fortress of the mind will rise — and it begins with a single block.

Set in the 13th century, Super Castle Game immerses you in a captivating medieval fantasy world of some description.

How to play

  • Arrow keys or WASD (and R to restart the level)
  • Tap on the screen
  • Gamepad left analog stick (and B to restart the level)

The icons are: toggle audio, restart the level, and level select.

For best experience on Android, use the ‘Add to Home screen’ feature. Seriously, it’s amazing how much better the game runs if started from that shortcut.

Thank you for playing!

Decentralized

Super Castle Game allows you to make your own levels: see the Community Levels page

Levels are just links to an instance of the game, so they can be shared freely on any social network, git repository, by email, and so on. This is the real, useful decentralized functionality.

The game is hosted on IPFS: ipfs://bafybeie5x57m5gdh6r3bz7wnfeifo22wvcb2efas2l5hplvnwl5vz6myua

For convenience, have some HTTP gateway links:

  • https://bafybeie5x57m5gdh6r3bz7wnfeifo22wvcb2efas2l5hplvnwl5vz6myua.ipfs.w3s.link
  • https://cloudflare-ipfs.com/ipfs/bafybeie5x57m5gdh6r3bz7wnfeifo22wvcb2efas2l5hplvnwl5vz6myua
  • https://bafybeie5x57m5gdh6r3bz7wnfeifo22wvcb2efas2l5hplvnwl5vz6myua.ipfs.cf-ipfs.com
  • https://gateway.ipfs.io/ipfs/bafybeie5x57m5gdh6r3bz7wnfeifo22wvcb2efas2l5hplvnwl5vz6myua

comments.author: Michael M.
comments.text: That’s an interesting puzzle! Though I don’t really see the castle in my mind, or anything else with the theme.

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text:
Thanks! The big idea (which I failed to articulate) was that the gray blocks are the stones from which the castle is built, so you’re kind of shaping them in your workshop. The castle walls on the title screen use the same colors as the grey pieces in the levels, and that’s no coincidence, they’re meant to be the same blocks.

If I ever make the full release of this game, I’ll include an animation or something to show how after completing a level they go to their place in the building, — I considered this during the compo but had no time or willpower left :)

comments.author: Ethan Smith
comments.text: Took a moment to realize the game and menu used the same mechanic, but was a nice surprise!

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text: Thanks! I wish I had another couple days or so to make a proper hub world, it was on my list of things but got scrapped in the end. Glad you still liked it!

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text:
I love this game. The presentation is wonderful, the way the first few levels are designed to teach the players the mechanics are perfect.

The only faults are that maybe it doesn’t fit the theme, other than the name and the drawing of a castle, the game itself is just an unrelated puzzle game (but an amazing one). And the difficulty ramps up maybe a bit too quickly too?

But despite the minor issues, still one of my favorite games this year. I love it!

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Loved the game, plenty of scope for replayability, found some of the levels tricky particularly J, L, N, O and Q.

The game felt really polished, the music was good, the graphics were good and it was easy to control.

I liked all the little animations and cuts between levels. Very good

comments.author: Elliot Nelson
comments.text: Nice, subtle sound effects, beautiful music, simple but polished graphics, and some cool puzzle tricks! Great job.

comments.author: Viljami Peltola
comments.text: Very addictive game! I loved every detail: animated everything, smooth transitions between views, … I didn’t need to read to get started to play. Also, the levels got harder with just the right amount and introduced new mechanics before giving a change to apply on following levels. I admire the skill how to put together so balanced, well implemented full game.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Very well made, love the simple and effective graphics, sounds and game play, got stuck on a level and just could not beat it!

Love the game, will come back and try to complete it and make some levels, awesome!

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
Nice! The design is super clean, and the gameplay is great. Feels very natural moving the cubes around the board, never clunky.

I think recognize the music from somewhere ;)

comments.author: Jani Nykänen
comments.text:
One of my favorite games this year, a very unique (or at least I haven’t seen this mechanic done before) puzzle game with a simple yet still very beautiful art style and a great music track.

My only bigger complaint is the lack of an “undo” button, which seems to be pretty standard in modern puzzle games. Restarting the whole stage after one mistake always feels a bit frustrating. Maybe this is something that could be implemented to the post-compo version, if there’ll ever be one?

comments.author: Armaan Mohammed
comments.text: Fantastic game. Theme is not clear as I dont see myself building a castle while doing the puzzles. But 3d games in 13kb is amazing and one with such graphics, reflections and gameplay is truly immpressive!

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
What a nice and clean presentation! I like the music, the soundeffects, the overall style (the reflection makes it really cool!)

Besides of the… uhm… very interpretative theme-connection, thats a very good game, which is almost ready to be a casual standalone-game!

And I’ve played it way to long now. It’s entertaining! Great job!

comments.author: Ben Fox
comments.text: really well polished, knew whose game it was as soon as I heard the music lol. Only thing I’d personally like more for this style of game is a display telling me which puzzle I was on out of the total, it would help me push through when thing started getting harder.

comments.author: Mirai_Dynamik
comments.text: lovely touhou bgm personally + 1⭐

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text:
Super polished! I liked a lot the ability of cutting blocks to move them around. Some levels are really hard, but that’s part of the genre :)

The presentation was perfect (graphics, music, animations, etc). I wish this game had anything to do with the theme other than its name. Despite this, great game!

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text: Impressive puzzle game and super polished! The animations and transitions are very smooth. I tried my best, but I can’t complete all the levels. XD

comments.author: Luis Gurmendez
comments.text:
This game is awesome, the graphics are really nice, I love the reflecting effect of the blocks and the in-out castle animation. This puzzle game really makes you think, some levels seemed impossible at first, specially the one I got stuck on. Something to improve would be to add the # of level I’m on and the # of total levels but is a minor thing

Really nice game great job!!! :raised-hands:

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text:
Great game Good and complicated puzzle with simple rules, good difficulty increasing, simple representation. Don’t see any problems except complexity (can’t beat several levels :’(

Nice work!

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: This game is amazing: music, sounds, graphics effects,… everything is very well done! The puzzles are really interesting and the difficulty increases progressively which makes the game even more addicitive.

comments.author: Marcin Walczak
comments.text:
Great, very well polished game. It makes you feel the need for just one more level.

Wonderful presentation and amazing music.

Small details like smooth animation, transitions between levels and even reflections make me smile.

Level editor and community created levels definitely gives extra replayability.

comments.author: Jeremy Burns
comments.text: This is a perfectly executed unique Sokoban game! The music is great, the transitions are fantastic, the difficulty curve is well-balanced. I’d love to see this expanded into a longer game, I’d buy it in a heartbeat! The one thing I’d suggest that seems to be standard in these types of games these days is an undo button. I keep hitting Z and getting disappointed when I have to restart and try to remember how to get to where I was after making a minor misstep. Despite that minor thing, this is definitely one of my favorite games this year! (The support for community levels is the icing on the cake, well-done with that detail!)

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text: Thanks a lot for the kind words! And yup the undo is sorely missing, I can’t believe I overlooked this feature entirely during both development and playtesting. This will be the first thing I change in the post-compo version.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: cool puzzle game, nice graphics, well done

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
This game is extremely polished and you packed so many puzzles in here! Great job. The music is amazing and fitting the theme, and the graphics beautiful and smooth.

I don’t get how this is a “castle building game”. I guess maybe in a very abstract way?

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text:
Thanks! :)

About the castle, the big idea (which I failed to articulate) was that the gray blocks are the stones from which the castle is built, so you’re kind of shaping them in your workshop. The castle walls on the title screen use the same colors as the grey pieces in the levels, and that’s no coincidence, they’re meant to be the same blocks. I agree it’s super vague though.

comments.author: Tim Sulmone
comments.text: I liked how it progressively got harder, felt like the beginning was a tutorial for mechanics involved in the game. Level editor was quite innovative.

api.name: B.
api.description: Use your cursor. This is a minimal name, let’s see if you can figure out how to finish it. No text or instructions are provided ingame. That’s deliberate.

comments.author: Elliot Nelson
comments.text:
Intriguing idea - sort of a WarioWare style game with lots of different minigames strung together. Was constantly curious what the next game was going to be.

This game is hurt a bit by lack of theme and no soundtrack/sound effects, but still a very cool concept.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Interesting to have all the minigames in this game, and not be told what to do at each phase.

Very simple games, but easy to get the hang of.

Uncluttered graphics are good too.

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text:
This is everything I love about games: it’s abstract, it’s insane, and that ending is fire!

And the git repo with random cat pictures is such a win. You, sir, are a legend.

comments.author: Emre Guneyler
comments.text: I love the way that I don’t have to think, super duper, snake was too long.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Simple, games we have all played 1000s of times but cleverly changing over time.

Nice entry

comments.author: Christian Staudte
comments.text: I am not so sure about the theme, but the concept is quite nice. Seeing how a game merges into different games.

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text:
I like the “figure it out” aspect of the game. Everytime you beat a level it’s another 2 seconds of “Uhm… what now?”

I like the simple artstyle.

Couple of negatives:

  • The snake game didn’t play so well with a mouse.
  • No audio

comments.author: Grav
comments.text: One of my favorites, though as others have mentioned, the way voting works will hurt it due lack of audio/theme. But very refreshing, fun and creative. doin’ its own thing

comments.author: Clément Pasteau
comments.text:
hey there!

I really love these kind of games, where you just have to figure out what to do and it’s not overcrowded with instructions or things to click.

My main feedback would be the missing audio! This makes players leave so quickly, because you get bored :(

Also some games are quite too long? For instance, when you have the ball and absorbs all the other ones, once you understand what to do, it gets boring because you just swing your mouse around for a few minutes…

Have you got any feedback with friends or family before submitting it? I would suggest doing that quite regularly, you learn so much by seeing players with your game :)

Great submission though, really like the concept!

comments.author: Frahaan Hussain
comments.text: When the game started, I thought this is too minimal but as it begun it was very fun. Simple but awesome and it was also a nice throwback to classics.

comments.author: Chris Subagio
comments.text:
This was pretty neat. I like the an artsy stab at things every now and again. Missing audio is a bit of a drag, but I just played some Imogen Heap to add vibes.

I think snake overstayed its welcome, but the sudden transition to breakout was delightful, so all is forgiven.

api.name: MediWars
api.description:
Generative game, every match is unique.

Controls

  • Click/drag: move your troops and attack
  • Drag: span camera

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
Hu? The enemies are running around like squirrels on caffeine! (I have a pretty descent gaming-pc, i guess you are not doing a time-based update, so on faster computers it just rus faster?)

I like the “infinite” landscape, and dragging around (a lot!) made the enemies slower :-) Also the graphics are fine. And that my troops are avoiding water and mountains.

Is there any way to end the game successfully? Or can i only run around until my king is dead? Didn’t find that out…

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text:
Not quite sure how to expand my army. Feels like it only get smaller as you kill enemies?

There’s a bit too much going on too without a clear goal.

I like the art style and I appreciate the infinite landscape! :D

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Nice background and character art, all works well.

Game play feels between turn based and real time, could be a little smoother.

Think I knew what I was doing but not sure there are tactics?

Over all well done

comments.author: Marcin Walczak
comments.text: I like the procedural terrain generation, but it’s progressively slowing down game.

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text:
The dudes are RUNNING!

There certainly is something to it. Needs a way to recruit people to your side, so that your entire group doesn’t get deleted in 2 unlucky encounters.

comments.author: Clément Pasteau
comments.text:
Hey there!

Really cool idea, it’s quite impressive on a big screen and makes you wanna navigate and visit everything.

Few things missing in my opinion:

  • music (so important!)
  • a goal? I wasn’t sure what to do apart from going around
  • I felt that dragging the screen also made the character move, so was scared of navigating

Super cool design overall, well done!

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
I like the idea of this game.

The generative aspect of the game worked pretty well.

The first time I played, nothing seemed to move or attack when I clicked, but the world map could be dragged around.

Reminded me of a command and conquer style game but with knights in battle.

comments.author: Keith Karnage
comments.text: This is an interesting concept. I like the look and feel of it, but I’m not sure what I was supposed to do. If I wander around I don’t find anything but individual enemies. If I fight them, my king always dies before very long. Feels like it needs a bit more.

comments.author: Stiggstogg
comments.text: From a technical point of view, this is very cool! The game also looks great. Regarding gameplay I was not so sure how I can influence the battle or what the goal of the game was. Would be good to add more explanation in case there was a goal besides to avoid that the king dies. I think there might be some things to improve on the gameplay side, but it is a really good base for a cool game! Congrats.

comments.author: Federico Jacobi
comments.text:
From a game perspective it needs work, and yeah, it is missing sound and all that. Others have mentioned the flaws.

From the technical aspect though, I think this is really cool and kudos to you! you implemented pathfinding, computer generated terrain, perlin noise, custom event listeners, etc. The implementation of the graphics is rather interesting: no bitmaps, just array data and color indexes and they actually look really nice.

This was a lot of work! I would love to see more development on this.

comments.author: Jedidiah Weller
comments.text:
Hi there Christian,

Thanks for submitting and good job on getting a submission in! Here’s my feedback for your submission:

Make sure to include instructions in your gameplay submission and the UI/UX itself. I understood the drag and click piece to move the units; but I did not understand at all the goal of the game. I found myself clicking to avoid the enemies but to what end? The procedural element of this submission was probably the best piece, since it worked well and seemed to be different each match Your readme on your source-code should be documented well for every open source project so other can follow along. Many developers (myself included!) will not look at a project until it’s properly documented 🙂 I would suggest using typescript and including eslint and stylelint for better code standardization

Feel free to hit me up on Twitter/X @jedihacks to have a discussion!

api.name: MongoRun
api.description: In this game, you will be controlling a Mongolian messenger who started an endless journey across the empire. You can switch between lanes to avoid obstacles using up and down arrow keys. To shoot birds in the sky, you can press right arrow key. You can also jump to avoid ground obstacles by pressing spacebar. Your goal is to run as long as possible before losing all Gereges.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Fun runner game, all worked well. Sounds and graphics are ok.

If you want to improve they entry I would suggest adding goals or levels, run 50 metres, get a reward. Run 100 metres and so on.

These games can be fun with some simple additions.

comments.author: Christian Staudte
comments.text:
Reached 4200 points :D

The addition of day and night is great and the graphics of the messenger is lovely, too. Sometimes I just struggled with the collisions (sometimes I did hit the birds while jumping and sometimes not?)

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: Good game, very well done! I love the horse animation and the switch day / night. The music is simple but effective. Maybe I can suggest you to add a simple progressive tutorial to help the player understand all the controls (up and down are obvious, but if you don’t read the instructions, space and right array are not so obvious).

comments.author: Matt Hackett
comments.text:
Very playable classic infinite runner game. Gave me Pitfall and Excitebike vibes. My main feedback is that the hit boxes weren’t clear, nor was getting hit.

Good looking game with a classic Atari aesthetic! Nice work.

comments.author: Antti Haavikko
comments.text:
5986!

Nice very retro vibes.

It’s near impossible to try to judge hitboxes and especially the lanes the birds are on. Actually it’s quite a common problem to make flying things position judgeable on an isometric(ish) view. Adding a shadow for them (on the correct) lane would help a lot.

The collision hitbox should be way smaller. It’s just too strict, unfair even. Quite often I had no clue what I hit even.

The best strategy really seems to be to just stay on the top lane. Obviously just jump the hurdles and just focus on the top most/smalles birds and shoot them when possible. But after around 3000 points it gets too fast and the arrow doesn’t have enough time to fly and hit a bird at all so it becomes all about jumping the fences. And seems like the hitbox somehow gets less strict at faster speed. But yeah then it just comes down to testing your reaction speed and hoping for a unwinnable fence+bird combo.

Good job! 👍

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
I really like the graphics, they are very NES-like, but I wish you went all-in with the NES-vibe with fonts and animations. The music is cool.

The gameplay is a little confusing at first. There’s so many birds, it’s hard to understand on which lane they are.

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Well done:

  • Liked the graphics, reminds me of Atari games
  • Being able to shoot birds out of your way was helpful

Feedback:

  • The collision for the birds was really confusing, and the hitboxes for the objects felt too big
  • Needing something more to keep me engaged. After awhile there’s not much else to do

comments.author: Frahaan Hussain
comments.text: It’s harder than it looks, felt like a classic NES game, could do with more variety and levels.

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text:
Well, nice runner. Usual set - several actions - change path, jump, fire and several types of barriers. Not super diverse, but good to play for some time.

Good Job!

comments.author: Magnus Lindh
comments.text: Funny and bizarre game. It raises a number of interesting questions: Are there seagulls in the desert? Was it common for mongolian messengers to do hurdle jumping? Did the monogols ever visit the pyramids in Giza?

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Enjoyed playing your game.

I like the avoid obstacles over multiple tracks kinda games, it was nice to have the extra elements of jumping and shooting.

The graphics were fun, I loved the seagulls.

api.name: Bewitched!
api.description:
The 12th Century was a time of happiness for witches of the world..

…until, an evil demon turned The Five Great Witches into cats and imprisoned them in cursed towers.

Can you, a lowly apprentice, rescue them?

Controls

  • Desktop: Hold left arrow to fly left and right arrow to fly right
  • Mobile: Hold left to fly left and hold right to fly right

How to play

Rescue the cat at the top of the tower and escape back through the entrance.

Notes

For mobile, works best on iOS and Firefox Android, too. I realised at the last moment that mobile Chrome had performance issues drawing circles to the canvas and had to tweak the mobile Chrome version. It is playable, though.

Hope you have as much fun playing as I did hacking this together. Enjoy!

comments.author: Jure Triglav
comments.text:
This is a very nice game! Simple controls, just left and right arrow keys, but in combination with the level and enemy designs, it provides an ample amount of gameplay depth, requiring quite a bit of strategy to overcome the well-designed challenges.

Update: There seems to be an issue with mobile controls. The game does suggest that it fully supports playing on a mobile device, but on an iPhone the left and right taps were often not registered, resulting in an unsatisfying death. This game would be perfect to play on the phone if this is fixed!

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text: Very nice minimalistic look, good controls and very clear objective.

comments.author: Emre Guneyler
comments.text: great, a game i want to play, except i cannot start at level 1 everytime i die.

comments.author: xem
comments.text: great but I die all the time because of weird lags

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Really enjoyed playing through the levels on this game.

The graphics are cute and work well.

The sound effects are good and the enemies challenging.

My son enjoyed playing this game too, trying to get to the top and back as efficiently as possible.

comments.author: Armaan Mohammed
comments.text: The game looks and sounds FANTASTIC! The animations are nice and look like a high budget game made by a studio! 😃. This Is the feedback: The pacing did not feel right. I kept getting stuck on level one and felt like this could be a level later on in the game (maybe 3/4). Pacing aside nice game with beautifull graphics!

comments.author: Jeremy Burns
comments.text: Great concept, and quite fun to play! On Chrome it hitches a lot, skipping many frames at a time and making it hard to play, but it was smooth in Firefox at least. The hair trail effect was a nice touch! It’s maybe a bit punishing to have to restart the game after a few mistakes, and the hit boxes seemed a little big, but otherwise it’s really snappy, fun to control, and obvious what to do. Awesome work.

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
G’day!

Oh, it was not easy, from the 20th time, not earlier…

Although the theme is somewhat generic, the gameplay is top notch! The complexity of the levels is very well chosen, so much so that it kept me in the “flow”. I also liked the graphics, especially how the young witcher blinking :D.

Restarting after death from the same level where you died is very lacking… It happens that you don’t understand new dangers the first time, and going through the previous ones all the time is tiring… I also noticed that although it is expected to press certain keys to start the game - any key starts the start. I also lacked control for the A-D (WASD).

It was fun to play, thanks!

comments.author: Grav
comments.text: Great game! The graphics are 🔥, and it had a lot of little touches, like the lightning on the title screen, the different hair color of the witches, the fireworks at the end. It has a really coherent art style, really polished in that sense. I also experienced the frame drops, but that might be due to my junk PC. My one feedback would be to replace the life system with a death counter. In the context of js13k your game has enough content to be satisfying without padding from the 3 life system. The enemy variety is crazy and the whole game kept me engaged. Really solid entry, well done.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Game play is very good and lots of fun, did not expect to enjoy this so much! Well made game, such simply controls but very challenging challenging.

Loved the cat and the quick escape needed once you saved them.

comments.author: Magnus Lindh
comments.text: Very smooth controls and impressive graphics, but restarting from the beginning is no fun. Still one of my favourite games in the jam. Good job!

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
This is awesome! The controls feel really good for being so simple. Animations (falling hair, munching skulls) look really nice, too. I also love the level flow– go up to save the cat, go down to escape the dragon.

Only thing on my wishlist is some sort of checkpoint system– either within a single level, or allowing players to choose which level to start from.

PS: This reminds me a bit of “Icy Tower.” I wonder if you’ve played that before.

comments.author: Chris Subagio
comments.text:
Adorable sprites, and a fun idea! Nice procedural hair too :)

Unfortunately this was one of those games where I made it to lv 4 on my first try I think, then for the life of me couldn’t make it out of lv 1 again after that. I just suck at reflex things!

For me, I don’t think the 3 lives thing was necessary, I would have just let folks try over and over again to beat the current level to that they could see everything you’ve made within the span of a gamejam play session!

comments.author: Jedidiah Weller
comments.text:
Hi Eoin,

Great work on this submission - you truly did a remarkable job! I’ll post my feedback below from my review:

  1. Equally as fun responsively on mobile and web, and as a mobile app expert I have a lot of appreciation for this. Bravo mi amigo!
  2. I enjoyed the mechanic of flying up only with a certain stamina - I’d suggest adding that in the instructions but it was easy enough to figure out
  3. I found that while falling it was easy to find ‘gaps’ in the enemies; so perhaps having a mechanic to drag them towards the player who’s trying to escape could be a nice bonus. The dragon creature though does a good job of adding urgency, so bravo here!
  4. One additional UI/UX suggestion would be to add a 1-2 second pause after the player picks up the cat. This way, you can add bit more cinematic flare before they start falling. You could also potentially have changes to the platforms themselves upon receiving the cat, but these are simply enhancement suggestions.
  5. For your source code, you might want to consider removing the zip file and adding it to git-ignore to make your package more clean. Additionally, converting to typescript for OOP and adding eslint and stylelint are always good ways to ensure consistency from community contributions.

Seriously, great work on this - one of my favorites! What’s next? Feel free to hit me up on Twitter/X @jedihacks to have a discussion!

comments.author: Victor Debone
comments.text:
Hello! Thanks for your submission to js13k! I’m sure you’ve worked hard on your game, but I’m not aware of how much time you spent on it, so excuse me if things I mention were in your mind, but got left out in the rush of the month or just because you hit the 13kb limit :)

  • My first impression and understanding (before playing the game, only reading the description, screenshot and title):
    • Is it a platformer?
  • Great work on:
    • Having a complete working game!
    • Adding sound effects!
  • I liked that you:
    • Created different mechanics directly with the enemies. The bats flying around killed me many times, but I needed to time them properly, while the spiders just got in my way.
  • I took some screenshots https://twitter.com/javascripl/status/1709711719700783226

api.name: Alquerque
api.description:
Experience the strategic depth of the 13th-century Alquerque board game in this digital adaptation. Test your tactical prowess in a medieval setting where knights, castles, and strategic maneuvers rule the day. Challenge friends in this classic game of capture and conquest.

Objective

Capture your opponent’s knights by strategically jumping over them to win the game. Choose your moves wisely, protect your knights, and outmaneuver your adversary to claim victory.

Features

  • Authentic Alquerque gameplay with a medieval twist.
  • Single-player mode.
  • Multiplayer mode for head-to-head battles.
  • Stunning medieval-themed visuals and sound effects.
  • Choose your side: Play as the Black or White knights.
  • Classic board game strategy with a 13th-century ambiance.

Controls

Mouse: Click to select and move your knight.

Objective: Jump over your opponent’s knights to capture them.

Win Condition: Capture all of your opponent’s knights or block their movement to secure victory.

Immerse yourself in the medieval world of Alquerque and prove your strategic prowess in this timeless board game challenge. Will you lead your knights to triumph or succumb to defeat?

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: The graphics sadly were not “stunning” and worse yet, the gameboard did not even render correctly. Nice game still :)

comments.author: Lakshman
comments.text: Thanks for playing!! I guess due to media queries the gameboard did not render properly.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: nice game bord is a good improve over the normal checkers

comments.author: Lakshman
comments.text: Thanks for playing!! Yes this game is a parent of checkers!

comments.author: Grav
comments.text: Nice work! I had the board rendering issue that others have mentioned, but it did not affect the gameplay. The sound effects added a lot to the gamefeel and the AI was good. Great game.

comments.author: Lakshman
comments.text: Thanks for playing!! I guess the board rendering issue was because of the media queries. I have used ZzFX for the sound effects!!

comments.author: Ben Fox
comments.text: Like the visual style, gameplay was easy and interesting. Definitely messed up and got triple jumped but still managed to win lol

comments.author: Lakshman
comments.text: Thanks for playing!! Glad to hear that, you lead your knight to Triumph!!!

comments.author: Frahaan Hussain
comments.text:
Very nice menu, great description which was in-depth, something many devs omit.

A tutorial would have been nice.

comments.author: Lakshman
comments.text: Thank you for playing!! I’m glad you found menu and description helpful. You’re absolutely right, a tutorial would have been a great addition, as I delved deeper into building the game logic and optimizing it, I lost track of that idea.

comments.author: Nathan Long
comments.text: I was a bit disappointed that this wasn’t a new game but an adaptation. I had a very hard time reading the rules in the Blackletter font and some of the moves seemed like I should have had more options than I did to prevent captures. The piece icons and board were lovely though.

comments.author: Lakshman
comments.text:
Thanks for playing! My main motive was to make people know that this game exists and was famous in 13th century, so I recreated this.

I choose that specific font for relating it to theme! And all the things present in the game are related to theme!!

The game rule is to compulsorily take the kill; when kill is possible. The same thing I was arguing it with my sister 😅. Otherwise the game never ends.

comments.author: Chris Subagio
comments.text:
Nice implementation of Alquerque! It looks nice and sharp, and plays as you’d expect. Would have been nice to see a little more animation of the pieces moving around, and the audio was a bit harsh, but otherwise a pleasant experience.

AI seemed to throw the game at the end though, not sure if that was on purpose as we’d hit a stalemate, but if it was, good job on not letting the game die in stalemate!

comments.author: Lakshman
comments.text: Thanks for playing! I really wanted to add animation, but I was running out of time. The audio I had selected was an explosion to match the theme, but it almost ended up sounding like noise.

comments.author: Justin Sullard
comments.text: It seemed to randomly interpret clicks at times, and I was not able to identify why. It just did a move that was not what I had chosen. Sometimes it seems like it was sort of a delayed rendering thing, where I was clicking on the piece to select it but it had decided it should already be selected, so then was interpreting my click as the position I wan’t go go to and selected the closest one perhaps?

comments.author: Lakshman
comments.text: During development and testing I never faced that randomly interpreted clicks, but when I found this issue the entries were closed and I couldn’t recreate the scenario.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
Fun game. I think played it before.

The game doesn’t render correctly in Firefox. Everything is slightly in the wrong place. The sound effects add nicely to the game, but the “victory” sound was very loud.

comments.author: Lakshman
comments.text: Thanks for playing! So many have reported the rendering issue, I need to work more on media queries!

comments.author: Stiggstogg
comments.text: Nice idea to adapt a board game for this jam. The execution is also very good. I think I encountered some bugs, that when I was planning my next move the opponent suddenly played his move. Not so sure about that or if I just misclicked. The game is very solid and I really liked that you were able to incorporate both single and multi-player. Congratulations to this game!

comments.author: Lakshman
comments.text: Thanks for playing! Maybe the automatically the pieces are moving because of setTimeout method that’s being called after sometime. But I couldn’t recreate the scenario.

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: Well, great chess like game. Simpler in some way, but harder in another. It looks like sometimes I had some control issues, like self-moving figures after you click on it (and it moves not in your planned direction) But anyway, interesting game, good Job!

comments.author: Lakshman
comments.text: Thanks for playing! Looks like there is some lag in setTimeout due to which the pieces are moving without user click. Have to check the logic once more.

api.name: Princess of Corinium
api.description:
You play as a princess who needs to cure the plagued villagers from the rats of Corinium.

Click or press where you want to walk to and the princess will find the best route there.

Watch out for enemies which will chase you when you get too close and will hurt you. There are also shy characters who will run away if you get too close.

The rats will hunt out villagers to infect them. If a villager notices a rat nearby they will flee somewhere else, and only come back if it’s safe to do so.

But if a villager and rat cross paths the rat will infect the villager and once below 80% health they will start bleeding a red, green or blue coloured blood.

To heal them, you need to be holding a potion which is coloured the same as the blood coming from the villager. Or a white potion will cure any villager.

Use keys to enter some buildings, make good use of the Wizard’s shop.

To succeed, you need to collect all coins, potions and contents of treasure chests and make sure the villagers are not all infected.

Controls are touchscreen where available or use mouse on desktop.

comments.author: Ben Fox
comments.text:
Fun little world to explore! It took a bit for me to understand exactly what was happening, but after that it wasn’t too hard to keep the villagers healthy. Nice visual effects, like the opening chests & sickness.

I really wanted the signs to have messages when walking over them.

I’m not sure if there was a “win” screen? I played for a while, and pretty sure I collected everything, so I can’t tell if at some point, the whole game froze randomly or if I collected the last item and the freezing was that I had gotten everything and won.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text: Thanks for the feedback. In the game I did implement message popups from the sign posts, and the game completion screen, but this was lost by the minification I used and unfortunately I ran out of time to test before submitting. You can play the non-minified game using the website link on the js13kgames entry page. Thanks

comments.author: Aleksey Litvinov
comments.text:
Looks like an RPG-action game. Had to switch to the description few times to understand what’s going on and to learn how to move. Managed to collect 8 keys and 41 coins, however, was not sure what’s next. And was hoping to pick up a weapon to get rid of the rat :D

Overall, I was surprised with the world available that you can explore — even enter buildings and different floors. However, would be nice to add some in-game messages to help with the start.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Thanks for the feedback. In the game I did implement message popups from the sign posts, including a short how to play, but this was lost by the minification I used. I did run out of time to implement the weapons and fights.

You can play the non-minified game using the website link on the js13kgames entry page. Thanks

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Nice simple controls with cute bright graphics, loved the look of the map and characters! The path finding worked well as long as you dont click twice in the same spot (I like to keep clicking).

Great that you can explore and enter buildings.

I collected quite a lot but not sure if there is a win screen?

Good job.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Thanks for the feedback. In the game I did implement message popups from the sign posts, and win completion screen, but this was lost by the minification I used and unfortunately I ran out of time to test before submitting to realise that this had happened.

Yes, the clicking to move was meant to be click once and wait for the character to get there, but others have said that multiple clicks don’t work as expected. Clicking on a square that you can’t get onto won’t make the character move towards it. Perhaps moving to the nearest available space would be better.

You can play the non-minified game using the website link on the js13kgames entry page. Thanks

comments.author: Thiago Romão Barcala
comments.text:
I liked the graphics a lot. The sprites, the little particle effects, the camera movement. Well done!

I didn’t get what the enemies do. The description say they will hurt me, but I didn’t see any health bar, and I just ignored them the whole time and nothing happened. I also just ignored all the villagers, and just accidentally cured them if they crossed my way.

The mouse control didn’t feel very natural to me, cause when I clicked the first time, the camera would start to move, then it was hard to click at some other point if I changed my mind.

After I collected all the items the game just froze, and I had to kill the browser.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Thank you for leaving feedback.

So the message popups I added didn’t work after I minified the code to submit, but they told you the general aim of the game, that doors needed keys and when you completed the game.

As for the NPCs, some of them are enemies who attack and chase you when you get too close, and some of them are scared of you and will run away when you approach.

You do get a health bar when hurt by the enemies, but once this has expired you don’t actually die. I was going to use the weapons to attack the enemies but ran out of time.

With the villagers, you need to take note of the colour of the “blood” they are spurting when they loose more than 80% health. Then if you are holding a potion of the same colour as their blood or a white “cure all” potion, then you can heal them.

Instead of freezing at the end, you should have seen a message saying you’d completed it, but as I say, this was lost during minification.

Thanks for playing

comments.author: Richard Davey
comments.text:
This felt like playing a Gameboy RPG. The graphics are lovely, and it has really nice little touches like the particle effects, world map, the ability to enter buildings, the Wizards Shop, etc. Controls are simple. The Princess moves accurately and swiftly where you click, and the camera is smooth.

The only downside was I wasn’t entirely sure what to do. The enemies didn’t seem to hurt me. My health bar vanished when a spider sat on me, but once it ran out, I could carry on just fine. This is probably just as well because despite there being swords and bows lying around, you couldn’t seem to pick them up anyway.

Curing the villagers was pretty easy - just run through them or wait for them to walk through you :) I think there is the kernel of a really nice game here, and with a few more gameplay elements implemented, it would be something special.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Thanks for the feedback.

The sign posts dotted around the level provided the player with hints and aims of the game, but somehow this was lost by the minification process, and I hadn’t tested the minified code enough to notice this unfortunately.

The health bar on your character does reduce when hurt by enemies, but you’re right nothing then happens when you run out of health. This was because I ran out of time. I’d intended to make the player move slower. Also as noted the weapons and attacks were not implemented.

The infected villagers spewing coloured blood required you to have a potion of the same colour to be able to heal them, if they die the become ghosts and haunt the graveyard at top right.

Thanks once again

comments.author: Nathan Long
comments.text: Adorable graphics, but deeply confusing gameplay.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text: Thanks, the signposts functionality which would have helped gameplay got broken by the minifier, so yes not much help.

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: Well, for now it looks like a RPG prototype, with simple and cute visual and several mechanics (collecting keys, potions and cois, loot chests, cure and take a damage from enemies) It’s not the funnies game in the world, for be it’s too chaotic, but boring But still, good job!

comments.author: Chris Subagio
comments.text:
I see from your other replies that this is basically an unfinished game, which is a shame because what’s already there is great! The graphics are adorable, your pathfinding is clean, there are already items and interactions there, even buildings to enter with multiple floors!

As for the missing combat… you know, it’s kinda nice not to have combat. I like that you’re a healer. Maybe lean into that even more: no direct combat, but healthy villagers will come to your aid, so all you have to do is keep everyone healed (and maybe deliver some nice weapons to them?) and off you go.

Great effort, I hope you come back next time and knock it out of the park.

comments.author: Ania Kubów
comments.text:
What a splendid game to play!

I thoroughly enjoyed the visuals. The sprites were consistent and the colour palette soothing and complimentary. The movements of the sprite was also very smooth.

It took a while for me to understand what was going on, but I feel that could be an easy fix. If you have extra time it would be great to implement the game play instructions into the game, saving the player having to read the game description.

Can’t wait to see this developed further!!

comments.author: Lakshman
comments.text: Nice game to play. I enjoyed playing it!!

comments.author: Björn Ritzl
comments.text: Cute game. The pathfinding works well and overall gameplay is good.

api.name: The Treasure of the Nibelungs
api.description:
Spurred on by The Songs of the Nibelungs, many set off searching for the legendary treasure said to still lay hidden in the Rhine river.

Keyboard controls

  • WASD or arrows keys to move
  • Enter or Space to attack
  • E to pick up items
  • Shift to dodge roll
  • Esc to pause/unpause

Gamepad is also supported.

comments.author: Oliver Smith
comments.text: Really well presented fun game. At times the collision detection seemed a little off or maybe I just wasn’t very good with the control layout. Great graphics and style with a neat interactive tutorial too.

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text: Nice graphics and the characters are super cute! Easily to understand by the tutorial. Adding a few more elements to the game, such as defense, counterattacks, and critical hits, would make it more exciting.

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text: I liked that you could skip the fight on the pier. I didn’t like that you need to be smashing enter/space for attacks - it felt clumsy. Dodge roll didn’t work a couple times and I’m not sure why. Picking weapons from the enemies is cool!

comments.author: Emre Guneyler
comments.text: nice, except a little hard, put more less time to kill enemies so i want to play more. i mean i have a shield why my health goes faster than the enemies.

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text:
Great graphics and fun game! The controller support made all the difference here for me. The first time I tried playing on keyboard and it was a bit frustrating. With a controller though that all changed and I played through to the end!

Occasionally I got hit even though I was quite far away, which felt unfair, and I wish the smaller sword swung faster, it felt quite slow.

Despite those minor annoyances I thought it was a nice little beat-em-up and enjoyed it!

comments.author: CaptainAligna
comments.text:
I felt so challenged in the last part of the game, I could not stop until I had all the riches in the world!

I had a lot of fun, it was a creative take on this style of game! I loved that it had a little tutorial at the start and I really enjoyed the stealing/picking up the weapon before the enemy mechanic, it gave way to many funny moments in the game!

Note: I have a feeling that despite being far from the long swords at the end of the game, I still got hit. I had to master dodging.

Good job~

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
A fun game, great graphics and narrative.

The tutorial was good and very helpful. The levels had a nice difficulty progression.

It would be good to be able to pick up weapons without pressing E, as a couple of times in battle I found myself needing to move and re-arm.

I’ll have to play again on a gamepad.

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text: Gamepad support for the win! I love how you can disarm enemies and steal their weapon, such a nice touch. The huge sword has a hit radius of a SpaceX rocket, and that’s actually fun. The boss battle is cool. Great job!

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Really nice game, love the pixel art and fighting!

Nice feature to be able to collect weapons but also to lose them and to have mobs collect them too!

Between levels my weapon was downgraded each time?

The controls were good, sometimes a little hard to time the roll but worked ok.

Nice animations and a mix of scenary.

comments.author: Christian Staudte
comments.text: What a cool game! I only whished it would have more levels/sections. The combination of story and gameplay is really well executed! Thanks for this game :D

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
After finishing the game, I am waiting for the game selection screen: from the beginning or to continue, I also think that the kick can also be added to the left mouse button.

I thought that I would find something in the barrels if I broke them, but I didn’t find anything, maybe I was unlucky, or it hasn’t been implemented yet.

Good game - congratulations!

comments.author: Michael M.
comments.text: Unfortunately, I could not pick items up: I have “search as you type” enabled in my browser, and you don’t call .preventDefault() on key events, so when I try to pick something up, the page search opens for me.

comments.author: Clément Pasteau
comments.text:
Hey there!

That tutorial is right on point, really perfect to pick up the game mechanics and get going. The graphics are great, too bad there’s no little music to play along.

I really enjoyed the controls, they feel really natural. I do feel the start of the game is slightly too hard? You already have to fight multiple enemies barebones and the story had not started yet!

Really nice piece of work

comments.author: Chris Subagio
comments.text:
The sprites in this were gorgeous, and they animated very nicely!

The combat was a little rough though. It wasn’t clear how much vertical reach weapons had (I swear I got hit by a big sword guy when I was at the bottom of the screen and he was at the top!). Hit reactions were muted, so things felt a little mushy too, like you’re not sure when you connect?

I did claim the treasure in the end, but I can’t say it’s because I figured out the combat, I just kept spam rushing until I got lucky :)

comments.author: Ania Kubów
comments.text:
YES!!!

So much about this submission hits all the makings of a successful game.

I am always a fan of the gameplay instructions being worked into the game flow and in this submission that was done perfectly and seamlessly with the storyline.

On top of that the visuals worked very well. I am a big fan of retro looking games so it was a pleasure to see pixelated sprites with a soothing colour palette to match.

It is nice to see that you kept to the theme also.

Well done!

title: Run away from pied piper of Hamelin
author: Mik39sch

api.name: Run away from pied piper of Hamelin
api.description:
You should find brother (Pink). If you are found by pied piper of hamelin (Red)…

Controls

  • Right/Left key: Change Direction
  • Up Key: forward
  • Down Key: Back

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text: For some reason this didn’t start on my quest 2 :(

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text:
At first this game seemed utterly broken, big grey blurry text, nothing after that on Quest… After a while on desktop I figured out you were in a totally black maze, so all you could do is look at the map to see where you were. It felt more like I needed to find the Pied Piper because it did not move at all.

This game has some potential, use lighting so you can see where you are going, make the Pied Piper move, add a bit of sound (closer to the Pied Piper quicker and louder), etc. would nog have made the development cost much more time, but would have greatly improved the game.

comments.author: ch!k0
comments.text: The screen was too dark to navigate since the color of the bricks/wall was almost black. Though it was fun going around the map and finding the piper-instead of escaping him. I agree with mr van egmond’s comment on changing the tempo/volume of the sound when the piper is nearby to add a thrilling touch

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
The game didn’t work for me I’m afraid, the screen showed an info page, then just a grey screen. A box appeared when I clicked on the screen.

Error in console was :

Source map error: Error: request failed with status 404 Resource URL: https://js13kgames.com/webxr-src/2023/babylon.js Source Map URL: babylon.js.map

Also, submitting the game to the “desktop” category means you can’t use any external dependencies like Babylon.js

comments.author: FoumartGames
comments.text: The game works, but it really feels broken at first as it takes some time to generate the maze and also hangs for a while the first time it has to render certain elements - I see that people don’t realize that.. Playing involves mostly looking at the map, as the 3D visualization is confusing. Besides it being all black with some random sky here and there to give clues on the environment, there are a few glitches that can hinder your orientation. For example there is no difference when you face a wall and there is one step ahead, or when you already have reached it. Walking through a corridor also does not give feedback (this can be fixed by just changing the ground tile - the same way as when you rotate around) There is also a bug when the RND has placed two corridors with just one tile of wall in between - the other corridor is visible as if looking through a glass wall. I liked that the back button actually moved me backwards, instead of rotating 180 degrees. Also liked that the enemies were somewhat clueless but not that much - some of them were stationary but others had movement patterns and it was not so easy to avoid them. They even walked through corridors and it can give you some intense moments there haha. I found the pink brother but it looked the same as the enemies - it was the only one that did not moved though, so maybe it wasn’t just luck. Anyways I had fun exploring this game!

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text:
The games does work, it just takes a bit to load.

I felt a little disoriented in the game, but I expect that is the intention.

I didn’t manage to find my brother as I couldn’t find a way to a couple of rooms (I must’ve not looked too carefully).

This game would benefit imensely from spacial sound so that we’re aware of where the Pied Piper is and I think having this would’ve pushed my innovation score a lot higher.

Regarding the blurry text at the start, you should change the canvas rendering mode like so:

ctx.imageSmoothingEnabled = false;

Where ctx is your canvas context

That should make the text a bit more legible. Alternatively, define the right canvas dimentions to match the player screen’s and increase the font size.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: Interesting game. Wish the movements were a bit smoother on desktop. I basically found myself looking mostly at the minimap. The first person I found was the brother, but I thought that was Red, not Pink. The red fellas don’t move of come after me so they were easily avoided. The brother was hard to catch and was passing through me. The connection to the theme is a little forced.

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
Nice mouse simulator!

Fix the graphics a little so that there are differences in the texture of tunnels and walls, and I also think it would be better to add a mouse skin for my brother, instead of a person (ratcatchers), if I understood the game correctly - it would make it more understandable.

Also, at the beginning, the game takes a long time to load, for a start, I suggest adding any “loader”, that is, a progress bar, which would let the user understand that everything is in order and the game is loading. Well, then try to optimize the download process itself if possible :).

Sounds will also add interest to the game!

In general, I described what I think is a good start from which you can create anything!

Thanks and congratulations!

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: Well, not a bad labyrythm game, but there are lot of problems. Levels are quite unrepresentative, it’s hard to anderstand anything from main view. Labyrythm generation sometimes generates isolated rooms (you can move in 2 rooms and rest aren’t available or I didn’t find the way) But good prototype, nice job!

comments.author: Sébastien Vandenberghe
comments.text:
I played on desktop as I was not able to run the game in VR.

The controls felt pretty strange and it took me a bit to figure what was going on. I think FPS controls and maybe more indication with spatial audio for the target/ennemy could help a bit (shamelessly stealing the idea from one of the other submissions).

It is not clear to me how we would “win” the game.

api.name: Minipuyo
api.description:
A super puyo puyo remake with a fancy medieval wall as a background.

Use arrows or WASD to move, OP to rotate.

comments.author: Allen Schmidt
comments.text: It’s definitely a well-executed super puyo puyo remake, but there is nothing added to it. I do like that you have included a bot to play against (even if it is just randomly making moves haha), and the graphics and sound work well. The control scheme is a little odd, at least coming from other games similar to this where pressing up can rotate (doesn’t do anything here). And I think there’s a bug after the game is over, as I had to refresh the page to get it to work again after that. Great job overall though!

comments.author: Gauthier Billot
comments.text: Thanks for the feedback! Indeed I could have used better controls… Yes the end-game empty screen is a bug that appeared minutes before submission, shame!

comments.author: FoumartGames
comments.text: If those color pieces were parts of Medieval equipment (swords, shields, helmets, etc.) that you had to combine into the correct shape, in order to arm a soldier for example, that would’ve been a different story. Now it does not have any connection with the theme. But it is nicely executed, good job!

comments.author: Gauthier Billot
comments.text:
Thanks but what about my stoned wall? Isn’t looking medieval? ;)

Your gameplay suggestion could be nice indeed but for a different game!

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text: It’s nice, and I like the creepy eyes everywhere.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Really well made and actually fun to play.

Improvements:

  • Add scores
  • Better CPU
  • Allow larger play area

comments.author: Gauthier Billot
comments.text:
Thanks for the suggestions, you are right about the scoring and cpu.

But for the play area I used the original one!

comments.author: Chris Subagio
comments.text: Perfectly fine Puyo clone! There was something a little off to the feel though, like there was a pause after drop before lines formed that didn’t feel right, and a few other small things that felt like unfamiliar friction, but otherwise, that’s Puyo alright.

comments.author: Gauthier Billot
comments.text: Thanks! And you are right the drop timing is not matching the original one…

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
I’ve not played games in this genre before so wasn’t sure if the right hand side by itself was a novel feature. Still the game was fun to play.

Graphics are fun, I like the little eyes.

It played well and has good sound effects.

The game was a bit small on the screen for me.

comments.author: Christer Kaitila
comments.text: an exellent version of a classic game. I really liked how the little pixel eyes looked around, making everything feel alive. stylish and well implemented!

comments.author: Gauthier Billot
comments.text: Thank you! Glad to see slightly moving pixels bringing life ;)

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text:
I never played the original but I quite enjoyed your version. Even without knowing how the game is meant to work, you can figure it out within the first 4 or 5 pieces.

I felt the AI wasn’t very challenging (seems to be making random moves?)

The graphics are really cute but I didn’t feel like it was very related to the theme.

comments.author: Gauthier Billot
comments.text: Thank you! AI is indeed a very simple random ;)

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: Well, some sort of dr. Mario (didn’t play puyo) Nice tetris like game, simple, but interesting. Enemy Ai are good enough. Good job!

comments.author: Victor Debone
comments.text:
Hello! Thanks for your submission to js13k! I’m sure you’ve worked hard on your game, but I’m not aware of how much time you spent on it, so excuse me if things I mention were in your mind, but got left out in the rush of the month or just because you hit the 13kb limit :)

  • My first impression and understanding (before playing the game, only reading the description, screenshot and title):
    • I don’t know puyo puyo :( so my impression is that this is a tetris game?
    • Ok, after playing some rounds I got it, it’s a competitive tetris.
  • Great work on:
    • Having a complete working game!
    • The controls and pieces placement work really well. I didn’t have any bugs or issues during my gameplay.
  • I liked that you:
    • Gave some character to the blobs by adding the eyes.
    • It has some nice graphics and fit really well with what you had going.
  • You could improve (again, I’m aware you might already know all the below):
    • I was not watching the enemy playing much, but I suspect – being fully aware how hard this is to get right – the strategies they were making were not paying off. They took some time to match the pieces and I could easily beat them after the second game.

comments.author: Gauthier Billot
comments.text: Thanks for you valuable feedback! The enemy bot was indeed the weak part : it was the last minute feature I’ve added.

api.name: Wanted
api.description:
Wanted is a charming pixel art adventure that invites players to step into the legendary shoes of the heroic outlaw Robin Hood. Sharpen your archery skills, and rob from the rich to give to the poor.

Avoid The King’s guard and get as many coins as you can.

Controls

  • Z - Shoot
  • X - Jump
  • < - Left
  • > - Right
  • Enter - Select
  • Esc - Go back

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: nice animations nice end scene however it get boring fast as no progression and nothing to do afterwards, could keep doging and hitting but what comes after that is wanted cool entry

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text:
I liked the overall presentation of the game. From a gameplay perspective, it’s too easy to just stay at the bottom of the level, wait for the “rich” to get money and continue there forever (the guards never became an issue).

What I would do is to allow rich NPC to spawn and walk only on the superior levels, to force you to be up there - once you steal from them, the guards chase you down until they lose you from sight (for example hiding in the lower level before going back up to steal more). This would make risk / reward more interesting.

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text:
Great game with tight controls, cute graphics/animations, and great music.

I only wish the game had some increasing difficulty.

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text: awesome pixel-graphics, and very good music/sfx some kind of progression is missing

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Well Done:

  • Smooth controls
  • The little pixel art people were really nice
  • I liked that the coins would disappear, forcing you to go collect them instead of just camping

Feedback:

  • Sometimes the non-killable units would act as a shield and make it impossible to kill the archers behind them, leading to being trapped and unable to kill the approaching enemy
  • Do wish there was a bit more to the game as it got repetitive after awhile

comments.author: Daniel Whiffing
comments.text: Wow! It looks great, it sounds great, and I had a ton of fun jumping around bad guys while trying to get as much gold out of the rich as I could! Would’ve been cool to see more mechanics, but this is stellar work!

comments.author: Marcin Walczak
comments.text:
I like the graphic and sound design. It reminds me of one NES game (can’t remember the name).

The controls are really smooth and responsive.

comments.author: Michael M.
comments.text: Most of the time I just kept at the very bottom, shooting left and right, which avoided the guards, while bringing in lots of money.

comments.author: Frederic Kyung-jin Rezeau
