The font you used doesn’t exist in my system, so all the text is overlapping and hard to read.

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: Good Job! Really nice work, interesting implementation of oregon trail with specific theme. It’s hard tu understand relative cost of goods at the start so it would be nice to add some default set of goods and travelers. Also adding some random event’s would be nice.

comments.author: Chris Subagio
comments.text:
This is a pretty neat interpretation of Oregon Trail! Had a hit of nostalgia firing it up:)

14795miles in 288 days, score of 542160. I think once I worked out that arrows are cheap, you can hunt as much as you like, and trade goods prices only go up, I worked out the optimal strategy was sprint for the next town to preserve camel food, then hunt like crazy in the last 100 miles, sell the food in town, stock up on the camel food (what do they eat, truck loads of caviar?!), and then dump the rest into goods. Scale up camels as you go to keep the load light. Other than random illnesses (I had dengue fever as a kid too!), it was smooth sailing.

I think it looked pretty neat, but like others I had font issues that meant I probably wasn’t seeing what you intended. The music was simple, but worked great. Also, hit the nostalgia spot :)

comments.author: Tim Sulmone
comments.text: Loved the gameplay, and played well with the theme. The login function wasn’t operating for NEAR but i noticed in its github repo the integration, so it may be an error on my side. The UI needs better scaling, had to zoom in 250% for the buttons to look normal (again could be my end, im using a 1440p resolution)

api.name: Super Polo Bros.
api.description:
Controls

  • Move: Left and right arrow keys
  • Jump: Space
  • Pick up tablet / throw tablet / flip switches: Ctrl
  • Switch brothers: Shift
  • Restart current level: R

Goal

Bring Niccolo and Maffeo and the golden tablet safely to the flag in each level.

Tips

  1. Watch out for Mongol bandits! If a bandit touches one of the brothers who is not holding the golden tablet, you will have to restart the current level.
  2. The tablet can depress pressure plates.
  3. If your character doesn’t have room to pick up the tablet, your character will kick it.
  4. Jump while throwing the tablet to throw it further.
  5. Hold jump for higher jumps, tap jump for lower jumps.

Description

In 1266 Marco Polo’s father and uncle, Niccolo and Maffeo Polo, reached the seat of Kublai Khan, the leader of the Mongol Yuan Dynasty, in Dadu (which is now Beijing).

They were asked to bring a letter to the Pope and were given a paiza, a golden tablet that granted them safe passage through the Khan’s lands. They made the journey back west, but were unable to deliver the letter immediately, as the Pope had died and a new one had not yet been elected.

This game is not how their trip went. (For one thing, the paiza was a foot long and 3 inches wide, not the size of Niccolo’s torso). Think of this more like the loosely related NES tie-in game for the historical event.

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Super simple graphics but very enthralling game-play, I enjoyed it a lot!

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: nice game i finished a few levels

comments.author: Allen Schmidt
comments.text: Nice little platformer! Reminded me a little of the older Apogee, where you have a single room and solve a puzzle. I got through a bunch of levels but got stuck on the level in the screenshot above. It definitely needs an indicator to show which brother you are controlling, as I accidentally switched between them and got confused a few times haha.

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
Cool, a throwback to C64-times :)

The jumping/gravity needs a little tweaking.

Nice room- and puzzle-designs

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text:
Them dudes are soaring like absolute rockets, no wonder they were chosen for the trip! Whenever there’s no solid roof, I bypassed any semblance of puzzles by jumping over most of the level. :)

Seriously though, that’s a cool idea: kind of like Portal 2 co-op but in 2D, with buttons and platforms.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
good entry! Sounds, nice pixels and a challenging levels.

Ctrl for pick up was a littel awkward

comments.author: Johan Åhlgren
comments.text: A very nice platformer, very retro! I liked the challenges it posed, where you had to use both Niccolo and Maffeo to solve the puzzles. Good job!

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: I played a few levels. Has some strong NES vibes, although it doesn’t exactly stick to the pixel grid ;) The game is fun, and you created some great 2-player puzzles, but the reason I didn’t play more than a few levels is that it’s soo slow. Make the characters 50% faster for a much higher engagement.

comments.author: Joanna Jardine
comments.text: Super nice entry :-) Puzzles were fun to solve and sound effects were fitting! Movements felt a bit slow, and controls were a little stiff - as mentioned already, maybe interacting with stuff would’ve worked better with something other than CTRL? Even so, I enjoyed playing through the levels!

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text:
I quite enjoyed this retro-style puzzle game.

Good level design, played a few levels.

I feel like the movement could be improved (jumping is a little strange, maybe due to the gravity?) and the characters feel a little bit too slow.

comments.author: Magnus Lindh
comments.text:
Fun but very frustrating. So easy to push the wrong button. I guess it is a good exercise in maintaining patience. The speed is perfect. Perhaps you could highlight the active player?

Graphics and sound are basic just like in the good old days. Still I wish the sound effects could have been somewhat smoother.

Overall, very nice puzzle game!

comments.author: Taufik Sani
comments.text:
First of all, love it!

Taking a popular game and developing it in your vision is not easy, but you managed to incorporate the 13th century into the gameplay.

I like how you have to control two players to achieve the goal, and go through levels of puzzles to get to the end game.

What could possibly be improved is to add the narrative at the start of the game to immerse the player right away into the 1st level.

comments.author: stxtus
comments.text: Controls take effort to get a hang of but… tell you what i like the details

comments.author: Frahaan Hussain
comments.text: Feels like a NES classic which is nice. Nice switching mechanic, improved menu system would be nice.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Love the look and feel of the game, it’s fun to play.

I like the graphics and the gameplay mechanics.

The levels get hard pretty quickly, but at least you get to retry the level if you die without having to go back to the very beginning.

Also, the sound effects work well.

api.name: Castle wars
api.description:
Minimalistic web strategy game. Player have to capture all enemy castles.

Vanila JS.

Controls

  • in menu:
    • left mouse button click to select options
    • esc to return to upper menu
  • in game:
    • left mouse button click to select your castles (usually blue)
    • right click on different castle - send troops from selected casle
    • right click on selected castle - upgrade your castle (if you have enough troops)

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text: This is a fun strategy game! Galcon but medieval. I dig the style of the castles, too.

comments.author: Chris Glover
comments.text: I played this for way longer than I expected to. Fun game, I liked the visuals too

comments.author: Santi Herranz
comments.text:
Nice gameplay, good engagement and well balanced

I would like to make a version for tablets by dragging your finger

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: Yes, didn’t get enough time for tablets. Also I think game needs some optimization. Thanks for feedback

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Game looks amazing for not using a 3d library. Fun to play on desktop, but I guess lots of clicking, may work better on tablet.

I liked all the little soldiers moving around the map, but after attacking I wanted them back to attach other bases.

I did ok in the tutorial when the enemy didn’t attack back, but past that I didn’t do very well, the CPU was a formidable opponent.

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text:
Well, soldiers are dying until enemy castle lives moves to 0, after that they became castle garrison) It may be not optimal rules Game isn’t perfectly balanced

But you are right, AI isn’t really smart, but super fast, so fast clicking is crucial

Thanks for feedback

comments.author: Grav
comments.text: This one is a hidden gem. For sure could be expanded to larger game. Emergent gameplay is great, just needs a few tunes and it’d be perfect.

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text:
Well, game isn’t perfectly balanced Also it’s not my rules, there are many games with that mechanic, like galcon. So this contest game is more about tech and hacks of fast prototyping)

But I’m working on my mobile game and hope it will be also such strategy, but more innovative.

Thanks for feedback.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Enjoyed playing, graphics are good and the controls and gameplay works well.

clicking very quickly seemed to be the best tactic, would have been good to have some other important choices to make.

I like the variations in levels / enemy colours etc

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text:
Thanks for feedback

Yes, game are not enough balanced. But I’m working on similar strategy (even more innovative) for mobile.

Yes, I can imagine more gameplay options - special buildings, global strikes (like spells for mana or spy for gold) etc

But this project was concentrated on tech details and also it’s super hacky to fit into JS13k

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: Impressive 3D: I like the camera movments and the ondulating landscape! The IA is great and the levels progression is perfect. Maybe adding some some music would be great

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: Thanks! Yes, I also wanted some music and effects and even started with some audio lib, but didn’t get enough time.

comments.author: Matt Hackett
comments.text:
Very simple and effective renderer; has a nice chill vibe to it.

The interface is nice ’n clean. It’s clear what you’re supposed to be doing and how to do it. Nice!

Gameplay-wise, it’s satisfying to send large armies after your enemies. The numbers don’t seem to “match up”, tho, by which I meant: if you have, say, 100 population and you attack an army of 50, it seems like your target “heals” as you’re attacking (or something), so your attack won’t finish off the army (but it feels like it should). A “fix” for this might be pausing the spawning while an attack is ongoing.

The maps with multiple enemy factions get very complex and interesting! It does make me yearn for a little more complexity in the inputs / decisions available to me, but maybe those are possibilities for the sequel.

Overall very playable and well crafted game for just 13K! Good work.

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: Thanks for feedback! Yes, it’s because they still produce the same amount of soldiers on attack. Maybe stop generating on attack time would be interesting mechanic, but then you also need to stop offender spawn too. Anyway, I also thought about some balance and gameplay improvement. Maybe in my mobile game, I’m trying to finish my mobile project.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
Lots of fun, this one! I played for quite a while because winning felt so satisfying. The animations, the strategy and the upgrades. The vibe of the graphics is really cool as well. The only thing missing is some background music or sound effects! The one thing I would complain is the lack of feedback when I’m clicking. I never know if my clicks are actually doing something or if I’m over-clicking for nothing.

I found that in this game the best strategy is usually brute force. I was very aggressive right off the bat sending all my troops out because if I could conquer more neutral castles first, that gives me an edge.

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: Yes, it would be better to add some additional strategies beside brute force, like spy or magic (not for 13th century ofk) Also it looks like some combinations aren’t balanced - small castles almost useless and you should have a chance to capture enemy castle without level reducing. Well, lack of time, as usual. Thanks for playing!

comments.author: Aurélio A. Heckert
comments.text:
I love this kind of “conqueror like” game! Unfortunately you don’t bring noting new to this kind, however I did play a lot, and I could play for hours if it was multiplayer. And the visual is pretty good!

The left click to improve is simpler and faster, however you need the user to read the manual. It sometimes is mandatory for 13k games, but we must try to escape form demanding manual reading. A button over the castle could be better.

The CPU opponent is not improving its castles. That helps me to always easily win.

Did I told I loved it? Because I loved!

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text:
He should sometimes improve them, it’s weird. But yes, he is artifical idiot, so. But he is fast at least.

I think balance and gameplay can be improved a lot in many ways.

Thanks for playing!

comments.author: Noël Meudec
comments.text:
What I liked:

  • Simple but good graphics
  • Interesting strategy (very similar to Risk)
  • Various levels
  • Good A.I.

What I liked less:

  • Hard to play with a trackpad (I don’t have a mouse…)
  • It gets quite hard pretty fast
  • No sound

Overall, it is a great game. I wish I had a mouse, as playing with a trackpad makes it way harder.

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text:
Yes, there are some problems with balance and on some point it became fast clicking contest. Also not adopted for touch or trackpad. I planned for sounds, even added some library and tested some effects, but as usual, lack of time.

Thanks for playing!

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
Oh and I’m hooked on this game… I’ve played all the levels and some random ones too - I really enjoyed it - congrats!

I think some relaxing music would improve the game! And multiplayer mode with different game modes is also possible: NxM, etc…

comments.author: Marcin Walczak
comments.text:
Fast paced, engaging and fun strategy game.

I like the blocky visual style and animation of terrain and castle’s upgrading / degrading.

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: Yes, I also wanted to add some levels on the island and add animated sea with same floating effect, but lack of time. As wel as for sound. Thanks for playing!

comments.author: Taufik Sani
comments.text:
The good: The game might’ve been developed with vanilla JS, but gameplay isn’t vanilla at all! This being in real-time feels exhilarating, forcing players to make mistakes under pressure.

I like the variety of levels with different scenarios - the game pans to the whole spectrum of players.

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text:
I tried) Next time maybe will try WebGL also I don’t like JS in general, so don’t want to learn libs, this fast hacky contests - my best options, at least they limited)

Thanks for playing!

api.name: Duelo
api.description:
Step into the arena and embrace the thrill of medieval combat in Duelo, a unique fighting game that puts you in the boots of gallant knights wielding mighty swords.

Duelo combines the fast-paced action of classic fighting games with the strategic depth of the combat mechanics from Prince of Persia. Engage in intense duels against skilled opponents. Block, parry, and strike with precision to claim victory in the arena.

Key Features

  • Weapon Mastery: Discover and collect swords of varying power as you progress. Upgrade your arsenal to unleash devastating attacks on your foes.
  • Armor Upgrades: Equip pieces of armor to increase your resilience and survive the fiercest of battles.
  • Shop: Visit the in-game shop (powered by NEAR) to trade collected items for gold.

As the battle rages on, keep a watchful eye on your foes, for legends speak of a rare prize, shimmering like the dawn’s first light, that may appear only after the crucible of level 15, if fate smiles upon you.

Controls

  • Arrow right/left to move
  • Arrow up to defend
  • Space to attack
  • Double arrow right/left for special move

Tips for success

Timing is key in Duelo. Study your opponents, anticipate their moves, and strike at the perfect moment to gain the upper hand. Don’t forget to visit the shop to keep your knight at the peak of readiness.

Sharpen your blade, don your armor, and embark on a knightly adventure. Play Duelo now and prove your worth in the arena!

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Great effort. I really liked the background and how it scales out so nicely when you put distance between the combatants.

comments.author: Thiago Romão Barcala
comments.text: I was afraid people would complain about the camera moving too much. :D Thanks for the feedback!

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
A very tough genre to attempt and this is a good effort, nice graphics including the background.

Made it to level 6 where an ememy with a sword killed me, would have been nice to have a chance against them, needed some upgrades maybe :D

Scored high on innovation as you never see this type of game.

comments.author: Thiago Romão Barcala
comments.text: The idea of the game was that you would lose, and start playing again, collecting the items from the enemies you defeat and slowly get stronger and stronger. It is a bit too hard to collect the items (sometimes you get two swords but you don’t have an armor), and this is where I wished people would exchange items in the shop.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: I liked the character design. For some reason, it made the fight look rather funny. Nice work on the graphics!

comments.author: Thiago Romão Barcala
comments.text: I made it to look like it is a kind of sport rather than fighting for your life, even though it is not historically accurate. Thanks for the feedback!

comments.author: jahas
comments.text:
It’s generally great game. It’s pretty innovative in my opinion. Some PROs:

  • Great idea to do single move at one time
  • It’s very playable as soon as you find out how to play and how to win
  • Upgrades have sense.

Unfortunately, there are some minor drawbacks:

  • When you loose you have to start from beginning
  • Upgrades happen very randomly. it takes you to place when your opponent will kill you with one hit and last ten prizes were gold.

comments.author: Thiago Romão Barcala
comments.text: Yeah, I noticed that sometimes when I was playing I could get far in the game while still having a very basic sword, which made the game too hard. I hoped this would serve as an incentive for people to exchange items through the NEAR store, but that didn’t happen.

comments.author: ch!k0
comments.text: the graphics used in the game were nice and the game itself was very interesting! just that some hits on the opponent didn’t have an effect and there were delays on the controls. also a finish page/display would have been a nice touch win/lose :) overall it was nice!

comments.author: Thiago Romão Barcala
comments.text: The controls can be a bit frustrating, I know, but it is part of the game. It is all about synchronizing your movement. Thanks for the feedback!

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: The game seems nice, but the fact I need to buy items through NEAR kind of limits the playability. I was expecting to be able to steal the armor and swords of the defeated. Really nice graphics, atmosphere and animations. Would be nice to be able to use just the keyboard instead of having to change to the mouse to click on buttons.

comments.author: Thiago Romão Barcala
comments.text: Yeah, after playing the submitted version I also thought I should have added some keyboard shortcuts. In the development version I had keys that would instantly defeat the opponent, go to the next opponent, etc. It didn’t disturb me during development, so I missed that. About stealing the items from the defeated, you actually can do that. If you defeat one opponent there is a chance that one of their items will drop and you can pick it up. The only thing you can do in the NEAR store is to exchange the items you collected with other people. So I would say it limits the playability. It easens it, or enhances it.

comments.author: Daniel Whiffing
comments.text: Very refined graphics! The gameplay is nice and simple but the starting enemies are a bit boring to fight since they mostly just stand there. It’d be nice if the opening levels were more fun to play since you have to start over when you lose. Hiding a major mechanic of the game behind NEAR is unfortunate, but it is what it is.

comments.author: Thiago Romão Barcala
comments.text: Once you get a sword the first enemies are so easy to beat that it didn’t bother me that much, since you can defeat them almost always with one hit. But maybe it would have been better if I had estimated the level of the player based on their items and started from a slightly more advanced level directly. Thanks for the feedback!

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text:
Not my style of game but it looks graphically very good. The movement is a little bit clunky or slow-paced.

I played through a couple of levels where I managed to defeat the enemies without any upgrade and suddenly I get one-shotted by someone.

Having to use NEAR shop limits the game massively.

comments.author: Thiago Romão Barcala
comments.text: You don’t actually HAVE to use the NEAR shop. It is just there in case you want to exchange the items with other players. Sure, it makes the game a bit easier cause sometimes the items you expect don’t drop for very long time, but it was supposed to be part of the challenge. Thanks for the feedback!

comments.author: Tim West
comments.text:
This seems like a fairly sophisticated game, really nice job on the execution!

I didn’t want to create an account to access the in-game shop, so it became extremely challenging to progress past a few enemies. I don’t blame the game for this design decision, but it does make it more difficult to appreciate the full game.

The AI sometimes seems unresponsive. At specific distances, the enemy will idle until the player approaches or attacks.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Pretty impressive 3d looking graphics.

The fighting mechanic is good, although feels a little jerky.

The dude with the sword is unforgiving, feel like I need a weapon to even consider taking him on.

comments.author: Tim Sulmone
comments.text: The near wallet implementation was wonderful! After every buy in-game it wrote on the chain a buy smart contract, loved seeing that. Gamefi :-)

api.name: The Mongol Horde
api.description:
The year is 1258. The Mongol Empire is rapidly expanding west, invading and conquering all lands they pass through. You have been notified that Mongol forces are drawing in, it is time to take up arms and defend the treasure!

Controls:

  • Select troops from the toolbar and click onto the map to place them
  • When placing a troop or hovering over it their attack radius will be shown
  • Hovringthe toolbar shows troop cost, health, damage, range, and attack speed
  • The bin tool allows you to remove troops but beware you only get 1 gold refunded!
  • Walls don’t fight back but provide good protection to archers or knights hiding behind them

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: having them randomly move is challenging and cool

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text: Very simple but very effective game! I liked it a lot - graphics, sound and gameplay work very well together. I wish there were more units available (or different strategies). In any case, a nice little game :)

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text:
Fun defense game and nice idea for the theme!

I found the game maybe a bit too hard, and I really didn’t like the popup right in the middle of the screen in between waves. I’m supposed to be able to use that time to place troops, but there as a giant message in my way.

Still, played a few times and made it to wave 13, a very nice little tower defense game.

comments.author: Alexander Petrov
comments.text: Difficulty went from trivial to impossible very fast at around wave 17. Rows of #4 - #3 - #2 worked every time, with occasionally having to rebuild some #4. But then a spam of the top tier enemies has overwhelmed me

comments.author: Aleksey Litvinov
comments.text: I think the tower defence gameplay fits the story very well! Managed to get to level 18 :) would be nice to add some upgrades in future :)

comments.author: FoumartGames
comments.text: I never used the delete troops button - a repair or heal would have been a better option. The latest waves had me spamming walls in the center of the field like crazy! At first I was surprised that the wolves also chased my treasure. Very nice and addicting defense game, my best: 19/256/428

comments.author: Luke Stapley
comments.text: For a small game, this was very good. Have a lot of great stuff. I think that the game moves at a good pace, but I wish I could do more to upgrade my fighters. Was assuming it was a tower defense with the roads, but I was rudely awakened by the first fight. I think this is a great job for what you were asked to do!

comments.author: Misael Braga
comments.text: I have enjoyed the graphics and the game is addicting, its great!

comments.author: Marcin Walczak
comments.text:
Nice tower defense. Making enemies move independently instead of beaten path gives a extra challenge.

Defending wave after wave, building up defenses, fortifying choke points and delaying the inevitable can be quite addictive.

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
Very nice!

Controls great– very intuitive to play. Really nice pixel graphics, too.

I made it to Round 16, but eventually I found myself just building knights and archers. I wonder if some upgrades or more varied unit types would be cool (spiked walls or long-ranged archers or something)

Anyway, very fun game. Nicely done!!

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text: I dig the graphical style, especially the tree sprites. The mechanics are really well done, they’re simple and you feel like you know what you’re doing at all times. Put this game on mobile, and you’ll totally have people playing during a commute.

comments.author: stxtus
comments.text: Playable fun little game. I got a hang of it after 1, 2 try

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
A fun game which I could play for hours.

It’s interesting to formulate a strategy to defend the treasure.

The graphics are good, and it’s easy to pick up how to play the game.

It seems a little hard that when a new wave starts all the enemies have full health, but all your remaining defenders stay on whatever little health they had at the end of the last wave.

comments.author: Björn Ritzl
comments.text: Nice variation on a tower defence game! I really liked how the attacking waves didn’t follow a predefined path (although they will the nearest path to the treasure). And the fact that you can block the path using the walls is a nice touch. I like the graphics. Good job!

api.name: Keep Calm
api.description: Fight off those attempting to lay claim to your castle by shooting them with your Kingly lasers.

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text:
For some reason the game always spawned right at my feet (both sitting and standing), which made things a bit awkward. I am not sure if I missed something but there wasn’t much to do other than shooting people (there was a counter which went to -infinity but no real goals).

Besides this, I think it’s a super cool toy: the little people walking on the forest, the killer lasers and the physics are awesome! With a few specific objectives, it will become a great game.

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text:
Simple but very nice graphics the triangular trees and fog together with a nice castle looks great.

You spawn with a big text in front of you, which does not disappear. Laser shooting is not very on theme (might have called it a magic wand), but satisfying to see the little soldiers fly off.

But no gameplay just super fast soldiers coming from two sides, no goal or progression. This has some nice potential, but as it is it should go in the unfinished category.

comments.author: Teddy Chen
comments.text: The environment looks nice! I like shooting lasers. A variety of attacks would make things more interesting, because I feel like I’m just pointing and clicking.

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: Game looks really fast and uncontrollable for me Looks like some issue: The file at ’data:image/png;base64,

comments.author: John Swana
comments.text: Missing playback hardware but, this game appears to have well worked on graphics

comments.author: Sébastien Vandenberghe
comments.text:
The game is beautiful and I love the full world you were able to bring together !!!

The gameplay and controls nevertheless would require a tiny bit more love. I think small tweaks there would make the game awesome !!!

comments.author: Fernando Serrano Garcia
comments.text: Very original and intense! :) I think the idea is quite good and serves as a foundation for further improvement and the incorporation of new dynamics, such as new weapons (bombs?, cannon turrets?) and new enemies.

api.name: Medieval Matchup
api.description:
A tranquil puzzle game where you match stones to build your tiny island with all the comforts of the Middle Ages!

Best experienced on Meta Quest!!

Works also on Oculus Go, Desktop and Android mobile (preferably on a tablet)

To rotate the model:

  • On Meta Quest use thumbsticks
  • On Oculus Go use the trackpad
  • On desktop use mouse-drag
  • On mobile use touch-drag

Refresh the page to go back to the menu.

comments.author: Teddy Chen
comments.text: Solid and well-polished game, great as a tranquil sit-down VR experience. The sound effects are well-timed whenever you make a match.

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text: Thanks Teddy. Quite surprised how much I could get in with even room left for some sounds and the seagulls.

comments.author: Rémi Vansteelandt
comments.text:
Sound is very buggy on Firefox. It cuts off a lot and hurts my ears. It’s better on Chrome but don’t forget to test multiple browsers.

Otherwise cool little game, I liked how trees grew after I completed the first level.

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text:
You are of course right, I tested on Chrome and Edge, but not on Firefox. Only after publishing I noticed that on Chrome the click sounds cut off after a couple of levels. Lesson learned for next year!

Thanks for your feedback!

comments.author: Keith Karnage
comments.text: Off theme. Played on a laptop. Simple, but solid. Could have used pleasant music as well, the ding got boring. Getting tiny games to work on VR seems pretty innovative to me. Too bad I don’t have resources to judge that. Gamewise it is an interesting take on majong. Seeing through the structure to the other side was a definite gain over other games like it. I like the symbol set used, they were easy to differentiate against one another, and the shininess of them was nice too. Sometimes had trouble clicking one, but otherwise it controlled fine. All in all this was a great, albeit simple game. It would be nice to see what else can be packed into a tiny VR game. Good job!

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text: Ouch, you hurt me with your off theme remark :-) I even looked up what kind of windmills were used in the 14th century! But it’s perhaps not as clearly linked to the specific period as some of the other games. Thanks for the constructive feedback!

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: graphics are cool nice work, background is very live

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text: Thank you for playing! I had hoped to add seagull sounds to polish off the background sounds, but only 12 bytes left :-P

comments.author: Nathan Long
comments.text: Fun, cozy, very straightforward. Not sure how the theme fits in, but overall a nice little game.

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text: Hi Nathan, thanks for your feedback. You’re not the first to comment on the theme, probably because for me the 14th century was more about the period than a specific event. Definitely something to think about next year.

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text:
Great Job!

Simple, but interesting puzzle, interesting method to pair generation (and unical images generation as combination of squares and dots with fill and different positions) Interesting, that it has a kinda story. Good level of work.

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text: You’re the first to notice there is a (extremely simple) story of landing on an island and civilization slowly taking hold. Your feedback is much appreciated!

comments.author: Jure Triglav
comments.text: Nice game! Features well designed symbols, relaxing wave sounds, good targeting and clear game mechanics. Loved how you progress through the game and the world builds around you. The seagull is a nice touch, too. Good stuff, congratulations! :)

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text: Thanks for your feedback. Although I kind of knew it, I was still surprised myself how much difference the small details made in the final result. The seagulls and the wave sounds were the last additions to the game but they had a big impact on the overal feeling of the game. Thanks for noticing :-)

comments.author: stxtus
comments.text: Nice graphics and color combinations

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text: Thanks for playing! Much appreciated!

comments.author: Sébastien Vandenberghe
comments.text:
Such a pretty game I want to keep playing :-) I have to admit I probably spent 40 minutes on it and new puzzles kept coming.

I love how much importance you attached to the graphic experience as it helps a lot with immersion and retention. The seagull animation is an amazing touch bringing life to the environment.

On the downside, the relation to the theme might be a bit far fetched even if I could still understand how it relates.

Thanks again for the game, I might go an play again.

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text: Thanks for playing and your very nice words! Even though I thought I was good on theme, your are not the first to remark on that. I’m definitely improving on that next year! :-)

comments.author: Andrew Higson-Smith
comments.text: Looks beautiful - nice twist on a familiar scene - fun to play

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text: Thanks for playing, and for your feedback!

comments.author: Fernando Serrano Garcia
comments.text: I love it! The game is enjoyable, the aesthetics are well-crafted and consistent, and the controls are comfortable and intuitive. The only thing I miss is a more challenging mode, such as preventing the use of pieces that are trapped inside, similar to what happens in Mahjong. Excellent job!

api.name: Saeculum XIII agricultura
api.description:
This is a click farming game, where your goal is to leave the peasant life behind and buy your own farm!

When you click on the soil it asks you for what it needs you to give it.

The plough, allows you to air the soil, use poop to fertilize the soil. Choose from one of the seeds to plant a crop of your choice., Discover the different stats that each plant has. Water your crops so that they grow the use the basket to sell them and make money.

Don’t forget that the kingdom will always collect what it’s owed.

You need to get 100 coins to buy your farm and win the game!

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text:
After you get a bit of money, the game is quite hard to lose because you only lose a percentage of what you make. It’d be cool if there was a minimum flat rate with a percentage on top.

After buying your own farm, it’d be nice to go to a second level where I could employ workers to plough/fertelise/water/seed the soil too.

As it is, it’s a pretty solid game! Good job!

comments.author: Luke Stapley
comments.text:
A bit tedious, but you get the rules quickly. I was able to beat the game fairly quickly.

I like the idea that you need to hurry as the money you have started to go down every minute, making you pick to farm more land or move quickly with a small crop.

Overall, a good starting point, but needs a lot more tinkering.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: Fun concept with a clear objective to work towards. Cute graphics. The “fantasy” font in the beginning looks looks like gibberish on my system. I hit an issue where i couldn’t water certain vegetables, as if there was something in front on them. I wish there were keyboard shortcuts to change actions. Ah and a little pet peeve: in our timeline, you couldn’t have grown wheat, onions and corn at the same time in the XIII century!

comments.author: Grav
comments.text: Great game you have here! As another commenters noted, the font didn’t render correctly on my system, but its fine. I was able still to read it by copying it into a text editor though. It is an interesting take on the farming sim, much more frenetic than the relaxed tone they normally take. Once you reach the watering phase, it becomes a bit like whack-a-mole which played well on my touchscreen. I liked that the icons show you exactly what you need to do next which eliminated the learning curve, and I feel the game is paced well. Adding some simple music would have elevated it a lot. Overall, this was a fun entry, well done!

comments.author: Richard Davey
comments.text:
Wow, my mouse-clicking finger really aches after 9 minutes of playing this! But I totally enjoyed it. You have to hoe the land, then add fertilizer, then plant seeds, then water multiple times before finally harvesting. All against the clock.

Tip: Don’t start like I did and try to hoe all of the land. Just focus on a small area (I found 4x4 was enough). Once you’ve a few coins in the bank (from harvesting), when the timer runs out, it takes some of them away and then resets. So you carry on building up the land until eventually you’ve over 100 coins and can pay off your debt.

Yes, it’s a little tedious, but in a nice and peaceful way. Some calming sound effects or music might have helped bolster it, but all in all, it was a highly enjoyable 10 mins completing this game.

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
I liked the game! Such a clicker.

Sometimes some fields were not active for watering. Also, I could sow the grain first, and then sow the corn after the fertilizer… it would be good in that case if the field just didn’t produce seeds. It would also be good to highlight the field when hovering more precisely (select or change the mouse cursor, etc.).

Maybe if you add rain and sunny weather that would have influence to the fields, it would add interest.

comments.author: Almut Kieffer-Jones
comments.text:
The first few rounds I lost (Why are 23% on 0 more than 0?)

When I finally got it, it was more fun! I like the graphics, how the crops slowly grow from step to step.

An idea to extend the gameplay/difficulty: “greedy king” mode, increase the base pay and percentage over time. (Also: Have a base pay at all, to show how you can lose before having income, e.g. display as 1c + 23%

comments.author: Jeremy Burns
comments.text: Interesting concept. I’d like to play further but everything I planted and fertilized was stuck on the leaf icon forever until I died when I played. The water didn’t seem to do anything.

api.name: belly flop
api.description: flop your belly, flap your baskets, flop your baskets, flap your belly, and vice versa.

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text:
I don’t understand. Maybe it’s for the best.

The graphical style is interesting, and the instructions are fun.

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text:
Best “medieval mushroom induced hallucination” simulator :D

Now more seriously - I have absolutely no idea what was going on, but I really appreciate the instruction for lowering the volume :)

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text:
“Lower your volume”

How about you lower the volume of the game…

comments.author: Jeremy Burns
comments.text: Curious, hypnotic, strange, memorable

comments.author: Francesco Bedussi
comments.text: I cannot understand if this is pure genius or just a random set of sound and pixels.

comments.author: Matt Hackett
comments.text:
My first thought: “no, YOU lower YOUR volume!” ;)

It’s like some kind of weird acid trip musical instrument.

I’m not sure what’s happening but the atmosphere and aesthetics are very stylized!

comments.author: Christer Kaitila
comments.text: Very cool! I wanted more agency. I wanted to grab it all and smash it together, twist it, burn it, swirl it around and shake it out, but instead I felt powerless except when I took control of my speaker volume. Nobody could take that away from me! My speakers have a physical volume knob. Tangible and touchable, I was able to wrest some control in my life and be an autonomous being in charge of their own life, for once, as this volume can’t be changed by mere software - no, once I turned down my volume - here in the real world - no hacker, no broken device driver, no 13kb of quality coding could take that away from me - once silenced, the moment was mine to cherish, my hand hovering on the volume knob of my speakers like the hand of a diety, the entire auditory spectrum of this game in my grasp, at my control, loud or silent at my whim. Feeling empowered, I closed the game, satisfied I’d learned the lesson.

comments.author: Allen Schmidt
comments.text: Having the understanding makes for beating the game, an easy concept, yet to grasp the graphics by the hands. Dice you can click, and the numbers influence the plow, yet even then the audio was lowered and text and text and text. I like how the lines squiggle when things happen, so maybe that is for the best of us.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
The glitching effect was really good, as were the surreal sound effects.

It was nice to just watch the screen as time went on, no interactions necessary.

From the description and inability I was at a loss as to how to influence the art piece.

But thanks it was an experience.

api.name: FREEEDOOOM!!!!!!!
api.description:
Take on the role of Scottish knight, William Wallace, in a retelling of the battle of Stirling Bridge. Charge along the bridge, fighting English soldiers, dodging arrows and avoiding obstacles. This game was created as an entry for js13kgames 2023.

(Firefox browser recommended)

🎮 Controls

  • Press W / ⬆ to jump
  • Hold S / ⬇ to slide
  • Press Space to attack

🛠 Tools

  • Built with kontra.js
  • Minified using rollup-plugin-kontra and roadroller
  • Pixel art text generated with minipixelart

Pther

Built by fellow scots. Art was created by us!

comments.author: Misael Braga
comments.text: The game is simple but the theme and the main sprite are awesome!

comments.author: Cameron Early
comments.text: Thanks Misael, we really focused on the art, we’re glad you liked it!

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Super simple and kind of fun, excellent stuff. Some more polish would have made it shine :)

comments.author: Cameron Early
comments.text: Thank you Cliff, we really wish we had more time to perfect it as there’s so much we wanted to add to it!

comments.author: Oliver Smith
comments.text: I like the art style and the parallax scrolling, it looks good. Some sounds would have added to it but it is fun to play and well presented.

comments.author: Cameron Early
comments.text: Thank you Oliver, the parallax background was something we were really happy with. We had sound effects, but we had to remove them in the end to fit all of the art in :(

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text:
Managed to get to 524 without sweating it, so it would definitely be better to make it more difficult faster :)

Besides this, I like many things about this game: it’s simple, but fun; the parallax and art style is great (the trees, castle and “dead sprites” are cool touches). I wish it had music but overall, cool little game.

comments.author: Cameron Early
comments.text:
Hi Almar, 524? Show off…

Only kidding, thank you for the positive feedback!

We had music and sound effects initially and they added a lot to the game, but it was between that and more detailed art / animations when it came to the size limit.

comments.author: Emre Guneyler
comments.text: great the things that are worked on, could be make better use of left and right arrows.

comments.author: Cameron Early
comments.text: Thank you for the feedback, that’s something to think about for next year… :)

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: Nice parallax animation and pixel art, and the William Wallace character looks a bit goofy, which is fun. The gameplay is a bit classic. Would have been nice to have some audio.

comments.author: Joanna Jardine
comments.text: Hey Jack, thanks for the feedback! This was our first time developing a game and entering a jam so while we definitely would’ve liked to make it a bit more unique, we ultimately didn’t have the capacity. Glad you liked the art! Your entry was also awesome, we enjoyed playing it :-)

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text:
I didn’t know William Wallace fought skeletons :). But joking aside really great graphics. Not many games pull off large sprites, animations, and parallax scrolling backgrounds, so well done! Very impressive.

Controls were responsive, I like that you can slide indefinitely. I do think the game is a bit too easy for too long. I made it to over 700 on my first try, so I think a faster speed ramp up would be nice. And of course audio. Some powerups or other things would be nice since the gameplay is pretty simple.

Still though, I thought it was a really nice entry with amazing graphics.

comments.author: Joanna Jardine
comments.text: Thank you for the kind words! Yeah, I see what you mean about the speed. We weren’t really sure what a good starting speed would be (too fast? too slow?) which maybe would’ve been solved with some more thorough play testing. Something to think about for next year :-) We absolutely thought about including power-ups but unfortunately didn’t have enough time in the end.

comments.author: Stiggstogg
comments.text: Nice endless runner with some nice graphics. I enjoyed playing it. Some music or sound effects would have made the experience a bit better.

comments.author: Joanna Jardine
comments.text: We appreciate the feedback, glad you enjoyed playing!

comments.author: Rémi Vansteelandt
comments.text: I love these simple running games. I do wish there was a bit more depth to it (enemy patterns, stamina, ammo, different levels to show progression…) and a few more animations/juice. That would have kept me playing even longer.

comments.author: Cameron Early
comments.text: Hi Rémi, thank you for your feedback! Those are good suggestions, definitely something for next year! :)

comments.author: Sophie
comments.text: Really nice game, the art is really nice especially since it’s by the creator of the game.

comments.author: Cameron Early
comments.text: Thank you Sophie! Appreciate it!

comments.author: Clément Pasteau
comments.text:
Hey there!

I’ll probably repeat what most players said, but let’s be honest, it’s a great full game! It’s quite rare to have a fully finished, playable and fun game so congrats.

Too bad you’re missing on audio, no need for sound effects, just a tiny background music would have added so much.

I also think ramping up the difficulty faster would make players want to try again to beat their score more! Having a leaderboard would have also made it incredibly more competitive and sticky!

Finally, great sprites, right in the theme. Really well done!

comments.author: Joanna Jardine
comments.text: Hi Clément! Thank you for the kind words, glad you thought the game was fun. We did have music and sound effects originally, but had to remove them due to last minute size constraints. It definitely would’ve made the game feel more polished by having music in (it was actually an 8-bit version of “Scotland the Brave”)! The idea of a leaderboard is nice, maybe in the same vein as old arcade cabinets.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text: Nice pixel art, all looked really nice. Simple gameplay but well made!

comments.author: Cameron Early
comments.text: Thank you Ryan, glad you liked the art!

comments.author: Isaac Benitez
comments.text:
Pretty cool graphics. Love the pixel art. It gets pretty fun when it starts getting faster.

It would have been great to have music and sounds. It would be nice to have more obstacles or enemies as well.

Good job!

comments.author: Joanna Jardine
comments.text: Thanks Isaac, glad you liked the art! :-) Unfortunately the spritesheet was ramping the file size up so we had to be very conservative about what we could and couldn’t add. Thank you for the feedback!

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
Recognized by Mel Gibson :).

Skeletons in 13th centery? :D

Good game, congratulations!

comments.author: Cameron Early
comments.text:
Hi Roman, there might’ve been skeletons in the battle of Stirling for all we know! 🤷‍♂️

Kidding aside, thank you for your feedback!

comments.author: Michael M.
comments.text: Small, but addictive! Two things you could improve: There is no penalty for just pressing the space bar the whole time, so I just did that and only had to care to jump/slide when necessary. If pressing space bar would prohibit jumping/sliding at the same time, the game would be more challenging. And there are sometimes 1px gaps in the background. Seems like an off-by-one error or something.

comments.author: Joanna Jardine
comments.text: Hi Michael, thanks for the feedback! Hmm, I did try to fix the gaps in the background, but as we didn’t actually import the spritesheet until the last day or so we couldn’t really get a good look at it. Unfortunately didn’t seem to budge when I moved the background’s position around either, so not too sure what’s up with it! D:

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
This was surprisingly fun. I’ve played similar games before but still had a good time playing for a few minutes.

The font is readable but often so big it doens’t fit the screen. Unfortunately a consequence of using miniPixelFont (I sadly used the same but regret it).

The art of this game is so adorable, specially the landscape… Perhaps the boulders should be bigger and more threatening than a small ball? I shouldn’t die from that! This game also really needs music and sound effects.

comments.author: Cameron Early
comments.text: Hi João, thanks for the kind words! In regards to the font, we’re definitely going to explore more options next year. One mistake we made was that we never playtested across different browsers, so unfortunately it wasn’t as consistent as we’d hoped (especially on Safari). The boulders were initially meant to be bigger, but we also had to consider what the sprite could realistically jump over. We thoroughly enjoyed playing your entry too!

comments.author: Matthew Barr
comments.text: The Empire Strikes Back of 13k games.

comments.author: Cameron Early
comments.text: Hi Matt, thank you for this high honour!!!

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
A fun game to play

Felt a bit like bop-it where you see something coming and have to react quickly with the correct key press - or die

Graphics were good and the way the game played was amusing, I particularly liked the ducking skid. Also thought the parallax scrolling worked really well

comments.author: Joanna Jardine
comments.text: Thanks Jasper, we appreciate the positive feedback :-) Glad you liked the animations, the ducking skid was my favourite to work on!

comments.author: Taufik Sani
comments.text:
Loving this side scroller! ✅ Level design is great ✅ Variety of enemies ✅ Sliding animation is cool (feels like Call of Duty sliding!) ✅Game gets more difficult the longer you progress

Suggestions:

  • Embedding the tutorial within the game (player encounters obstacle, game is paused, player is instructed to press W, etc. repeat for sliding to dodge arrows, and killing enemies) - and the game just continues as per normal.
  • Pause button (Esc?) to allow players to refer to controls again within the game|
  • sound effects and background music would be great to complement with the theme of the game.
  • as much as i love sliding around, you could challenge the player by preventing players from permanently sliding if they hold the down key.
  • This can game can easily implement a leaderboard - sort of like Flappy Bird?
  • I think the increase in difficulty is quite slow - I think you could use the 200 score speed at 100 to make it more challenging.

comments.author: Cameron Early
comments.text:
Hi there, thank you so much for the positive feedback! The sprite’s sliding animation did remind me of COD while playtesting :)

All of your suggestions are actually ones that we’d thought about during development, but either didn’t have enough time or weren’t sure how to implement them. This is our first time making a game (and entering a game jam), so we’ll take all of this feedback and hopefully improve for next year!

api.name: Market Street Tycoon
api.description:
You are a merchant in the village market, trying to succeed. To do so, buy from the local suppliers and resell them in the weekend market.

Demand for you products depend on the market demand, but also on your reputation selling those products, as well as the price you set compared to what people would pay for it.

There are 9 products to unlock: 🍎 🍞 🪵 🥚 🥧 🏺 💎 🌶.

The game goes on unless you run out of money.

Control the game with arrow keys + Enter, with the gamepad, or on mobile with the virtual gamepad keys.

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text:
Love the detail of the shadows of the crowd going by. Adds a ton to the game with a pretty simple effect, really nice!

Unfortunately at least for me the controls were completely broken. When I tried to change stock, it would jump back up to the previous item and increase the stock of that. I could never actually increase the stock of a secondary item so I gave up.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: I’m sorry to hear about the broken controls. I did test it on a couple different mobile devices but there’s always variation. I’m glad you like the little details. I wanted it to feel alive an not boring even if the market phase is mostly “for show”

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
A nice idea, like a 13th Century Lemonade Tycoon! Nice graphics, UI and animations. I liked the crowd walking past, nice touch.

The font was a little hard to read in some places and the controls a little clunky on some of the buttons.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: Oh, I never played Lemonade Tycoon but I guess that’s pretty much the same kind of game! Sorry about the font. It was an early choice to save bytes but then ran out of time to improve it. Look out for new version releases, I might have already fixed it ;)

comments.author: ch!k0
comments.text: i think the song is stuck in my head lol! i had fun selling and re-stocking my merchs. though whenever there’d be 13+? items, there’s a weird floating brown box that will appear on the cart’s counter and the items images are allover the place. also the monetary symbol and numbers were quite hard to make-out, maybe use smaller fonts next time?

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: I’m glad you liked the song so much. It’s actually based on a part of the classical music Les Patineurs. I regret adding the brown box, it looks weird. I’ve removed it in new versions, as well as improved the font, but glad you enjoyed this version regardless!

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
Ah, my dream comes true, selling stuff on a market! “Jaguars’ earlobes! Dromedar prezels! Get them while they are hot!”

I like the graphic-style (especially the customer-shadows) and the buy-sell workflow with the control over the sell-prices.

Maybe it would be better to be able to use the buttons with the mouse or touch.

Music and sfx are good too.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: Ah, you’re the only one so far that unlocked the secret Dromedar prezels suppliers! :) I wanted it to be a NES-style game, that’s why it’s keyboard/gamepad only.

comments.author: Thiago Romão Barcala
comments.text:
I spent hours playing this game. I got more than 1 million dollars. This is extremely addictive.

Even though the audio is very simple it feels nice, so I kept the music playing the whole time.

One thing that annoyed me a bit is that there is always some offer to sell me wood for all the money I have. I ended up making 99% of my money by selling wood, which is great, but I didn’t have the option to skip buying the wood, to try to improve my reputation for the other goods. Also, I got a great deal to buy 22k wood, and I did it, but then the game started to use A LOT of CPU, to the point that the music couldn’t play anymore. So the next time I got an offer to buy 100k wood I refused till I stopped playing. Maybe I played more than I was supposed to.

The reputation thing is very cool. It is hard to get started, but it reflects well how businesses work. Once you reach some reputation you can put whatever price you want and people will run to you to buy your stuff.

Well done!

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: Thank you for stress testing my game more than anyone! 😂 I didn’t expect people to play so long, and certainly didn’t test it rendering 22k items. No wonder your game froze!

comments.author: Sami Heikkinen
comments.text: Simple idea works very well. Great retro feel to it, although the texts were somewhat unreadable at times. I noticed myself playing “just one more round” for quite some time :)

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: Glad my game trapped you just a little bit ;) The font choice was unfortunately, as other reviewers commented on. I have released an improved version on github if you fancy checking out.

comments.author: Daniel Whiffing
comments.text: Nice fun and simple gameplay loop! For such a text heavy game, I think you should’ve refined the font rendering a bit more. The controls can also feel a bit sluggish. But it’s fun, and the graphics and music are charming. Good job!

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: Thank you! I regret not paying more attention to the font. It’s ugly, unreliable, and performs very badly, as well as being the number one complaint by players. I’m addressing those issues in a new release outside the competition. So thank you so much for the feedback.

comments.author: Joanna Jardine
comments.text:
Really fun game! The graphics were very neat and the gameplay was addicting, the music was a straight up bop. You’ve obviously executed this very well and I love the little clock counting down during each round. Only other comments would be that the font was a little hard to read (albeit this was already mentioned, though) and the controls were a bit stiff. I would’ve liked better use of the mouse so I would just be able to click on buttons. I did notice some lag too when clicking the ‘next’ button before the start of each round.

All round, well done! This is a neat game with lots of charm to it :-)

comments.author: Cameron Early
comments.text:
This game is so cool! I love the almost “stock market” idea where you can buy and sell at different prices. The jingle that plays when people are shopping is really catchy too. If I had any complaints, it would be that the font can be a bit illegible at times and the controls felt a bit laggy when naviagating menus.

All in all, really fun, addictive sim!

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
The control did not work immediately, you have to press it several times for it to finally work.

I liked the animation of the buyers’ shadows! Overall a good idea, played up to 4k+ thanks and congratulations!

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text:
I wish I could do a half stars because this game would get full marks in Graphics except for the fact that the font is very hard to read. The shadows of people are a nice touch (though at first I thought everyone was going by ignoring my stall).

In terms of gameplay it’s definitely up my street, I quite enjoyed this.

comments.author: Ania Kubów
comments.text:
Very much enjoyed this game! Who knew running a market stall could be so thrilling? The part that made this the most enjoyable for me is the ‘stock market’ approach with pricing your inventory. Having a peek at the source code is much advised.

The retro visuals were very cool and the colour palette well thought out. Some bits were a bit hard to read due to the font but that is an easy fix. I can see this game being adapted to teach young children about supply and demand if you want to take it that route and try monetize somehow.

Well done!

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: Thank you, super kind words! I feel a little bit more encouraged to continue developing it! The hard-to-read font was indeed the first thing I fixed post-comp.

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text:
I dig the art style! The peoples’ shadows are super cool, and I see you’ve updated the font in the post-compo version, I like the new one a lot more.

One thing I’d change is, please make the buttons clickable!

comments.author: Björn Ritzl
comments.text:
I enjoyed playing Market Street Tycoon. There is a certain charm to these trading games.

The font did not render very well in my browser (Chrome, macOS).

comments.author: Victor Debone
comments.text:
Hello! Thanks for your submission to js13k! I’m sure you’ve worked hard on your game, but I’m not aware of how much time you spent on it, so excuse me if things I mention were in your mind, but got left out in the rush of the month or just because you hit the 13kb limit :)

  • My first impression and understanding (before playing the game, only reading the description, screenshot and title):
    • The title and the cover promise me a “Lemonade Tycoon” game. From the description I think there’s a rhythm where buy stuff during the week and go to the market on weekends to sell.
  • Great work on:
    • Making a complete working game!
    • Have the UI interactions really nailed down! It felt really good to be buying and choosing the prices for my goods.
    • The audio and the music. The music on the market gave me a real sense of rhythm as in “This is a different environment”.
  • I liked that you:
    • Added those people into the market without adding them. I’m sure that using the shadows and letting my brain fill the gap was a great move over actually having graphics for actual people and spending a lot of time and bytes on them.
  • You could improve (again, I’m aware you might already know all the below):
    • The font choice was a bit too extreme. I see that you already fixed it on your branch, so great job updating it.
    • Without some fixed costs or risks to take besides the spoiling, there was not much strategy to be made other than just sticking with the game week by week. With the stuff that didn’t spoil, I didn’t have a rush to sell them cheap.
    • I see that you tried to add more mechanics like reputation, demand or unlocking products, but I lacked the feedback to make choices that improved those. And without using any of them I didn’t feel I was held back by the game.
  • I took some screenshots https://twitter.com/javascripl/status/1709346333742563764

api.name: Rock Feud
api.description:
Which army/team destroys all of the opposing team’s bricks will conquer the land! Since cannons during this era weren’t as advanced as the ones in the following centuries, accidents might happen during a battle. strategy and mostly luck might help your team win.

Controls

  • Y to start
  • Arrow keys to maneuver the target mark
  • A to fire cannon for the reds
  • L to fire cannon for the blues

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Nice simple game.

Not sure why you needed to switch between fire buttons for different teams but yet had the same arrow buttons to maneuver?

The ball didn’t always go the way you expect them to but it does say in the instructions that accidents happen.

comments.author: Luke Stapley
comments.text: Something is wrong with the shots firing. Started to shoot at themselves. It’s very raw, and I think it’s a great test, but you need to move towards making a new and more improved game. If this is your first game ever, great job on the effort!

comments.author: Rémi Vansteelandt
comments.text: Accidents are a nice touch but felt a bit unnatural. Would have been great to have an animation that said something went wrong, otherwise it feels like a bug.

comments.author: m-nakasato
comments.text: The idea of playing against each other is interesting. Do we need to lower the probability of making a missed shot? When the number of bricks ran low, it was difficult to hit the bullets, and it was difficult to decide whether to win or lose.

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text:
For a first attempt at making gaems in JS, this is neat!

Keep it up, there’s a lot to learn, but the stuff is exciting.

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
G’day!

Reds won 5-0 :) An interesting idea and its implementation. It was fun to shoot :).

I liked the idea of a gun that has its own minor malfunctions - I had fun :D

I think it would be even more fun if the enemy team moved dynamically to make it harder to hit, or if the cannon fired multiple cores sometimes, or even better if this game could be played with two players…!

Although in my opinion it would have been better to keep the sight from the previous round, I had to start from the same start all the time, it was difficult to adjust the sight when there were few units left. When I won for the reds - it was written that the blues won :). Several times it seemed that the nucleus had no collision with the army (rarely). I was also surprised that the initial text “Press Y to start” was an image, as I might have preferred text to the canvas (although there are times when an image is better than text, for example if the text contains a lot of styles, or its size will be smaller than the code that describes it).

In any case, this is a good start, thanks and congrats!

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: Ok this has the potential to be a really fun single-keyboard 2-player game. But Most of the times, the cannon balls shoot in the wrong direction. Is this what you meant in the description, that accidents happen? It seems to happen a bit too often. It would be nice if the position of the cannon was preserved between turns. Also, on a technical note, relying on the keypress event is unreliable because it depends on the users key-repetition settings. You should listen for key down and key up, and apply a constant movement to the cannon. Graphics-wise, the minimalist style is fine, but you could try to make the game less blurry but sizing the canvas correctly. It’s a nice idea with good potential if you want to continue it.

comments.author: Michael M.
comments.text: Unfortunately, I could not properly play: I have “search as you type” enabled in my browser, and you don’t call .preventDefault() on key events, so when I press a or l, the page search opens for me.

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Well done:

  • Interesting concept to use breakout and make it a versus battle

Feedback:

  • The shots firing in random directions most of the time makes it extremely difficult to hit anything, and is mostly frustrating rather than an interesting quirk
  • Since it’s turn based it would have been nice if the firing key was the same for both players like the move keys are
  • The wall in the middle combined with the max angle mean there’s only a small window of angles that will make it to the other player, which makes it even more difficult to aim for exact blocks

comments.author: Stiggstogg
comments.text: I can imagine that this might be a fun experience when you play it in local multiplayer. I think it is a very good idea to add some randomness to this simple game, as with that it adds some fun to it, eventhough the gameplay is not very deep. The controls I would adapt a bit. Either use the same fire button for both players or give them both their own controls: WASD for one player and Arrow buttons for the other so that they can leave the hands on the keyboard during playing. It is a pretty simple game and maybe not the most innovative, but I think you could have a bit of fun if you play it with two players locally. Contratulations to your entry.

comments.author: Taufik Sani
comments.text:
I think if you could add some audio (when bricks get destroyed, or the shooting of the cannons), that would help with the game being build as part of the theme.

Controls was a little difficult as the respective teams’ cannons kept friendly firing against their own blocks even thought it was aimed towards the opponent.

It just feels like pong and tetris graphics combined into one

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: It’s really weird, looks like you have velocity from previous army firinig, so your targeting become useless.

comments.author: Jeremy Burns
comments.text: Cool idea, but the “accidents might happen” mechanic makes it more difficult to play than fun I think. The projectile keeps its velocity after it breaks a brick, even when switching teams. So if red’s shot ends going down-left, then they will always shoot their own stack on the next turn. I thought it was a bug until I read the explanation text more carefully.

api.name: Harold is Heavy
api.description:
It’s the 13th century in the Floating Isles of Isildor, and the king is rounding up baby dragonkin everywhere. All but the improbably heavy Harold (because nobody could figure out how to move him).

Throw Harold’s weight around in this silly platformer and rescue your friends from the king’s ugly, boring crates! Arrow keys or WASD to move, spacebar to jump.

comments.author: Sebastian Dorn
comments.text:
Short and fun, with a lot of attention on the animations/effects. All that screen shake from jumping made me a little dizzy, though.

Does not work correctly in Firefox: The screen is tiny. But everything is fine in Chromium.

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text:
I know Harold is heavy and all but it would be nice to have the option of disabling screen shaking. Great wee game though :)

Your game worked just fine in FireFox on my Windows10 computer also :)

comments.author: Chris Glover
comments.text: Very polished and a new mechanic for platforming as far as I am aware. Not so much on theme, but a nice game

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text:
Harold’s heaviness made me a bit dizzy with that vertical screen shake, but overall it’s a cool game - graphics and music are very cool :)

(And I liked a lot the “speed run timer” at the end!)

comments.author: Emre Guneyler
comments.text: thanks very innovative and amazing. difficulty is great too, glad i could see the endgame.

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text:
I love the idea of crushing everything, the blood splatter is very satisfying. Also like the porcupine idea, forcing you to jump near them to flip them before smashing. Reminds of in Lion King where you roar at the porcupine. Usually platformers with heavy gravity are frustrating, but this one was still very well balanced and nice.

The problem for me was that there’s no interpolation on the camera. With so much fast vertical movement, you really really need to add interpolation. This way Harold can jump up and down quickly all he wants, the camera will smoothly move in his direction, rather than directly in time with him.

As it stands, it just kind of hurts my eyes stomach after playing too long. The screen shake doesn’t help this, but it’s really the camera that is the bigger culprit I believe. If this was adjusted, I’d love to play this more!

Really great idea and presentation though! A little camera tweak and I think it’s a wonderful game! The graphics, concept, mechanics, and level design are all really really great!

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Really cute character and animations, was great to see your design process with getting the heaviness of the character!

Love to play a good platformer and this one is really well made.

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
Really fun! Physics felt good and heavy, and crushing dudes was very satisfying!

One more thought on the camera (in case you ever make an expanded version and want to add some features). Would be cool if down arrow moved the camera down (so you could better plan your long jumps to push down unstable platforms).

comments.author: Christian Staudte
comments.text: What a cute game! The whole idea around Harold’s weight and how it influences your surrounding is really genius. I am only a bit sad there are so few levels.

comments.author: Crystal Parker
comments.text: I got stuck on the platform that was supposed to drop if jumped on from too high when I tried to crawl through the gap.
games: Journey to the East; Silk Road Adventure

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Enjoyed playing this game. The story, graphics and animations were great. I liked the variety of enemies. The music and sound effects sounded good and the level jump when you hit the ground was a nice touch.

The platform spacings were very unforgiving at times meaning lots of time spent restarting levels.

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text:
Landing a crushing blow on an enemy is deliciously satisfying. Audio also slaps! In general, I dig the art style.

Nice touch on hedgehogs that need to be flipped before they explode in a spectacle of blood and gore.

Of all js13k games, Harold would have benefited the most from gamepad support. Clearly a skill issue on my part, but controlling this living meteor as it drops menacingly from above onto a tiny floating block is hard.

comments.author: Allen Schmidt
comments.text: Really great little platformer! Audio and graphics feel cohesive and complete, and I liked all the little extra touches. It was definitely pretty short, but I enjoyed it. Well done!

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
G’day!

I liked the animation of shaking under the weight of the dragon! I also liked the hints that inform the player about new things and keep the attention. The graphics also look good, as does the music. I was also pleasantly surprised that at the end of the game my progress was played back - cool!

Although the theme is questionable, it was fun to play, thanks!

comments.author: Sami Heikkinen
comments.text: Very smooth platformer! Easy to pick up and get going. Once I managed to get stuck between ground and a moving platform (probably my own stupidity, but still). Looks and sounds really good. Especially I like how the hero really feels heavy.

comments.author: Marcin Walczak
comments.text:
Fun little platformer.

The ground bouncing on Harold’s impact is quite satisfying. In game tutorial is also well welcomed. Also the controls are really smooth.

As others mentioned, adding an option to disable screen shake would be a good idea.

For some reason on Firefox canvas is tiny (changing screen size and/or zooming in/out triggers canvas resize tho).

comments.author: Christer Kaitila
comments.text: amazingly polished and cool! also really hard!! this game has tons of personality. nice work!

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
The relation to the theme is a bit forced but this game is really cute and fun! The music and sfx are adorable. It’s too bad it’s so short! Hope you release more levels. The little details in the animations of the characters and platforms are quite polished and were not left unnoticed!

Apart from the odd CSS issue in firefox, I think the game would look so much better if you didn’t change the height and width of the canvas element. There are many visible glitches in the pixel art, e.g. gaps between pixels, because the pixel art is not “real pixels” but up-scaled. If you fix that and make the screen movements a bit softer, the game would look instantly better.

comments.author: Adrien Guéret
comments.text:
A nice game with a lot of details in animation. The “heavy” behavior of the character is a nice touch and provide some nice gameplay ideas. I unfortunately stucked myself below a platform without being able to reset the level, so I had to reload the whole game and my progress was not saved :’(

Despite this point, I really like this game, well done :)

comments.author: Joep van Duinen
comments.text:
What a great game! although I understand you want the game to be challenging I would have probably allowed for a double jump to make it a bit more forgiving in terms of game plays.  

Very cool art style and storyline, I would definitely share this with my friends, 10 out of 10!

comments.author: Tom Harris
comments.text: This was a really fun game with nice visuals and music!

api.name: Runekeeper
api.description:
Runekeeper is a turn-based strategy game where you play as a mage defending your lil’ tower.

This game has a small neural net embedded for character recognition (takes about 2.5kb). The entire game was made as an excuse to write a lightweight neural runtime… Therefore, the controls rely exclusively on drawing symbols to make moves :)

Controls

  • Draw (mouse or touch) symbols seen along the grid to command the Runestone to move.
  • Use the left half of screen to move up and down.
  • Use the right half of screen to move left and right.
  • Passing through a powerup orb will cast a spell when landing.
  • The powerup color dictates spell type.
  • The powerup shape dictates the attack pattern.

Defeat as many enemies as possible. Good luck!

comments.author: Daniel Cohen
comments.text: This is such a cool concept for input.

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: I love the concept and it seems to me incredible to have a neural network embedded in a 13k game! I really enjoyed playing, especially in my mobile. The sounds and the game of thrones music are pretty cool!

comments.author: Emre Guneyler
comments.text: game over and i wonder why controls don’t work. great i guess it’s working.

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text:
That was very interesting. The movement with drawing runes did take a short wile to get used to, but somehow did feel quite nice. The way the runes animated after drawing felt nicely polished.

High marks on Innovation and controls!

I did feel like there were not enough powerups (or every soldier getting through should only reduce one point) because as soon as they break through you are not able to get them after that.

comments.author: Chris Glover
comments.text: Cool idea and great technical achievement. I do feel that my enjoyment of the game would have been improved if I could simply click where I wanted to go as there is a fairly fun/deep tactical game here distinct from the drawing mechanic.
