Perhaps if I had to draw symbols accurately under time pressure, or the magic worked better the more accurately I drew, or something it would integrate better into the gameplay?

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Well done:

  • Amazing job getting drawing recognition into such a small file size
  • I like that the powerup symbol tells you how it will attack

Feedback:

  • I needed something more in order to kill enemies. If a powerup doesn’t spawn in the correct location or an enemy gets through there just wasn’t anything I could do to attack them, so they just killed me
  • While the input is innovative, it also is a bit frustrating when you think you drew the symbol and the game doesn’t recognize it
  • I didn’t realize that you could move more than one space at a time

comments.author: FoumartGames
comments.text: Cool concept and really nicely done. I found it hard to stay alive for long even if drawing the runes correctly. At some point I wanted to pause so I drew just a straight line “-” but it interpreted it as “w” and moved me away. The animations and the magic effects are super cool. For the character - I would prefer to move the mage and the violet hexagon thingy to be on top of the tower.

comments.author: Lakshman
comments.text: Neural network under 2.5kb. Awesome!!!

comments.author: Richard Davey
comments.text:
What a brilliant input mechanic! Draw the horizontal or vertical axis symbols to move the run around the grid. Landing on the different power-ups gives you a variety of attacks with which to defeat the oncoming knights. You can blast them away with fire, water, or electricity.

It’s quite amazing that it managed to recognise the input in such a small filesize game. I found it was accurate nearly all of the time, with just 2 symbols (the circle and the one below it) giving it trouble sometimes. When it got it wrong it was a bit frustrating as your rune could often just bounce on the spot, giving the knights another chance to move forwards.

Even so, this is a great game. The graphics are nice, the animation used well, the music boppy, and even nice little effects with the power-ups. I’m not entirely sure how ‘on theme’ it is, but regardless, it’s well worth playing.

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
A little more description of the magic, some balance/mechanics for generating charms personally, because randomness is very difficult to defend against and will be even better for me!

Interesting and good game, thank you and congratulations!

comments.author: Chris Subagio
comments.text:
Fantastic idea, and a surprisingly effective implementation of a recognizer in such a small footprint. Looked and sounded really sharp too.

On the gameplay front though, the recognizer failed often enough to impact enjoyment. I couldn’t get the knack of drawing the lightning symbol at all, maybe had a 50% success rate? I also couldn’t quite work out if every game was winnable, it seemed like I had to have a glyph to kill an enemy, and sometimes there really wasn’t a glyph in any viable position?

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text:
Nice graphics/animations, cool music, and satisfying sound effects. The controls are innovative, and I have no idea how you can recognize the symbols (with the neural network.) I will probably look in the source code. The only problem I had was that sometimes the input would recognize a different character than what I wrote (but my writing is horrible, so that was probably the case.)

It would be interesting to experiment with the input to cast spells instead of moving.

Overall a pretty good experience.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
First time I’ve seen a mark recognition game in js13k, and this one is pretty impressive.

I liked the way you can choose between horizontal and vertical movements depending on the side of the screen you draw.

I was able to nudge the enemies which seemed to effect their health, but they seemed to recover pretty quickly.

I guess using the spells is the way to go. But aiming to land on them had no effect, I seemed to have to pass them by.

comments.author: Björn Ritzl
comments.text: Nice little board/strategy game! The movement on the board was refreshing and the powerups were of a good variety! Well done.

comments.author: Frederic Kyung-jin Rezeau
comments.text:
10/10 for the technical feat of embedding a neural net. Love the controls, the original idea and the execution!

Using a different set of symbols for vertical and horizontal moves may have allowed smoother gameplay but I guess this may have been due to size contrainsts.

Great potential for iterations as well.

title: The First Horde
author: Santiago Zapata, Juan Carlos García and Esteban Martínez Villegas

api.name: The First Horde
api.description:
After having gained intelligence during the battle of Kalka River in may 1223, the Mongol army retreated.

The Kievan Rus felt at ease while Batu Khan planned an invasion like no other before.

It’s 1237 and it’s time to strike!

Take control of a commander of the Golden Horde under Batu Khan and destroy the supply lines of the Rus while avoiding them, so you can lay siege to their cities and rise as a powerful Mongol conqueror!

  • Move your horde using the arrow keys (or swipe in mobile)
  • Outmaneuver the Army units and raid the Supply lines.
  • Once all supply lines are destroyed, the cities will be vulnerable to raiding.
  • Forests will hinder your movement greatly.
  • Every time you raid a city, you will be given a choice to improve your horde, choose wisely.
  • You must conquer Rus before the end of the XIII Century.

comments.author: Crystal Parker
comments.text: Fun game, I like how the sound helps you know that you’re getting pummeled.
games: Journey to the East; Silk Road Adventure

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
interesting mechanic, it could benefit from some more “eyecandy” (Effects to visually see, that the army unit is fighting my horde, etc.)

i wonder, if it would be a better gameplay, when batu khan would only move, when i press a key, and stands still otherwise?

soundeffects and music are simple, but matching the action.

comments.author: Lakshman
comments.text: Controls in mobile could have been made more better, other than that the game play is really good. I really liked it. I enjoyed playing it.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Nice idea, fun to play, sometimes when you have to wait for the swords to move can be a pain.

Nice seen anything like this before as far as gameplay goes.

Nice music and sounds,

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: This was fun! The controls are a bit chaotic at first but then you start grasping them. The graphics are super minimal and it looks great. I guess it looks different depending on which emoji font you have, by the Linux emoji fit perfectly. The music is lovely as well as the sound effects. I ended up dying because I got too greedy. The game is actually very challenging. If I would improve this game, I would say better menus, fonts, dialogs etc would go a long way. But otherwise, the game is already fun.

comments.author: newman s lanier
comments.text: Okay - Dang! I loved everything about this game. The music really matched the intensity of the play. The Play was simple but engaging. the Graphics, likewise, were simple but understandable and fit the theme. Wow! I got to level 6 and would play again

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text:
It sees like the circle tie in to the size of your horde somehow, but at one point I only had 2 but I still had tons of circles flying about.

I would appreciate if you could defeat some of the armies because otherwise it becomes a very slow game of waiting until they move out of the way to strike at the supply lines.

I enjoyed the simple mechanic though and it keeps you entertained for quite a while.

comments.author: stxtus
comments.text: Love the beats in the music and the color scheme

comments.author: Tom Harris
comments.text: This was a cool and unique game. The music fits nicely with the theme. The basic gameplay of taking supply lines and raiding the city was fun but could do with some more interaction to keep it interesting - maybe we are able to fight the swords rather than having to wait and avoid them.

comments.author: Jeremy Burns
comments.text: Great concept, easy to learn, and the sounds suit the game well. At first I thought I could harm the army a little bit, and they completely destroyed me. It might be nice to be able to trade some amount of the horde off in a skirmish to get at a resource line faster. Overall well done!

comments.author: Björn Ritzl
comments.text: I found the game to be brutally hard. It is difficult to raid the supply lines without getting close to the army units. I found it a bit hard to tell how much impact the various upgrades had on my abilities. I would have liked to be able to move diagonally. Did you consider letting supply line raiding replenish some of the horde?

api.name: Exit the Castle
api.description:
13th Century Badger presents Exit the Castle.

You are trapped in the Sheriff of Nottingham’s castle and must survive against his knights to make your way out.

13 levels of top-down pixel art action.

Complete each level to choose an upgrade and improve your odds.

Controls

WASD or arrow keys to move, mouse to aim and click to shoot

Thanks to

  • MoDsama for the character sprites
  • Frank Force for the ZzFX sound generator
  • Timur Manyanov for tinyfont.js

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Being swamped near the spawn-point was annoying. Very well polished and presented.. Love the 13th century Badger intro, complete with the sound-track!

comments.author: Oliver Smith
comments.text:
Thanks for the positive and constructive feedback Cliff.

Point taken about being swamped near the spawn point. I didn’t have time to add pathfinding, so the knights only move toward the player on a vector. I added them in the line of sight on each level to make it challenging, which it wasn’t otherwise. Unfortunately I didn’t realise the browser I used for play testing and tuning was throttled so in the final submission the game runs faster than intended which makes it challenging in a way I didn’t anticipate.

Really happy to hear you enjoyed the intro, I had fun making it. Thanks for playing.

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text:
Instead of that intro, I’d rather have a more interesting ending :)

What I liked the least was being surrounded by enemies on your spawn point - most of my deaths came from this. But besides that, if you are quick enough to kill them all the leveling system feels great and the sprites are cool. Nice game :)

comments.author: Oliver Smith
comments.text:
Thanks for providing some thoughful feedback Almar. I created the intro before I was even sure I would make a game, so that is why more effort went there but I agree I should have been more creative with the ending.

The game runs faster than I intended which makes the enemy spawn camping worse than it should be. They do that to give them a chance because they only attack on a vector but with the speed the game runs it does make that annoying.

Glad you appreciated it anyway. Thanks for playing.

comments.author: Emre Guneyler
comments.text: you give me extra health, but i don’t feel it you know.

comments.author: Oliver Smith
comments.text: Thanks for the feedback Emre. Yes, extra health could be more and the bar should display it better. Thanks for playing.

comments.author: Misael Braga
comments.text: This game is really good, i spent a bunch of time playing. I could easily pay for a game like this.

comments.author: Oliver Smith
comments.text: Thanks for the wonderful feedback Misael. It really made my day to know you enjoyed playing my game and valued it so much. I enjoyed making it too. Thanks for playing.

comments.author: Aleksey Litvinov
comments.text:
Despite a simple idea, very addictive gameplay. Lovely art as well :) Some feedback: would be nice to have an option to shoot from keyboard, also was not feeling that extra health is valuable.

Good job! I liked your game :)

comments.author: Oliver Smith
comments.text:
Thanks for the considered feedback Aleksey. I was aiming to just keep players engaged but I will sure settle for addictive. An option to shoot using the keyboard is a good suggestion. The health perk probably isn’t enough for the balance of the game and it doesn’t help that the bar scales to the same width whatever your maximum heath is so it isn’t obvious visually that you have gained anything. That is something I would definitely want to fix another time.

I am happy you liked it anyway. Thanks for playing.

comments.author: Richard Davey
comments.text:
Love the intro! This game is highly polished. The sprites are cute and well drawn, the sound effects fun, and it’s nice and juicy. Move your Robin Hood character around the level and use the mouse to fire your bow. Shoot all the guards, and the last one will drop a key for the exit.

I played this game several times and felt I could have gotten further had my mouse finger not been hurting so much! You have to press it for every arrow, and the guards take a lot of arrows. Even so, dispatching them is great fun, and I love how, between levels, you can choose to get extra health, improve the accuracy of your shot, or even get twin shots.

Being swarmed immediately at the start of the later levels is easily the thing that causes most deaths. A little affordance here would have gone a long way, along with autofire for oldies like me :). Otherwise, it is a really great little game.

comments.author: Oliver Smith
comments.text:
Thanks for the detailed feedback Richard. So pleased you liked the intro, I enjoyed making it. I could have done with your description for the game you summed it up well. It runs faster than I intended unfortunately and that has affected the gameplay. Autofire would be a nice idea, Robotron style. I wanted to include increased fire rate as another perk which might also have helped but didn’t manage it.

Glad you enjoyed it despite the challenge. Thanks for playing.

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text:
The sprites are great! Thanks for mentioning the artist, I’m in love with her work.

The intro is brilliant, and the core gameplay loop is solid. I feel like the level design could use more variety, and maybe a different enemy type could’ve been nice (since the guard always run towards you, the accuracy perk gets almost no use — if one of the enemies was e.g. mage, who always runs away from you and shoots projectiles from a distance, you’d want to invest in accuracy or chase them into corners or something).

All in all it’s a lovely game! I want this on mobile, with like 1000 levels, so I can pew pew during a commute.

comments.author: Oliver Smith
comments.text:
Thanks for the thoughful feedback Mark. I like the character sprites too. I wasn’t satisfied with what I had done for them and these are exactly what I was after and they fit in well with everything else I had done. Credit where it is due.

It’s great to know you liked the intro. It is what I did first and it motivated me to create a game to go with it. A different enemy type with projectiles would be a good idea to mix things up.

Getting the game to work well on mobile sounds like an interesting challenge. I hope I will be up to trying that.

It is really validating that you had fun with it. Thanks for playing.

comments.author: Johan Åhlgren
comments.text: Very difficult game and at times the controls were a bit unforgiving (having to move back and forth a while just to enter a corridor). Very polished though, with a lovely retro feel. Well done!

comments.author: Oliver Smith
comments.text:
Thanks for taking the time to provide some feedback Johan. I’m sorry you found it difficult. I was quite upset to realise the version I had submitted was running too fast which disrupts the gameplay balance I had worked on. I think the problem you had with the corridor is because of the perspective style I chose. I didn’t make the edge of the top walls clear enough for the style of game.

I am pleased you appreciated the effort to make a polished product and got the retro feel that I was after. Thanks for playing.

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text:
I enjoyed playing this game. The controls were good, the graphics, animations and sound effects are superb. The theme is also well interpreted. I like how the arrows have a little randomness to the aim. It makes the game more enjoyable and slightly more challenging, which is good. It would be cool to receive 1 bonus perk after each death, making the game more replayable.

I’m not sure what eagle eye means, is it better aim?

The only thing I’m missing is some music

comments.author: Oliver Smith
comments.text:
Thanks for the positive feedback John. The bonus perk for replays is a good idea, definitely a consideration for the future.

Eagle eye is indeed better aim, it reduces the randomness a bit more each time you select it. I should have just called it ‘aim’ or ‘accuracy’.

I would really like to have done some music. I spent too much time on the intro and started the whole thing too late but would prioritise it more another time.

It is really rewarding how much you appreciated the game. Thanks for playing.

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
It would be nice to be able to skip the splash screen. Also, I think it would be good if there were also archers among the opponents, then it would be more difficult to play in the last rounds, otherwise it becomes a bit boring when you have three arrows flying in all directions now :D. It would seem to expand the paths between the walls a little - otherwise I often got stuck because of the belly :D.

Maybe it would be even more fun if you could shoot arrows that could bounce off the wall once :).

Although the topic does not really resonate with the competition topic, overall a good game, passed and moved on, thanks and congratulations!

comments.author: Oliver Smith
comments.text:
Thanks for the feedback and suggestions Roman. I’m sorry you want to skip the splash screen but agree it should have been an option. Opponents firing projectiles is a good idea to add some variety. I agree more work could be done to make the corridors easier to navigate too.

I was working on a ricochet option for the arrows but it was complicated by the fact that unlike a ball, for example, they need to be rotated appropriately too and that was enough to make me abandon it because of time. Definitely something that would be nice to add though.

The topic is pretty well anchored in 13th century English folklore but maybe I didn’t make enough of that.

I am pleased you enjoyed it. Thanks for playing.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Graphics are really nice, sounds also. All works well but the HP is not enough and no matter how hard I try I get hammered and getting to level 6 is so hard.

Some levels you start in a position where you cannot avoid damage.

comments.author: Oliver Smith
comments.text: Thanks for the considered feedback Ryan. Good to know you liked the graphics and sound. I agree the HP is not enough as the game plays. The submitted version runs faster than I intended so the difficulty level doesn’t work in quite the way I wanted. Sadly I couldn’t start working on this competition entry sooner and so ran out of time for thorough testing. Those are the breaks I guess but I’m glad you got far enough to appreciate it. If you can get past level six then more significant upgrades should make it easier from there. Thanks for playing.

comments.author: Marcin Walczak
comments.text:
Well made and quite enjoyable game. Love the graphics (good choice of the artist). Gameplay wise is polished and fun, although can be a little but challenging.

Controls are smooth, but spamming arrows with mouse can put a strain your finger.

Getting killed on spawn can be a little bit annoying. You could move on some levels enemies a little bit further from player’s spawn point to give more time to react.

13th century badger… Is that that a “Robin Hood: Men in Tights” reference?

comments.author: Oliver Smith
comments.text:
Thanks for your thoughts and feedback Marcin. Good to know you liked the graphics, most of them are my own but I needed help with the characters so credit where it is due for that.

I agree the game is a bit challenging, it runs faster than I intended and that affects the gameplay.

I hadn’t thought of Men in Tights but it is a similar humour to Mel Brooks for sure. I think I remember Monty Python having a “20th Century Frog” intro which was probably the influence.

Glad you enjoyed it and thanks for playing.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Love the game.

Graphics are nice and work well with the theme. Sound effects are good.

The enemies are pretty hard to defeat especially when they come in large groups. So the power ups offered between levels are essential.

comments.author: Oliver Smith
comments.text:
Thanks for the positive feedback Jasper. I am really happy to know you enjoyed my game. It runs a bit faster than intended so it is quite challenging but the power ups do help and make it possible to complete if you can be quick enough.

Glad you appreciated it. Thanks for playing.

comments.author: Isaac Benitez
comments.text: Congrats! The graphics are pretty cool! The game is a bit too difficult, but fun. I would probably make the enemies a bit slower. Or give more space to the rooms.

comments.author: Oliver Smith
comments.text: Thanks for providing some feedback Isaac. The game does run a bit faster than I intended which has unbalanced the difficulty. Good to hear you found it fun anyway. Thanks for playing.

comments.author: Magnus Lindh
comments.text: I like the Robin Hood theme. It is unfortunately almost impossible to play with a touch pad. I guess it could be a good mobile game with some more variation in the enemy types. Overall a solid entry.

comments.author: Oliver Smith
comments.text: Thanks for the feedback Magnus. Glad to hear you got the theme and appreciated it. I don’t have a trackpad and can only imagine how difficult that would be. Another time I would want to prioritise making it playable on more devices and mobile would very much be an ambition too. Thanks for playing.

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: I like the intro music :) The game is very well done and it is really fun to play. The pixel art is also awesome., this is a nice game

comments.author: Oliver Smith
comments.text: Thanks or the feedback Homer. Great to hear you liked the intro. I enjoyed putting that together. It is really motivating how much you appreciated the game. Thanks for playing.

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
Pretty difficult at the beginning, but after i got the triple-shot, and legendary damage the gameplay turns into reverse-bullet-hell. poor NPCs :-)

Very nice graphics, and gameplay. Tried it multilpe times, only beat it once. Still, very entertaining!

comments.author: Oliver Smith
comments.text: Thanks for the feedback Christoph. The game is more difficult than I intended really but I hoped the last levels would be a fun blast anyway. Congrats on beating it. I’m really pleased to hear you found it entertaining. Thanks for playing.

comments.author: Noël Meudec
comments.text:
What I liked:

  • The introduction!
  • The power-ups
  • The cute graphics
  • The controls
  • The sounds effects

What I liked less:

  • The enemy AI. It is fairly easy to use walls to our advantage.

Overall, it is a really good game. I had a fun time finishing it. The last levels are not really the hardest one as the enemies have a hard time with walls.

comments.author: Oliver Smith
comments.text: Thanks for the feedback Noël. Good job on finishing it and great to hear it was fun. The enemies just attack on a vector. I would have liked to add pathfinding which would have made it more interesting but great that you enjoyed it anyway. Thanks for playing.

comments.author: Benjamin Boyd Austin
comments.text:
Very nice! I played through it several times. Think I made it to Level 11

Graphics are great! Intro made me laugh out loud! Upgrades were a nice touch!

I was getting caught on the walls a bit; not a bug I don’t think, just that it’s hard to see the color difference between the “wall” (darkish purple) and the “south-facing wall” (darkerish purple). Maybe the contrast could have been increased a bit?

But overall really fun game. I’m going to try again later and see if I can beat it!

comments.author: Oliver Smith
comments.text:
Thanks for the feedback Benjamin. Really pleased to hear you enjoyed the intro, I had fun making it. I agree the graphics for the walls make it hard to see where the collision point is. Some more contrast would help. I need to work on how improve that for this style of game.

Glad you had fun with it enough to come back for more to try to beat it. It is definitely possible if you can be fast enough and with the right strategy. Thanks for playing.

api.name: The Mystery at Glamis Castle
api.description:
It is 1290 Scotland, and following the death of Queen Margaret there was no clear successor (the Great Cause). Witches placed a spell on Glamis Castle and whoever could solve the puzzles and enter will claim the kingdom.

Use a WebXR Pointer or WASD/Mouse to move and interact.

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text: The puzzles with flowers were cool!

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text: Nicely rounded game. Good buildup of puzzles quite elaborate with good progression. Solid playing field with different areas and things to look at. Works on desktop and Quest.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text: Looks great and nice puzzles, played on PC but will test out on a headset also.

comments.author: FoumartGames
comments.text: Impressive graphics and animations. Cool puzzles as well - had me amused for a while :)

comments.author: Nathan Long
comments.text: Quite good! Clever puzzles. Felt very much like an escape room. Pretty impressive this is below 13k!

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text:
Well, short, but really interesting puzzle game. Type of the puzzles are kinda the same, but still interesting.

Good graphics

Great job!

comments.author: Sébastien Vandenberghe
comments.text:
Puzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzlessss !!!!!

Thanks a lot for creating and proposing this game. I loved the various puzzles within the game. The difficulty is progressing up nicely as the games goes forward.

The Graphics are good and immersive and the theme respected. You also successfully added audio and a final screen so that you have a full game.

This is overall a success !!!

comments.author: Jupiter Hadley
comments.text: Wow what an amazing entry! I really like the graphic style and the puzzles. That first code, there are so many ways to “solve” it and I think that makes it so fun.

comments.author: Fernando Serrano Garcia
comments.text: Very original puzzles! I spent a good amount of time playing with them. The controls are also very intuitive, and I really appreciate the locomotion control and the ability to rotate the camera with the thumbstick. I would definitely love to see more puzzles in the future. Great job!

api.name: Medieval Commerce
api.description:
Welcome to Veliky Novgorod in 1260.

You play as a trader who tries to earn enough money to pay back to Mongol tax-collectors by selling salt and grain. Your goal is to earn 200 silver for the upcoming round to satisfy mongols.

Good luck.

comments.author: Elena Litvinova
comments.text: Wow, wow, wowwwww! I so love it, can’t stop while playing.

comments.author: Isaac Benitez
comments.text: Pretty cool pixel art! I love the color pallet and the characters. The bumping of the characters when they hit an object is pretty cool. Things that could be better are: would be nice to have music, the collision detection is not great (I was walking on water), and it is a bit difficult to know what to do, and where to place the jars

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
The graphics are really nice and the cpu moves around well, although you can block them with the pots :D

Simple game all functions well.

comments.author: Chris Subagio
comments.text:
Gorgeous pixel art!

Took me a long second to understand what to do, but eventually worked out that the wizard always picks up salt, and the princess always wants grain, so it’s just a matter of organizing so that you can drop those on the tables ASAP. The game is a little simple, but it’s executed well, and quite satisfying to beat!

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
The game looks great, the pixel art is cute.

Unfortunately, the gameplay is not at all challenging. Always the same girl asks for grain in the top counter, and the wizard asks for salt in the bottom counter. Always at the same time. Once you understand this, it’s a matter of doing the same thing a dozen times until you reach 200 silver. There also this issue where the canvas doesn’t fit the window for some reason.

The game has immense potential, but right now it’s not exactly fun.

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text:
I found that you could keep dropping salt and grain on top of each other, so I’d just drop salt and grain termitently on each side of the table and the wizard and the princess could always pick something up.

Controls are super easy to use, and the gameplay is quite self-explanatory.

I thought the game would’ve been a bit more immersive with a few sounds (like pickup/drop item).

Good effort!

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
Love the Kenney graphics. The game idea is good and the level designed worked well.

Wasn’t sure what the difference was between the orange and brown sacks or barrels? Or what I was meant to be doing with them.

Or what the stand on the path was for, or even why the lady and the wizard stopped for a bit and sometimes left stuff and sometimes took stuff.

Would love to play again, but perhaps need more instructions.

I wasn’t able to go diagonally, and some of the buildings could be walked through.

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text:
The graphics are nice and cohesive.

I don’t know what I’m supposed to to, but I think I need to move the bag of coins (I think they are bag of coins) outside of the castle.

Adding some sound effects and music to the game, and help define a clear goal, I think you have something that can be addictive.

comments.author: Joep van Duinen
comments.text:
Very nice game with a great implementation of the theme! The pixel art is also very nice! Gives the game a nice, old-timey aesthetic.

The gameplay is good as well, it keeps a steady pace and rounds are not too long. The time to help out a customer is very short however, which makes it quite difficult to satisfy the customers order. It would be nice if the customers already show what they want to buy when coming down the bridge, that way you have some extra time to get the product from your store for them.

All in all though, very nice experience! Well done!

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: Well, looks more like prototype, there are no so many actions and options, only two clients in the same time. But playable.

comments.author: Mark Vasilkov
comments.text: Reminds me of that mission in Mafia 2 where you carry the boxes around.

comments.author: Jedidiah Weller
comments.text:
Hi there,

Great job on this submission! The graphics were really well done and I could tell it was thought out. Here’s my feedback and suggestions:

  1. There was a serious UI issue where the top part of screen is cut-off. My guess is it’s because the “Play” window on js13games is overlapping with the top of the screen, but this is a good reason to add padding on the UI of your window. You might also want to introduce Full screen mode. I wasn’t able to see what’s at the top
  2. I really enjoyed the ‘bounce’ that you added to the interactions - that gave it a unique feel and bonus points!
  3. I’d suggest adding more description/story to the gameplay - I think you could really take this somewhere with extra story and extra transitions or character dynamics.
  4. Music/Sound - definitely needed here to make it truly engaging!
  5. Bonus points for utilizing typescript and eslint!
  6. Bonus points for naming convention and file organization
  7. Don’t forget to add a license file!

Overall, good submission - keep up the good work and hit me up on twitter @jedihacks for any discussion!

comments.author: Victor Debone
comments.text:
Hello! Thanks for your submission to js13k! I’m sure you’ve worked hard on your game, but I’m not aware of how much time you spent on it, so excuse me if things I mention were in your mind, but got left out in the rush of the month or just because you hit the 13kb limit :)

  • My first impression and understanding (before playing the game, only reading the description, screenshot and title):
    • Without knowing anything, great look at the cover art, but it looks like those packs? Given the title and the tiled aspect of the cover art, it feels like I’m going to be building my shop or some freedom in that aspect.
    • After I played, I checked the source and yes (no judging), the pack was from Kenney, great choice!
    • Where is that guy taking all the sacks from?? Is there a train station or something left to the screen? :D
  • Great work on:
    • Making a complete working game!
  • I liked that you:
    • Was focused in giving a more systemic play to the player.
    • Got a game focused on the logistic problem of organizing the shop stock, which usually in games are magically behind the counter.
  • You could improve (again, I’m aware you might already know all the below):
    • While I was playing the game, I was not necessarily tracking the amount of steel or fame I had. As both customers were coming in steady pace, sitting at the same place and buying the same thing, I was only focused that they had the grain and the salt on that table at all times. I don’t think varying these would make it necessarily more fun, as I enjoyed that I needed to keep the stock organized so I didn’t got locked and had easy access to the next thing.
    • I could see you doubling down on the sacks management more and more. Different sizes, more items, stacking of sacks, some cooldown time where you have time to organize the shop.

api.name: The Silk Trail
api.description:
Get to the end of The Silk Trail and make as much money as you can along the way.

Throw your items at incoming bandits to survive by pressing the left side of the screen.

Press once to start throwing, again to confirm angle, then once more to confirm power and release.

You can swap to the next item by tapping yourself on the right side of the screen.

If you have no item in your hand, you can tap the left side of the screen to catch incoming items.

Tips

  • When you make it to a new city, make sure to stock up on weapons and buy low and sell high!
  • You move faster the lighter you are, so don’t get greedy! Throw heavy things away to make it to the next city.
  • There is an indicator at the top showing your progress to the next city.
  • You can use the arrows at the bottom to see the full inventory.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: This game is really hard to play. I felt like the three-click action wasn’t very intuitive or easy to grasp. The shop is a little confusing too. How am I earning money? The music and sfx were really nice and the graphics were cute despite their simplicity.

comments.author: Daniel Whiffing
comments.text: Thanks for playing! I struggled to make the game intuitive as I ended up adding too many systems and not having enough time to bring them all together clearly. I also agree the throwing system could feel better. I’m glad you enjoyed the art and audio though!

comments.author: Grav
comments.text:
It’s a very good entry once you understand it. To anyone who is a bit overwhelmed, the goal of this game is not only to reach the end, but make as much money as you can. Weight of items plays a huge role, it determines how far you can throw the item, and how quickly enemies catch up with your cart. You can actually finish the game without facing any enemies if you carry nothing, but you will “fail” in that you made no money.

This game’s systems are very well integrated I think, as you need to make decisions on how many enemies you want to face vs how much money you want to make vs how far you think you will need to throw items based on how many enemies will catch up to you. Good job!

comments.author: Daniel Whiffing
comments.text: Wow thank you for the insightful review! I was disappointed that I couldn’t get the game feeling more fun, but you understand the design of my game perfectly! I just needed to put more time into refining those systems, and I think there could be a cool little gameplay loop here. I really appreciate your time and kind words.

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Well Done:

  • The graphics were nice. The people and camel sprites looked nice
  • Music was nice too

Feedback:

  • For some reason everything sold and bought for 1 gold. Didn’t seem to be consistent either as some times items were 1 gold but then others they cost the price listed. My first stop I was able to buy the entire shop of items
  • Not sure if doing so broke the game but after I cleared the shop of all it’s items the 2nd leg of the journey was broken. The wheels were moving super fast and enemies came in super fast (almost like 2x to 3x animation speed). The level kept going on too and didn’t seem to end. There was no progress indicator either
  • The combat system was unintuitive. Especially at first where clicking the left side of the screen doesn’t do anything as the first item isn’t equipped. Had to click the right side first and then the left side would allow me to aim and such
  • I wasn’t sure what the different colors of the items meant
  • I wasn’t sure how to buy items as clicking on them didn’t seem to do anything. Took awhile to figure out that clicking on them made them selected (bold) and then I could click the buy button at the bottom

comments.author: Daniel Whiffing
comments.text:
Thanks for playing my game and thanks for making Kontra!

I’m glad you liked the visuals and music. I agree the game itself is unfortunately broken and the gameplay unintuitive. Had less time than I expected closer to the end of the jam.

comments.author: Joanna Jardine
comments.text: Pretty fun game! Sound effects and audio were nice, with simple graphics. Would’ve loved seeing more polish on it, but nonetheless a great entry! Well done :-)

comments.author: Stiggstogg
comments.text: Nice game. I really like the music and the trade-off decisions you had to make between weight and the weapons. I think it needs some more balancing. If I did not buy anything I was always faster than anybody and never had to engage in a fight. For desktop I would have also added some keyboard mechanics. I think the tapping on the screen works very well for mobile, but I is a bit cumbersome with the mouse, especially if you have a large screen. One small detail: I would place the “Next” button (between cities when you buy stuff) always on the left side, this is more intuitive. Anyway, I really liked the game and with a bit of tweaking it will be very good!

comments.author: Taufik Sani
comments.text:
I like the player progression within the game - buy/sell weapons on the marketplace.

The game has a bug where if you throw the purchased emerald, your screen becomes this and game gets stuck: https://ibb.co/dgh7ssL (Uploaded image on ImgBB so you can look into it)

Interesting choice with the audio - doesn’t feel 13th century, feels very “space” like.

Controls UI can be refined to make it easier for the player to aim.

comments.author: Tom Harris
comments.text: I liked the music but it didn’t feel like it fit the setting of the game. It wasn’t always clear how things worked nor the most intuitive, but it seems like there is some complexity to it. I had some issue where the enemies were never reaching me and the level was finishing without any battle occurring.

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: i can see a bright future for this if shaped well and polished, keep working till you reach the point where it be best version of itself

api.name: Shikken
api.description:
Shikken was the title of the supreme leader of Japan during the 13th century. When the Mongols demanded that Japan bend the knee, the shikken of the ruling Hōjō clan commanded the forces that prepared for the invasion, and were ultimately successful in keeping Japan free.

Take control of the shikken’s defenders in this minimalist-styled, Zen-like, city-builder resource management game. Expand your network of buildings, upgrade them to produce resources, and manage the growing workforce. Pillaging Mongol invasions will be ever present until two divine winds (“kamikaze”) come to the rescue.

How to play

Tap or click and drag to move the background map around. Tap a crossroad node or building, tap “Upgrade”, select an upgrade that you can afford. Tap “Jobs” and reassign your people represented by moving circles of different colors. Many buildings will take some resources as an input, and after workers work there for some time, they will output something else. You’ll need carriers to transport goods from place to place, and you’ll need to turn buildings on and off to prioritize certain production. To unlock “Kamikaze” build rice farms and shrines.

comments.author: Leo Kuo
comments.text: I love minimalist-styled graphics. Also, the concept of connection is great and innovative! But it’s a little bit confused what to do in the game. It will be better to provide some in-game tutorial!

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text: I really like this settlers-gameplay and the clean look of it. a tutorial would be fine, it took awhile until i figured out how to play it.

comments.author: Chris Subagio
comments.text:
This was great! I think the look was pretty successful, minimalist but with enough character from the palette and the layout to invoke a Japanese scroll. Audio was missing, but I ended up playing a while so I cheated and played some koto music in the background.

I did play until I could fire the kamikaze, but I’m not sure if it crashed right after. I’m not sure I actually understood enough about how nodes and carriers worked to actually optimize, but eventually there was just enough stuff going on that the invaders couldn’t overwhelm me anymore. Had a good time playing that far!

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Well done:

  • Interesting gameplay having to expand the nodes and build your resources
  • Fun watching all the little units and resources move around

Feedback:

  • Took awhile to figure out what I was suppose to do
  • The “expand” option, while interesting, could lead to stacking everything as close together as possible to reduce travel time
  • Wish I had a way to prioritize jobs. Often I would have a grain field not producing because workers were on other locations. Wanted to be able to say “this location is #1 important, always work it.” Especially important after an attack way and need to reboot my workers
  • I was never sure if upgrading a location would increase it’s production or just it’s capacity
  • It was difficult to keep track of how much resources I was generating and what was dependent on those resources so I could know if I needed to build more of something

comments.author: Grav
comments.text:
Great game, like others it took me quite a while to understand. I was struggling until I started building storehouse sor warehouses, which I didn’t realize were integral to organization. The graphics are good, I liked the way it was all on a book and you scrolled over to the game. simple but effective, not overcomplex. Though, a lot of the structures were difficult to tell apart, and I had a bit of trouble understanding which resource was which early in the game.

I spent quite a while playing it, its a very good game once you understand it. I managed to send the second Kamikaze, which pauses the game and puts a peace symbol in the top left - it didn’t crash for me but I can see how someone might get confused at that part. Congratulations!

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text: Very polished, I liked the book style. I feel centering the first node would had made it more interesting. Or adding some random nodes in the initial map. Nice work.

comments.author: Matt Hackett
comments.text:
Minimalist, polished, and stylized little simulation game.

The “drag-left” interface with the gameplay to the left of the title area was unconventional and therefore confusing to me. It took some time to understand what the game was asking of me before I could see what was happening.

The goal / progression was not clear to me but the game is on theme and well crafted. Good job!

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
I had so much fun with this game. At first it was really frustrating because I just couldn’t grow enough soldiers to fight the enemies without losing 90% of my population, but eventually i was able to have a nice city with a big surplus of resources. Unfortunately, when I was reaching 50 people, the game became unbearably slow and I had to stop. On my phone it was too slow to play from the start and panning the map triggered the page refresh gesture.

Very simple but effective design. I really liked the soft colors. Some things were annoying, like how the stock said “wood, wood, wood, wood, wood” instead of “wood x 5” which made things a bit confusing later in the game when i had dozens of items. The game was a bit confusing to understand at first, but I eventually got the handle of things.

I would have liked a way to know how much of each item I have in total. It’s very hard to distinguish between what is a brown square or a brown triangle or a brown triangle but pointing up.

I didn’t like having to move the screen around all the time. That’s a nice feature on mobile, but on desktop it’s not necessary. Let me just see the whole map or zoom out. The UI in general could be more ergonomic.

I didn’t understand the purpose of the priest and I didn’t see an option to build shrines.

comments.author: Ben Fox
comments.text:
I really like the visuals, minimalist style. There’s enough complexity with various upgrades and such that it really has a lot of playability! The complexity of everything tho is pretty nicely hidden behind the relatively simple interface.

Only thing I wanted was some sort of overview of all the resources I had available.

A few things it took be a while to figure out:

  • the on/off toggle (and it seemed like the transporters still took things to the “off” buildings)
  • Building new roads between buildings
  • New resource types that came from Upgrades

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text:
Took me a little bit to figure out what to do here, but “city-builder resource management game” was enough to keep me hooked until I figured it out.

Overall I love the graphical style (and it does look Zen due to the minimalistic feel), however I would definitely not class this as Zen in its gameplay (and that’s not a bad thing at all), I was always conscious of that timer on the top left.

I think the UI is a little too big for the game, at times it took up more than half of the screen unnecessarily.

If I have more workers than worker-places they get confused and abandon on of the places and congregate at one place though - not sure what caused that behaviour.

comments.author: Noël Meudec
comments.text:
What I liked:

  • The game mechanic is good.
  • I enjoyed building my village. The freedom of building it the way I want is cool.
  • Simple but efficient graphics.
  • Battles (and strategy to stay alive)!

What I liked less:

  • Sometimes the AI is not acting the way I want it to.
  • I spent most of the time waiting for things to happen and I didn’t have much to do during those phases.
  • No music.

Overall, it is a solid strategy game. I had a fun time building my village and defending it. Thanks!

api.name: Kukulkan
api.description:
According to historical and mythological records, during the 13th century in America, the fall of Chichén Itzá - the last of the great Maya cities - took place. In the center of Chichén Itzá stood the main temple of Kukulcan, the feathered serpent. Centuries of glory turned the spiritual incarnation of Kukulcan residing in the temple into a powerful serpent, but as the city was abandoned, the lack of faithful followers quickly caused it to lose its power. With an irreversible destiny and no more followers, Kukulkan wants to escape before disappearing, but to achieve this, it must confront images of the new gods and artifacts of the upcoming civilizations.

Kukulkan can move using WASD or the arrow keys. To shoot, you can use the “I” or “Z” key.

The order of the bosses is random, and depending on the time of day, the boss’s difficulty varies, being easier during the day and more challenging at night. Try all permutations of bosses and times of the day so everybody knows you’ve won the game!

Many (MANY!) things happened this month, so it’s incredible that I was able to upload this in the state it’s in. Some things that were left out include an upgrade system, two more bosses, better patterns, music, and improved graphics.

comments.author: Rob Louie
comments.text:
Love the graphics and the concept, I’m a huge fan of old shmups like Mars Matrix, Ikaruga, etc. Needs the ability to fire repeatedly by just holding the fire key, because smashing fire really fast gets old pretty quickly. The “standard” for this type of game is hold to fire, but while firing you move a bit more slowly, so the player has to balance holding fire + less mobility, with not shooting but being able to dodge better.

I think having that plus some of the other thing planned would have made this amazing. As it stands, it has a great presentation and great visuals. If you develop it further after the competition I’d definitely play a more polished version a lot more!

comments.author: Miguel Ángel Pérez Martínez
comments.text: Thanks! I was inspired by cave shooters! I wanted to do auto fire on hold but for that I needed a lot more of bullets on screen to feel good. I was going to do that after adding 2d billboards as I can’t have hundreds of 3d objects on screen (The sdfs are prerendered but marching them in real time is still slow, and I wanted it to run well on my low end integrated GPU). But, life got in the way and I had to abandon it in the state it was. I had two more bosses planned and the idea was to be able to choose your own path and choose upgrades after each boss. I even got left almost 2KiB to work with! Thanks a lot for your feed back and for playing it! I wanted to play yours but my poor gpu is not enough and I didn’t want to vote without being able to properly test it. Will try to get access to a better computer!

comments.author: Emre Guneyler
comments.text: a little hard to control first try couldnt shoot anything, second time was better but couldn’t kill anything.

comments.author: Miguel Ángel Pérez Martínez
comments.text: Sorry to hear that! The game needs a lot of tweaking, I had to abandon it and post it as it was. Thanks for playing it and leaving a comment!

comments.author: Samuel van Egmond
comments.text:
Overall a great game. Graphics are very nice with 3D animations an all. Quite different set of bosses which gave it a nice and varied feel.

No negative remarks for this one!

comments.author: Miguel Ángel Pérez Martínez
comments.text: Thank you for trying it and leaving a comment!!

comments.author: ch!k0
comments.text: i remember the game galatica while playing. it was very fun. the graphics and the sounds were on point too. though the intro texts would have been nicer if it included which keys were which. if i haven’t read the games description i wouldn’t know which key(s) is the one for shooting. also a much readable font would have been nicer-though i think you were sticking to the mayan theme-which was a nice touch but just a tad bit difficult to read :)

comments.author: Miguel Ángel Pérez Martínez
comments.text: Thanks! I agree with the font. I didn’t want to use a generic one from the browser so I went with this option as I was able to do it with very few bytes using a couple of sticks. Showing the inputs in game is always a problem for me, I don’t know where to put them while looking good. Also I wanted to have more than one way of controlling them for right-handed / left-handed usage so that made things more difficult in terms of screen real-state. Will have to be more creative next year with this! Thanks for playing and leaving feedback!

comments.author: Christian Staudte
comments.text: I am really impressed with the complex bosses and the 3D graphics. To me the bosses were extremely difficult. Some upgrades and a scoring system would have been nice

comments.author: Miguel Ángel Pérez Martínez
comments.text: Thank you! The bosses are not super tuned and leaning towards difficult. Depending the time of the day is the difficulty of the boss so I wanted to tweak them to be pretty easy at the beginning of the day and pretty difficult at the end for more replayability. I didn’t have the time so they are not as fined tuned as I would like. Upgrades were also on the roadmap (There is even a lot of code for them inside) but didn’t had the time to finish it. Thanks for the feedback!

comments.author: Marcin Walczak
comments.text:
A pretty good looking shmup.

I like game’s visual style and serpent’s animation (animated text is also a nice touch).

Different bosses gives a nice variety of gameplay.

comments.author: Clément Pasteau
comments.text:
Hey there!

Super impressive! I wasn’t expecting to see some 3D so really well done. That feeling of playing a mix of strategy & action, like mini-games, really enjoyable.

I do miss a bit of music to tag along, that would have made a big difference :)

Great that you thought about playing with wasd or arrow keys, it’s really important for those who don’t have a qwerty :) Last feedback, I did find the font a bit hard to read, a bit too much maybe!

Great job overall!

comments.author: Magnus Lindh
comments.text:
Super impressive 3d snake shoot’em up! The gameplay is however very repetitive and the bosses are too hard. Also the sound effects could be more elaborate. Maybe you could try mutating the sound effects slightly each time they are played?

Small remark: The fractal landscape and the clouds are very cool but for some reason the clouds are not visible in an rectangular area around the player. I suppose this is intentional to visualize the game area.

comments.author: Justin Sullard
comments.text: YAY! Someone else chose an American theme! I was unable to beat of the enemies, as there was no way to avoid being hit by them from what I could tell, even moving forward and what not did not seem to work. It looked awesome though, and I loved the sound effects.

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
I remember when I played the game “Tanks” on Tetris, I always wanted to fight against the boss, instead of going through all these levels leading to him, well, it’s not “Tanks”, but you made my wish :).

Interesting bosses, each of which has at least 3 levels of difficulty, I think I saw three of them in total, I managed to defeat two, but the third… Also, each of the bosses is unique, which requires a strategy according to each one. Attention and creativity are your true assistants in dealing with them.

Perhaps if the events did not take place only in the mountains over which you fly, and also had certain Mayan artifacts, such as statues, etc., then it would add to the theme.

It was fun! Thanks for playing and congratulations!

comments.author: Tom Harris
comments.text: This was a fun game with a retro 3D feel, and there was a nice variety of bosses. I found them quite challenging but was able to defeat a couple, and I think the challenge added to the replayability of the game.

comments.author: Victor Debone
comments.text:
Hello! Thanks for your submission to js13k! I’m sure you’ve worked hard on your game, but I’m not aware of how much time you spent on it, so excuse me if things I mention were in your mind, but got left out in the rush of the month or just because you hit the 13kb limit :)

  • My first impression and understanding (before playing the game, only reading the description, screenshot and title):
    • The cover art at first looked like some adventure game, it reminded of DiG. After reading the description I think I’m in for a shooter?
  • Great work on:
    • Having a complete working game!
    • 3D! I really enjoy when developers go out their way to make the graphics work in this very limited size. Using SDFs gave you some good flexibility in modelling the bosses.
    • The theme and research on it! The lore really makes it pop among the games I have seen.
  • I liked that you:
    • Have the different list of bosses through different playthroughs.
  • You could improve (again, I’m aware you might already know all the below):
    • When I played the first time, I thought finishing the boss health meant I was advancing to the next one. After playing other times and realizing there was really different sprites, I noticed I never killed any of the bosses.
    • I’m not that good in shmups, but after trying a couple of times killing one boss, I gave up. I don’t think that’s a symptom of it being hard, but of the expectation of what’s waiting next, which is another boss, even more powerful than the one I barely managed to kill (well, I actually didn’t even manage the first, but you got the point).

api.name: 13C Defense
api.description:
While thinking about the theme of the 13th century, I thought about making a game related to the Mongol army. I was inspired by battles involving Mongol troops in the 13th century, including the Battle_of_Cheoin.

Like a typical defense game, enemies (Mongolians) move along a set path. Eliminate enemies before they approach the gate.

Movement is done using the arrow keys, and when leveling up, abilities are selected using the number keys 1, 2, and 3.

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: This was a pretty neat game, a bit like a one tower tower defense game. I feel that it could do with a bit more balancing though :)

comments.author: Almar Suarez
comments.text: Once I understood I only had to move, it became (too) easy…until level 5. The difficulty spike needs some balancing. I like that some arrows miss (specially horses), but perhaps not missing so much. I would also like to increase shooting speed - it seems impossible to beat.

comments.author: Richard Davey
comments.text: I really liked the concept here! Similar to a Tower Defense, but you can move around and auto-shoot at anything within range. Being able to upgrade during each level was fun, and the graphics were perfectly fitting for this type of game. The only issue was the balance - the first 4 levels are very easy (I didn’t even have to move to complete level 4!), but level 5 was insane, unleashing a constant flow of baddies you couldn’t ever hope to defeat. I suspect the dev just ran out of time :) still a great concept, though!

comments.author: Rémi Vansteelandt
comments.text:
There’s a lot of potential in this game. It feels like a mix between Vampire Survivors and a tower defense game. Difficulty was really low at the beginning, then I suddenly got swarmed by dozens of mounted knights and couldn’t do anything about it.

Things that would take this game to the next level:

  • having companions to help you
  • more complex castle shape
  • have enemies attack from different positions
  • overall, more enemies, more action, more animations/juice

comments.author: Chris Subagio
comments.text: Great pixel artwork, interesting idea! I didn’t really have to do anything for the first 4 levels though, and then the 5th level completely overwhelmed me. I suspect the author ran out of time to do level design :)

comments.author: Javi Agenjo
comments.text: It takes time to start being fun, as the first levels are just about staying on the corner of the map and do nothing. Moving your character should be part of fun, like adding stuff to pick up in your base.

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
I liked the idea and implementation! Good game!

Unfortunately, the gameplay has slowed down a bit, the first 4 levels are easy, but after the 5th there are a lot of enemies, you don’t have time to physically hold a decent fight. A little balance - and I could still play for a long time :).

Thanks and congratulations!

comments.author: Keith Karnage
comments.text: I think this is a great jam game. Good animation for the memory restriction. The movement of the horses felt great. My biggest suggestion would have been to add them earlier. The game scales pretty slowly, and then you are suddenly over run. I would have scaled a little bit faster. The first few levels I just sat in the one corner and no one got by. A second route for them to take might have helped encourage movement as well.

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
I enjoyed playing the game. Loved the graphics and galloping horses.

At first I thought I could just place the defender at the top left and get power ups as I proceeded through the levels. But then horseback riders appeared and I needed to adjust my strategy.

A fun game with replayability.

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text:
This game was so easy it auto-played itself if you stood on the top-left corner.

Then the horses came and it became nearly impossible (especially if you don’t know what they’re coming).

It needs a better difficulty curve (rather than a cliff).

Otherwise, I always enjoy a tower-defense game. It would be quite enjoyable if not for the sudden difficulty.

comments.author: Aurélio A. Heckert
comments.text: Why not allow to click in the option? Chose a targeting strategy is nice too. (weaker, stronger, faster, further, nearest…). The theme is good, but the tower defense implementation and the usage of history features was low. You demonstrated potential.

comments.author: Frederic Kyung-jin Rezeau
comments.text:
The game is fun, difficulty ramps up quite fast.

Love the autotargeting system mechanic and it could work very well with more sophisticated paths/entry points for enemies so you could bring strategy/depth without necessarily increasing the number of units.

comments.author: Jedidiah Weller
comments.text:
13C Defense https://dev.js13kgames.com/2023/games/13c-defense Hi there Kang!

Great job on this game! Really enjoyed playing it and you did great work on a couple things:

  1. Most notably, the rotation of the bow was very well done. It matched the enemy perfectly and was a good feature to introduce
  2. Simple controls were easy to use after a while.
  3. I liked the introduction of the additional enemy types

For some improvements I could suggest

  1. I’d suggest introducing different color scheme to the enemies with stronger life, that way it’s clear what type each are. When first playing myself and my wife didn’t realize that there were two types. If it’s included, it might be too small for us to see due to the pixelation
  2. It would be nice to introduce two entry points earlier. I found expanding the radius and damage to be OP by camping in the beginning.

Overall, great game and great job! What’s next? Feel free to hit me up on Twitter/X @jedihacks to have a discussion!

api.name: Thirteen Potions
api.description:
You are a knight who has to collect the 13 potions in the castle as fast as possible. But be careful, there’s 13 ghosts who want to slow you down!

Use your arrow keys to navigate around the map as fast as you can! My record is 52 seconds ;)

This game is disqualified because I mistakenly didn’t think that the Phaser framework would count towards the final size (my bad), but it was really fun making my first game for this!

comments.author: Alexey Kalmakov
comments.text: I love how you added knight and ghost animations. And the back sprite for the knight is a nice done. Sorry that you misunderstood rule about framework. Good luck next time.

comments.author: Tiago Correia
comments.text: Cute graphics, fun treasure-hunt race type game. A shame about going over the size though. Now you know for next year! :D

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
I enjoyed playing the game so much (and I’d used the same Kenney tileset in my own submission) that I wanted to have a go at rewriting your game to not use the phaser library and to fit everything within the 13kb limit.

In doing the coding I came across things which needed to be done which I’d never tried to do before, like putting images onto a canvas rotated and flipped. Also doing the red tint for damage wasn’t easy to do.

All in all a fun game, would love to see your future games in this competition

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text: Simple but fun, pixel art is great, I finishd in 109 seconds. Hope you come back next year :D

comments.author: John Edvard
comments.text:
The graphics and controls are great, but I miss some sound effects and music.

It would be cool to defeat the ghosts, but I understand this is a game to collect all potions as fast as possible.

I see this is in the unfinished category because it’s using Phaser, but I think it could have been submitted to the regular category if you used a different framework, like Kontra, or LittleJs. They have what you need to create what you have made.

comments.author: FoumartGames
comments.text: Graphics are great, the castle assets are such that I want to interact with everything! An anvil, give me a hammer! The stage was nicely put and had me wandering for a good while, it was fun.

comments.author: Mefistofel
comments.text: Simple labyrynth like game. Not finished, but playable. Looks pretty. Good job

comments.author: stxtus
comments.text: Good tilesets. And animation sadly you couldn’t put this amazing game together in time

comments.author: Jupiter Hadley
comments.text: I like the graphics a lot! The game itself is simple and well made - it’s a shame that your final size was too big, but it looks like you learned a lot!

api.name: Feudalistic Overlap
api.description:
This is a game for two. Call someone!

You should choose a coin type (gold or silver) and place one of that in some place.

Your friend will do the same thing the other coin type.

You can overlap any placed coin with another with superior role.

Have fun! If you are not a peasant.

comments.author: Michael M.
comments.text: I had to play against myself. The game is really well polished. At first it seemed quite interesting, but then I almost immediately saw an almost obvious strategy for the second player to at least enforce a draw: always cover the coin of the other player with the next higher one, except for the pope, then you play your peasant anywhere. This way the first player will never have more than two coins open, so he cannot win. With that strategy, unfortunately, the game becomes quite boring.

comments.author: Grav
comments.text: Great game, the graphics are really good. I found someone to play against. We didn’t use Michael’s draw forcing technique, but it did turn out to be a draw. There is a lot of strategy in it if you play properly, I really thought I was ahead as I was saving my larger coins, but it turned around at the end ☹️. I’m not sure if this was deliberate choice or not, but nothing happened when we placed last coin, was hoping for a winner to be declared. Was fun, thanks.

comments.author: Jack Le Hamster
comments.text: The graphics and effects on the coins are very polished. It’s an interesting variant on tic-tac-toe, but some AI would have been nice. I’m just trying those entries on my own.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: A fun twist on tic-tac-toe! I didn’t have anyone to play with, but will give it a try another time. The graphics are really smooth. It was a bit hard to read the initial text because it went away so fast. There should be a way to restart the game once it ends. I like the theme approach. If this game had some sound effects and a bit more polished animations it would be amazing.

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
G’day!

Tic Tac Toe - level 2 :)

From the good:

game for two

looks good

Feedback:

You can’t lose here, I constantly overlapped the opponent’s coins and when there were no coins left, nothing happened, as well as when winning - I expect to be able to repeat the game. Also, when placing coins quickly, the voice will still speak all the moves even if they are long behind…

I think it would be better if the voice announces the current move and has not yet had time to announce that a new move has already taken place, then the current voiceover ends and starts the next one move.

The start screen that describes the sequence of shapes - disappears too quickly, I can’t read it… although it looks cool!

It would also be good to consider the possibility of not only dragging coins onto the playing field, but also the possibility of selecting a coin with the first click, and instead of dragging, simply press the mouse cursor on the cell where you want to put it.

Unfortunately, I did not find resonance with the topic of the contest…

Good game! Thanks!

comments.author: Homer Dilpleu
comments.text: The graphics are very well done and I like the revisited tic-tac-toe concept. I was not able to play with someone but I imagine several strategies can be developped. I would have appreciated being able to play against an IA.

comments.author: Ryan Tyler
comments.text:
Nice graphics! We played a little fast and had to let the voice catch up at times.

Looks like a good mobile game!

comments.author: Noël Meudec
comments.text:
What I liked:

  • It was… a surprising experience.
  • Voices! Woaw :)

What I liked less:

  • It is Tic Tac Toe.

Overall, this game is twist on Tic Tac Toe. Not my thing, but it had a “woaw” effect thanks to the voices. Good job!

comments.author: Mohammad Jawad
comments.text: voice is a cool effect and graphics are awsome, other than that it dont have any juice such as a worthy apponent ai to play with

comments.author: Björn Ritzl
comments.text: Interesting idea. But why would you ever place a coin other than the one you have on hand with the highest value/role? The graphics are of high quality, and there’s even speech synthesis. Cool.

comments.author: Krzysztof Bociurko
comments.text: Nice, considering two player constraints, but needs an end game screen

comments.author: Jedidiah Weller
comments.text:
Hi Aurélio,

Great work on this submission - it was a really unique take on tic tac toe! Here’s my feedback and suggestions below:

  1. The beginning instructions went by way too fast - and because of the style of the background they were on I could hardly read them. I’d suggest slowing this down and focusing on readability there
  2. The coins - Bravo on the design! Loved the shine and polish on these, really good work here - this looked professionally done
  3. The concept - again, bravo! Really great concept
  4. Controls - I had some minor issue with drag and drop of the coins. I think the game could have less ‘bounce’ in it and still be really fun and engaging
  5. Code - I’d like to see more code structure here and a breakout of files with appropriate naming.
  6. CSS - Would be great to see stylelint included in the repository to enforce standards
  7. README - well done but should have build/usage instructions
  8. License - need to add!

Overall, great submission - should be proud! I look forward to seeing what you do with it next. Hit me up on twitter @jedihacks for any discussion!

api.name: Angry Temujin
api.description:
Genghis Khan was born Temujin and was left out of his clan as he became fatherless. He then climbed, fought, conquered, and terrorized asia and europe.

To play … WASD …

Unfortunately, there is a lot left to do … but it does play!

comments.author: Cliff Earl
comments.text: Hey that’s really good for what it is. I’d be keen to see the entire thing :)

comments.author: Richard Davey
comments.text: This plays like Vampire Survivors, only with a teeny tiny Genghis Khan as the main character! Move around, try and time it so your spinning sword slices the baddies up, and see how many you can kill. After a certain level, you’ll get new followers that will help you out, tracking after you and killing anyone they meet. As the enemies grow in number, you’ll need to try and collect as many followers as possible in order to progress. It’s very good fun for what it is. I would love to see how this could have progressed with more time spent on it.

comments.author: Rémi Vansteelandt
comments.text:
This is obviously highly inspired by Vampire Survivors, but I still loved it! The eagle mechanic is really cool, I wish I could have sent my eagle to attack, or have multiple attacks (but maybe that’s against the idea behind this kind of game?).

Anyway I only have a few suggestions:

  • sound
  • more animations (when hit, when you get hit, when someone joins the clan)
  • upgrades/gameplay depth

In your description it sounds like you didn’t finish it, but I think you can be proud of what you have! Would love to see it developed outside of js13k.

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
Reminds me on “Magic Survival” / “Vampire Survivors”.

It’s a little bit tiny on the desktop-screen.

Good start, would love to see the game with the remaining featuires implemented.

comments.author: Johan Åhlgren
comments.text: It was quite fun, and felt innovative. It was difficult to understand what was going on at times - like some enemies don’t die but turn into allies, and one time I got some kind of “super shot” which killed a whole bunch of enemies. It would have been nice with a bit more feedback on what was going on, and maybe hints on how I would achieve e.g. the “super shot”. But criticism aside, great job!

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text: OK, at first I thought the game was going nowhere but once I got my first minions, it got really fun. This game has lots of potential. You could make the window stretch to the whole screen, as the game was too small. The graphics are really cute despite the minimal style. The controls are easy but grasping the use of the spinning weapon takes some getting used to. Again, once you get minions, that’s less of a problem. Wish there was a way to heal and to better control the minions. Also, a way to restart the game without refreshing is a must. Some music would have been a great touch.

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Well done:

  • Great job getting a game in
  • Lovely little vampire survivor like game. Sprites were nice (though small)
  • I liked occasionally converting enemies to followers when they died

Feedback:

  • On desktop it’s pretty tiny, would have been nice to be more zoomed in
  • Telling enemies apart from allies was a bit difficult. Took me awhile to realize the converted troops and especially the riding units were friendly
  • Would love to see where this goes given more time

comments.author: Björn Ritzl
comments.text: Horde survival games are fun! Angry Temujin has potential too, but as you say in the description, there are a few things left to do. The game does play well though. Horde survival games are all about optimizing your build while gradually facing more and more and new types of enemies. In Angry Temujin you get some of these features for sure, but I have no control over the upgrades/new units I get. I also found the graphics to be a bit on the small side.

title: Dragoom
author: Zachary Rankin, David Fagan et al

api.name: Dragoom
api.description: Enter the world of Dragoom, where fire-breathing dragons rule the skies and towns blaze in roaring flames! As a guardian of the realm, your mission is to douse the raging fires and save the buildings and the bitty’s. Challenge yourself in this frantic, fiery race against time, battling the dragon’s inferno and striving to keep the world intact for as long as possible. How long can you keep the flames at bay?

comments.author: Christoph Schansky
comments.text:
After i found out, that i am supposed to click on the flames, it was quite fun to play, at least a few moments.

I like the graphics.

After a while (a thousand clicks later) i got a bit bored of just clicking, and stopped. Then it took the dragon ages to set the last tower on fire…

Sound-effects/a soundtrack would add much to the game. And some variation (more than “just clicking”)

comments.author: Jasper Renow-Clarke
comments.text:
I liked the pixel art and concept of the game.

I went through several levels, although wasn’t sure what changed as the levels changed to alter the difficulty. Also wasn’t sure what the little people did.

But overall fun to play.

Lots of mouse clicking for a desktop game, so I think this works better as a mobile or touchscreen game.

I’ll have to play again on a touch device.

comments.author: Christian Staudte
comments.text:
At round 15 - 20 the game really starts to escalate (in a fun way). I got to 34 (: The graphics are also very well made!

The only thing I would have liked to see are multiple levels with a map that becomes incrementally bigger (although this might only work on Desktop and not on Mobile)

comments.author: Roman Miniv
comments.text:
G’day!

My result: Final Score - 3335 | Final Round - 34

Very nice game, I liked it! Such a version of a clicker, but I have never seen such an idea and presentation - it turned out cool! I liked the dragons - I like simple :). The random placement of towers in each new game is also pleasing. Dragons seem to also randomly choose a line of fire :).

As an addition, maybe it would be a good idea to add the ability to shoot dragons somehow, or during a certain time interval to give the opportunity to put a shield on some object, then you could add more towers, put a shield on some, and save other - that’s how we live :D.

It was easy and fun for me to play, although my hand gets tired over time :D, thanks!

comments.author: Steven Lambert
comments.text:
Well done:

  • Graphics were really nice. Especially liked the dragon shadow
  • I liked that if you didn’t put out the flames quick enough they would permanently destroy that tile
  • I liked that the dragon could hit a tile multiple times and it would destroy it faster

Feedback:

  • Wasn’t intuitive what to do at first. Took awhile to figure out I needed to click on the fire on the towers to put them out
  • I wasn’t sure what the people did. Most of the time I would try to put them out like the towers but nothing happened. But sometimes they would be put out and I’d score points. It was never consistent
  • The game has no penalty for losing a tower tile, which meant that all I needed to do was guard 1 tile for the entire game and the dragon couldn’t win. Losing all other tiles didn’t matter. This also means that the game gets easier as you lose tiles as you’re not having to guard as many spots anymore
  • Lots of clicking super fast is very tiring

comments.author: Nathan Long
comments.text: Took a second to figure it out, but there’s a nice ramp up into increasing insanity as you try to put out the fires.

comments.author: João Lopes
comments.text:
